title: Zimbabwe’s diaspora reshapes real estate and farming investment trends

content: Zimbabweans living abroad are increasingly turning to social media for investment advice, reshaping trends in real estate and farming. Digital influencers Kundai Chitima, 31, and Kelvin Birioti, 20, have built large followings by posting videos and updates about opportunities in Zimbabwe. Their content—ranging from property tours to agricultural tips—is influencing decisions on whether to return or invest. content: Social media feeds are flooded with health advice—some helpful, some dangerous, and most unchecked by medical professionals. A 2023 Pew Research Center survey found 68% of Americans have tried at least one health tip they saw on platforms like TikTok, Instagram or Facebook. But doctors are warning that many of these suggestions bypass scientific scrutiny, sometimes with serious consequences. title: Midlife fitness boosts lifespan and health by 5+ years study finds
description: New research shows high midlife cardiorespiratory fitness adds over 5 healthy years to your life. Experts recommend daily exercise to improve longevity and prev
content: Adults who maintain high cardiorespiratory fitness in their 40s and 50s gain more than five additional years of healthy life compared with less-fit peers, according to research published Wednesday in the journal Circulation. The study tracked 12,000 adults for three decades, finding that those with the highest fitness levels not only lived longer but spent fewer years managing chronic conditions like heart disease and diabetes. title: Your Body Clock May Enhance The Benefits of Exercise, Study Finds
description: Research shows morning or evening workouts may maximize exercise benefits depending on your body clock. Timing exercises to circadian rhythm improves fitness ou
content: People who exercise in sync with their natural body clock may see greater health benefits than those who work out at fixed times, according to new research. A study published in Science Advances found that individuals who adjusted their exercise routines to match their circadian rhythms—whether early morning or late evening—experienced improved fitness outcomes compared to those who ignored their internal clocks. The findings suggest that timing workouts to one’s natural energy peaks could enhance results for weight loss, muscle gain, and cardiovascular health. The research team analyzed data

title: Rap shooter gets 20 years for Young Dolph murder case closure
description: Cornelius Smith Jr. sentenced to 20 years for shooting rapper Young Dolph in 2021. The case ends after he admitted guilt during a co-defendant's trial.
content: Cornelius Smith Jr., the man convicted of fatally shooting rapper Young Dolph in 2021, was sentenced Friday to 20 years in prison after pleading guilty to second-degree murder. Smith’s confession came during the trial of a co-defendant, solidifying his role in the high-profile killing that shocked the hip-hop world. The sentence, handed down in Shelby County Criminal Court, marks the end of a case that drew national attention and left fans mourning the loss of an influential artist. title: Study finds yawns contagious before birth, even more than thought
description: New research shows yawning is contagious even before birth, more powerful than earlier believed. Scientists confirm fetal response to external stimuli.
content: A new study published in PLOS ONE confirms that yawns are contagious even before birth, challenging earlier research that suggested this behavior begins after birth. Using 4D ultrasound technology, scientists observed 24 to 36-week-old fetuses reacting to yawns from people outside the womb, proving social behavior starts in the prenatal stage. The findings indicate contagious yawning is far stronger than previously believed, with fetuses responding within seconds of seeing or hearing a yawn. title: Lawyers sanctioned $110K for fake AI citations in landmark Oregon case
description: Two Oregon lawyers fined $110,000 for submitting 23 fabricated citations linked to AI hallucinations. Judges across the U.S. crack down on AI misuse in legal fi
content: A federal judge in Oregon has levied the largest penalty yet for AI misuse in legal filings, fining two lawyers $110,000 after they submitted 23 fabricated citations and eight invented quotations generated by artificial intelligence. The sanctions, imposed by U.S. District Judge Ann Aiken in Portland, mark the first major judicial response to the surge in lawyers relying on AI tools to draft court documents. description: Chelsea face Leeds in FA Cup final with protests, manager exits and poor league form. A win could ease club tensions and salvage a troubled campaign.
content: Chelsea arrive at Wembley Saturday for the FA Cup final against Leeds United after one of their most turbulent seasons in recent memory. The club has cycled through three managers—Thomas Tuchel, Frank Lampard, and now Graham Potter—while enduring persistent fan protests over the team’s direction and ownership decisions. title: USS Gerald R. Ford ends 11-month deployment, longest since Vietnam
description: USS Gerald R. Ford, the world's largest aircraft carrier, returns to Virginia after an 11-month deployment, the longest since Vietnam. Crew faced a fire during
content: NORFOLK, Va. — The USS Gerald R. Ford, the world’s largest aircraft carrier, returned to Virginia on Saturday after an 11-month deployment, the longest since the Vietnam War. The USS Gerald R. Ford and two destroyers docked at Naval Station Norfolk, where about 5,000 sailors waited to reunite with their families for the first time since June of last year. The deployment involved combat operations across multiple continents, including support for U.S. military actions against Iran and participation in the capture of then-Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro in 2024. content: U.S. President Donald Trump placed arms sales to Taiwan at the center of high-stakes talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping this week, as the two leaders wrapped up a two-day meeting in Washington on Friday. title: Why 'Ballmaxxing' May Be More Dangerous Than You Think
description: Doctors warn ballmaxxing—saline injections to enlarge testicles—can cause infections and permanent damage. Learn the serious risks now.
content: Health professionals are sounding alarms over a viral social media trend called ballmaxxing, where men inject fluids directly into the testicles to increase their size. The practice involves using substances such as saline or surgical lubricants like Surgilube, with some users claiming their testicles grew to the size of grapefruits. Medical experts, however, warn that such procedures pose serious health risks, including severe infections, scarring, and possible permanent damage to male genitalia. title: What Man City's FA Cup win means for Premier League places in Europe
description: Man City's FA Cup win alters Premier League Europe spots. Top eight now qualify, with sixth possibly in Champions League. Here's how the new rules work.
content: Manchester City's FA Cup triumph has reshaped the Premier League's European qualification race ahead of the 2024-25 season. The Blues' victory means the top eight clubs in England's top flight will now secure a spot in European competitions, up from the previous seven. This change follows a decision by UEFA to expand qualification pathways for domestic cup winners. For the first time, the sixth-placed team in the Premier League could qualify for the UEFA Champions League if the FA Cup winner finishes in the top six. title: Dead whale found near Danish island after failed German rescue
description: A dead whale was found near Denmark after a German rescue team attempted to save it. Experts had warned of likely harm to the animal. Period.
content: A sperm whale was found dead near Bornholm, Denmark, on Friday, days after German rescuers attempted to free it from shallow waters off Rügen island. The animal, later identified as a 14-meter male sperm whale, had become stranded multiple times in recent weeks, prompting local authorities to call for assistance. Rescue teams from Germany and Denmark worked for days to guide the whale back to deeper waters, but the animal repeatedly returned to the shallows before finally succumbing. content: Labour MP Wes Streeting has become the first senior figure to publicly declare he will run to replace Prime Minister Keir Starmer if the party triggers a leadership contest, calling for a full and open competition rather than a pre-arranged handover. content: The number of bariatric surgeries performed for weight loss has declined as prescriptions for GLP-1 medications like Ozempic, Wegovy, and Mounjaro skyrocketed, according to a new study published by Loyola Medicine. title: Flip phones beat smartphones in 4-day digital detox challenge
description: CNBC reporters swapped smartphones for flip phones in a 4-day digital detox experiment. Learn why going back was harder than expected.
content: Two CNBC Make It reporters swapped their modern smartphones for basic flip phones in a four-day digital detox experiment that revealed just how deeply ingrained daily tech habits have become. The reporters, who usually rely on high-end smartphones for work and personal tasks, traded devices to test their ability to disconnect from digital distractions. Flip phones forced dramatic lifestyle changes The experiment immediately disrupted routines for both reporters. Tasks like checking email, navigating city streets, or ordering food became noticeably slower, requiring more planning and patience.

title: Sleep 6.4 to 7.8 hours nightly to slow biological aging study says
description: Getting 6.4 to 7.8 hours of sleep nightly may slow aging in brain and body. New Nature study links sleep duration to biological clocks and late-life depression.
content: Sleeping 6.4 to 7.8 hours nightly may slow biological aging in the brain and other organs, according to a new study published in Nature. Researchers from Harvard Medical School and Brigham and Women’s Hospital analyzed sleep data from over 40,000 adults to examine the relationship between sleep duration and aging clocks—biological markers that predict aging rates. The findings suggest that deviating from this sleep range accelerates cellular aging, increasing risks for age-related diseases and cognitive decline. title: US proposes 40% cut in Colorado River water for 3 states amid drought

The proposal, revealed Wednesday during an Arizona water officials’ meeting, comes after seven states drawing water from the river failed to reach a consensus on voluntary cuts. title: 300,000 daily LIRR commuters disrupted as NYC railway workers strike
description: New York’s Long Island Rail Road strike halts commutes for 300,000 daily riders after union negotiations with MTA fail. Talks stalled over wages and healthcare
content: Workers for New York’s Long Island Rail Road (LIRR) walked off the job Saturday, crippling the nation’s busiest commuter rail system and stranding nearly 300,000 daily passengers. The walkout by 3,500 union members—including engineers, machinists and signalmen—marks the first strike on the LIRR in more than three decades. The stoppage follows the collapse of negotiations between five unions and the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA), which manages the system. title: US charges man with plotting Iran-directed attacks on Jews in London and New York

title: US-Nigeria joint strike kills ISIS leader in West Africa: Report
description: US and Nigerian forces eliminate senior ISIS leader in rare joint operation in Nigeria. Confirmed by both Trump and Nigeria's Tinubu. Period.
content: A senior leader of the Islamic State (IS) group was killed in a joint US-Nigerian military operation in Nigeria’s insurgency-hit northeast, US President Donald Trump announced on Friday and Nigeria’s President Bola Tinubu confirmed the following day. The operation targeted a high-value IS operative in Borno state, a region long plagued by Islamist insurgencies linked to IS and Boko Haram factions. Nigerian President Bola Tinubu and U.S. President Joe Biden separately announced the killing, which marks a significant blow to the group’s leadership in Africa. title: Ultra-Processed Foods Linked to Heart Disease, but Healthy Swaps May Lower Risk
description: New research shows ultra-processed foods significantly increase heart disease risk. Doctors advised to discuss dangers and healthier swaps with patients. Expert
content: European scientists are calling for urgent action to address the health risks of ultra-processed foods, particularly their link to heart disease. In a new study published this week, researchers from leading institutions across Europe found that regular consumption of ultra-processed foods significantly increases the risk of cardiovascular conditions, including heart attacks and strokes. The findings, based on long-term data from thousands of participants, underscore the need for stronger public health interventions and better dietary guidance. title: Ultra-Orthodox conscription dispute pushes Israeli government to brink

content: Israel’s government edged closer to collapse on Tuesday as ultra-Orthodox Haredi parties withdrew their backing from Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s coalition over a dispute on military service exemptions. The move follows months of tension between Netanyahu and his Haredi allies, who have made draft exemptions a core demand in exchange for their political support. The crisis deepened Tuesday when one faction of United Torah Judaism (UTJ), led by Degel Hatorah, demanded the coalition collapse unless the exemptions were codified into law. content: A Russian missile slammed into a residential apartment building in central Kyiv on Friday, reducing part of the structure to rubble and killing 24 people, including three children, Ukrainian officials said. The strike, which occurred around 9 a.m. local time, targeted a densely populated area, flattening multiple floors of the 10-story building and trapping residents under debris. Rescue crews worked through the night to recover bodies and survivors, as President Volodymyr Zelenskiy arrived at the scene to lay red roses and vow retaliation. content: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer faced escalating calls to resign Tuesday after weeks of mounting political pressure and declining public approval. The crisis deepened as senior lawmakers within his own party joined opposition figures in demanding his immediate departure. Polls released Monday showed Starmer’s approval ratings at their lowest point in over a year, with just 28% of voters expressing confidence in his leadership. content: Thousands of protesters filled central London Saturday as a far-right rally organized by activist Tommy Robinson and a pro-Palestine counter-demonstration clashed under heavy police supervision. Authorities sealed off key areas to prevent violent confrontations as tensions simmered over the Israel-Hamas war and immigration policies. content: Former British health secretary Wes Streeting said Saturday he will run to replace Prime Minister Keir Starmer as leader of the UK’s Labour Party, escalating pressure on the prime minister amid internal party unrest. content: The United Arab Emirates UAE will exit the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries OPEC on May 1, 2024, the country confirmed Tuesday. The decision marks the end of nearly six decades of membership for the Gulf nation, which joined OPEC in 1967, the same year the cartel was founded. In a statement, the UAE’s Energy Ministry said the move was a strategic economic decision, not a political one, aimed at allowing the country to boost its oil production and maximize revenue from crude exports. content: Two men were taken into custody Friday at London’s Euston station in connection with a fatal incident in Birmingham the previous day. A man died after being struck by a van driven by a motorist who allegedly reacted to the removal of flags from lamp-posts, police said. content: Gaming headset maker Turtle Beach on Tuesday introduced the Stealth Pro 2, a $349 wireless headset that directly challenges SteelSeries’ $399 Nova Pro Omni. content: Martin O'Neill made a bold prediction in March: Celtic would have to win all seven of their remaining games to secure the Scottish Premiership title. content: US President Donald Trump cautioned Taiwan on Wednesday against declaring independence, just hours after holding a summit with China’s President Xi Jinping aimed at lowering tensions across the Taiwan Strait. The warning came during a White House press briefing, where Trump stated that both Beijing and Taipei should "cool down" their rhetoric to prevent further escalation. title: Trump Reclassifies State-Licensed Medical Cannabis, but It’s Not Legal Just Yet
description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche reclassifies state-licensed medical marijuana as less dangerous under federal law, easing tax and research barriers. Trump
content: The Trump administration moved Friday to reclassify state-licensed medical marijuana under federal law, easing decades-old restrictions but stopping short of full legalization. Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche signed an executive order April 23 that shifts marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III of the Controlled Substances Act. Schedule I drugs, like heroin, are defined as having no accepted medical use and high potential for abuse. Schedule III drugs, like ketamine, are considered less dangerous and have recognized medical benefits. The reclassification marks a major policy shift after

content: Celebrity chef and restaurateur Tom Colicchio turned a little-known Italian tech company into his most profitable investment ever, scoring roughly a 15x return. Nigerian security forces and local vigilantes began immediate search operations to locate the missing children and bring the perpetrators to justice. content: Danish authorities confirmed Saturday that Timmy the humpback whale, whose controversial rescue attempt sparked outrage, has died. Environmental Protection Agency officials said the whale’s carcass was found Friday near Anholt, a small island in the Kattegat strait between Denmark and Sweden. Genetic testing confirmed the identity of the 27-foot humpback on Saturday. title: Dead humpback whale found off Denmark was recently rescued in Germany
description: Dead humpback whale found off Denmark was the same animal rescued in Germany two weeks ago. Tracking device confirms identity.
content: COPENHAGEN (AP) — A dead humpback whale discovered Thursday off Denmark’s Anholt Island has been confirmed as the same animal rescued from Germany’s Baltic Sea coast just two weeks earlier, Danish authorities announced Saturday. The carcass washed ashore near the small island in the Kattegat Strait, which links the Baltic and North Seas, south of where the whale—nicknamed “Timmy” and “Hope” by rescuers—was released on May 2 after a controversial seven-week rescue operation. title: How Three Lions anthem united England football fans for 25 years
description: Three Lions created by David Baddiel and Frank Skinner has topped charts for 25 years. Discover how the English football anthem became a national symbol and its
content: The anthem that became 'Three Lions (Football's Coming Home)' began as a joke. In 1996, comedian David Baddiel and his former comedy partner Frank Skinner) teamed up with Lily Allen's brother Alfie Allen and musician Ian Broudie of The Lightning Seeds to create what would become England's most enduring football anthem. Originally titled 'Three Lions on a Shirt,' the song was commissioned by BBC Sport as part of Euro 1996 coverage. title: Thousands protest at Eurovision final as five countries boycott over Israel
description: Five nations skip Eurovision final in protest as Israel competes amid Gaza war. Thousands rally outside event in Vienna to oppose inclusion.
content: Five European nations pulled out of the Eurovision Song Contest final in Vienna on Saturday to protest Israel’s inclusion amid its war in Gaza. Spain, the Netherlands, Ireland, Iceland and Slovenia withdrew, marking the largest boycott in the 70-year history of the competition. Thousands of protesters marched through Austria’s capital to voice opposition to Israel’s participation, calling it a double standard by organizers. description: Tens of thousands marched in central London Saturday to commemorate the Nakba, when 800,000 Palestinians were displaced during Israel’s 1948 creation. Organizer
content: Tens of thousands of demonstrators filled the streets of central London on Saturday for the largest Nakba anniversary march in years. title: Mars ESCAPADE mission discussed on latest This Week In Space podcast

content: NASA’s twin spacecraft for the ESCAPADE mission are en route to Mars, where they will study the planet’s magnetosphere and atmosphere. The mission, led by Dr. Robert Lillis, is the subject of Episode 210 of the This Week In Space podcast, hosted by Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik. The spacecraft, named Blue and Gold, are expected to arrive at Mars in 2025, where they will conduct joint observations to help scientists understand the planet’s climate history and the loss of its atmosphere over time. title: PCOS renamed to PMOS after decades of misdiagnosis confusion
description: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) officially renamed to polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS) to correct decades of misinformation. Ends long-standing
content: The common hormone disorder previously called polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) has been officially renamed polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS) by a global medical consensus group. The change, announced this week, seeks to correct widespread misconceptions and diagnostic inaccuracies that have persisted for more than 30 years. Researchers say the new name better reflects the condition’s true metabolic and endocrine nature, which extends beyond the ovaries. Experts from the International Hormone Society and the Endocrine Society led the push for the rebrand, citing evidence that PCOS

title: Eccentric Exercise Builds Muscle Faster With Less Effort
description: Eccentric exercises like lowering weights build strength more efficiently with less strain, research shows. Benefits muscle, heart, and brain health naturally.
content: Eccentric exercises, often overlooked in fitness routines, may offer a more efficient way to build muscle strength with less physical strain. Unlike traditional concentric exercises—which involve muscle shortening movements like lifting weights or pushing upward—eccentric exercises focus on muscle lengthening, such as lowering a weight or walking downhill. Research suggests these movements generate up to 40% more force with less energy expenditure, making them ideal for older adults or those recovering from injury. title: Manchester City end 7-year WSL wait with 'magical' trophy win

title: 10 best laptops for most users in 2024: MacBook Air, Windows picks

title: Vilseck, Germany fears 5,000 U.S. troop withdrawal under Trump
description: Vilseck, Germany’s economy relies on 5,000 U.S. troops. Residents fear Trump may pull them out, threatening jobs and community ties.
content: The quiet streets of Vilseck, Germany, a town of 6,500 in eastern Bavaria, hum with anxiety after President Trump threatened to withdraw 5,000 U.S. troops stationed nearby. The Rose Barracks military base has anchored this community since 1951, providing jobs, spending power, and decades of cross-cultural friendships. For many residents, the base isn’t just a military outpost—it’s the town’s lifeline. The cuts follow the disappearance of 6,000 open job listings, signaling a broader shift in resource allocation. While Meta has not explicitly cited AI as the reason for the layoffs, the timing and parallel reductions in tech and finance point to a clear trend: AI is reshaping employment in industries that have long relied on knowledge workers. title: Device helps users improve posture with offline AI for $350 price

content: A new desk gadget from Deep Care claims to improve posture and movement habits with AI, all without an internet connection. The device, priced at $350, attaches to chairs or desks and monitors user movements using built-in sensors. Unlike many health tech products, it operates fully offline, eliminating privacy concerns tied to cloud-based tracking. title: The Longer Poop Stays in Your Body, the More It Can Affect Your Health

content: Stool transit time affects more than digestion. A 2023 study in Nature reveals how the speed of waste movement through the body reshapes gut bacteria, influencing long-term health. Researchers analyzed stool samples from 1,000 adults, comparing transit times to microbiome composition. Findings show slow transit correlates with higher risks for diabetes, obesity, and cardiovascular disease, while faster transit may offer protective benefits. content: The 70th anniversary Eurovision Song Contest final arrives Saturday in Vienna with 25 acts set to perform for Europe’s pop crown amid a charged atmosphere of protests, boycotts and heavy security. content: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has issued a nationwide recall of a single lot of Xanax XR 3-milligram tablets after tests showed the pills failed to meet dissolution specifications. The voluntary recall affects 60-tablet bottles from Viatris Inc. with lot number 8177156, batch expiration date March 2025, and NDC 0069-0753-30. content: Taiwan’s Deputy Foreign Minister Chen Ming-chi said Saturday the island would continue pushing Washington to approve a pending $14 billion arms deal, the second major military sale awaiting President Donald Trump’s signature. The push comes after Trump warned Taiwan against declaring independence during an interview with Fox News following his high-stakes summit with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, in Manila. content: Ukraine’s Elina Svitolina Elina Svitolina claimed her third Italian Open title in Rome on Sunday, defeating American Coco Gauff Coco Gauff 7-6(6), 6-4 in a tense three-set final. content: Sudan’s military announced Friday it had recaptured the southeastern town of Khor Hassan in Blue Nile state from the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), a paramilitary group engaged in a brutal civil war against the army for more than three years. The army’s statement reported heavy casualties among RSF fighters and destruction of military equipment during the operation. Khor Hassan’s seizure marks a significant shift in the conflict’s southeastern front, where control of the town provides strategic access to central Sudan and the Ethiopian border. title: Study: Immigrants face steep housing barriers in Germany
description: New German study reveals immigrants find it harder to secure affordable housing, worsening integration and labor market gaps. End of housing equality?
content: Immigrants in Germany are increasingly locked out of affordable housing, deepening social divides in a market already strained by soaring demand and limited supply. A new study highlights that newcomers face disproportionate barriers to securing rentals in cities like Berlin, where even modest apartments require renovations or sit on the outskirts, far from jobs and services. content: Long Island Rail Road service came to a standstill Saturday after unionized workers launched a strike at 12:01 a.m., crippling North America’s largest commuter rail system. Five unions representing roughly half of the LIRR’s workforce walked off the job, halting all train service in the New York City suburbs. Reports indicate the company, led by Elon Musk, could file publicly as soon as Wednesday with a roadshow launching June 4. The public debut on the Nasdaq under the ticker SPCX is scheduled for June 12, according to sources cited by Reuters. The mechanical monks, programmed to mimic human movements, marched alongside human participants in the annual event celebrating Vesak, the most sacred day in Buddhism. Seoul’s lantern festival, a centuries-old tradition, transforms the city’s Jongno District into a vibrant display of light and color each year. The review, commissioned by South African Rugby Union (SARU), examines the impact of the expanded domestic league on player fatigue and injury risks. content: A British Army soldier died Friday at the Royal Windsor Horse Show after falling from a horse during a mounted display, police confirmed. The service member, part of the King’s Troop Royal Horse Artillery, was pronounced dead at the scene by medical responders following the incident near the showground in Berkshire. The tragedy occurred just after 7 p.m. as the soldier exited the arena following a performance. content: Three major social media companies—Snap Snap Inc., YouTube Google and TikTok ByteDance—have settled a lawsuit filed by the Breathitt County School District in Kentucky. The district alleged that the companies’ addictive designs disrupted student learning and fueled a mental health crisis, driving up costs for public schools. While the settlement terms were not disclosed, the agreement marks the first resolution of its kind and sets a potential precedent for more than 1,000 similar lawsuits nationwide. content: Barbara Roberts has defied expectations for decades after her 1996 HIV diagnosis. At 74, she remains healthy, active, and optimistic, thanks to advances in antiretroviral therapy that have transformed HIV from a fatal diagnosis to a manageable condition. Roberts was 44 when she first noticed something was wrong. She initially dismissed her symptoms—fever, night sweats, and fatigue—as a stubborn case of the flu. content: A driver in his 30s intentionally rammed a car into a crowd of pedestrians in Modena, northern Italy, on Saturday, injuring seven people, police confirmed. The attack left two victims in serious condition, with injuries requiring hospitalization. Authorities took the suspect into custody within minutes of the incident after witnesses intervened and detained him until police arrived. Investigators said the man, whose identity has not been released, allegedly attempted to stab a passerby who tried to stop him immediately after the collision. The actor and filmmaker told Brut at the Cannes Film Festival on Friday that writers who lean on AI to pen scripts should abandon the craft entirely. “If you use AI to write your stories or scripts, then you shouldn’t be a writer,” Rogen said. “I don’t understand what it’s supposed to do.” Rogen’s remarks came during a discussion about AI’s growing role in Hollywood, a topic that has divided the industry. content: A team of Thai and French paleontologists has identified a previously unknown giant dinosaur species from fossils unearthed in Thailand’s Phu Kum Khao region, researchers announced Wednesday. The creature, named Gigantoraptor krausei, lived approximately 70 million years ago and is estimated to have weighed up to 50 tons—comparable to nine adult African elephants. The discovery was published in the peer-reviewed journal Scientific Reports. content: PHILADELPHIA — Kyle Schwarber Kyle Schwarber can’t explain the surge fueling his hot streak, but his bat spoke loudly Sunday night. The 37-year-old Polish forward, who joined from Bayern Munich in 2022, scored 119 goals in 191 appearances across all competitions. Dr. Tracy Beth Hoeg confirmed Friday she was “fired” from her position as acting director of the FDA’s Center for Drug Evaluation and Research, a role she held since January 2025. content: > 📰 Continuing coverage: Martin O'Neill leads Celtic to Premiership title with 7 straight wins Celtic FC ended an eight-month title chase on Friday, defeating Hearts Hearts 2-0 at Celtic Park to secure the Scottish Premiership. The win, sealed with goals from Kyogo Furuhashi and Anthony Ralston, marked the club's 54th league title and first in 12 months. The 32-game pursuit spanned 2,880 on-field minutes and 48 hours of relentless pressure from rival fans and media alike. title: ArXiv bans authors for 1 year if AI writes entire papers alone
description: ArXiv will penalize researchers for one year if AI tools write their papers without human input. The repository tightens rules on large language models in scien
content: The arXiv preprint server on Tuesday announced stricter penalties for authors who submit papers generated entirely by artificial intelligence, including a one-year ban from the platform. The policy, effective immediately, seeks to curb the misuse of large language models in scientific writing as AI tools grow more sophisticated and accessible. Moderators will flag violations during the review process, with penalties applied retroactively to already published work if necessary. arXiv founder Paul Ginsparg confirmed the change in a statement, citing concerns over the erosion of human oversight i

title: Reed Jobs plans $1bn UK cancer care investment after father’s death
description: Reed Jobs, son of Steve Jobs, aims to invest $1bn in UK cancer care to transform outcomes after losing his father to the disease in 2011. Ends cancer as a death
content: Reed Jobs, heir to the Apple fortune and son of co-founder Steve Jobs, announced plans to invest $1bn from his venture capital fund Yosemite into UK cancer care. The move follows the death of Jobs’ father in 2011 from a rare form of pancreatic cancer at age 56. Jobs, who was 12 when his father died, said the experience motivated him to fund research aimed at eliminating cancer as a leading cause of death. "I saw my dad have cancer when I was a kid, and unfortunately that happens far too often," Jobs told The Guardian. "And that really motivated me to try to transform outcomes for other people

title: Maldives cave rescue diver dies in search for drowned Italians
description: A Maldives rescue diver died during the search for two Italian tourists who drowned in caves. The government confirms the diver's death amid ongoing recovery ef
content: A Maldives military diver died Monday while assisting in the search for the bodies of two Italian tourists who drowned in underwater caves, authorities confirmed Tuesday. Staff Sgt. Mohamed Mahdhee of the Maldives National Defence Force (MNDF) was part of a coordinated recovery operation in the southern Addu Atoll, where the tourists were reported missing Sunday. The government told the BBC it was investigating the circumstances of Mahdhee’s death, which occurred during the dive. Dive conditions complicate rescue efforts The search for the missing Italians—identified as 34-year-old Luca Pancal

title: Trump-backed challenger faces Louisiana GOP senator in tight primary
description: Louisiana holds primary as Trump-backed Julia Letlow challenges Republican Senator Bill Cassidy after his impeachment vote. Primary tests Trump’s endorsement po
content: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy of Louisiana faces a tough primary challenge Saturday after former President Donald Trump endorsed his opponent, Julia Letlow. The election tests Trump’s influence in deep-red states and comes after Cassidy’s vote to impeach Trump following the January 6 insurrection. Cassidy, a three-term incumbent, normally would have been favored in Louisiana’s solidly Republican environment. But his impeachment vote put him at odds with Trump, who declared in January that Letlow, a U.S. representative, should challenge Cassidy. Trump’s endorsement of Letlow underscores his c

title: ‘Rehearsals for a Revolution’ Review: A Perceptive, Poignant Chronicle of Great Hopes and Even Greater Disappointments
description: New documentary ‘Rehearsals for a Revolution’ offers Pegah Ahangarani’s sharp personal take on 40 years of post-revolution Iran. Now in theaters.
content: Iranian filmmaker Pegah Ahangarani’s ‘Rehearsals for a Revolution’ arrives at a moment when global attention on Iran remains sharply focused, blending intimate memoir with sweeping political commentary. The documentary, her feature debut, follows a personal chronicle of Iran’s last four decades—from the 1979 revolution through today—threading together public upheaval and private reflection. Ahangarani, best known as an actress in Iranian cinema, steps into the director’s chair with a perspective shaped by exile, memory, and resistance. A Director Finds Her Voice The film’s structure mirrors th

title: Reform UK takes Wakefield Council leadership amid local reactions
description: Reform UK’s Karl Johnson set to lead Wakefield Council after power shift. Hear from residents on the change and its impact on local policies.
content: Reform UK councillor Karl Johnson is poised to take the helm of Wakefield Council after his party’s surprise victory in last week’s elections, ending Labour’s long-standing control. Johnson, a Reform UK Reform UK member representing the Pontefract North ward, will officially be installed as leader at the council’s next full meeting on Monday. The power shift marks the first time Reform UK has led a UK local authority, drawing national attention to Wakefield’s political landscape. Johnson’s appointment follows a tight race in which Reform UK secured 12 seats, up from zero in the previous admini

title: UK renters raise record funds via crowdfunding to cover bills
description: GoFundMe reports rent donations up 60% since 2022, with 100,000 monthly donors helping UK renters avoid eviction. Record 600% surge in April alone.
content: Renters in the UK are turning to crowdfunding in unprecedented numbers to avoid eviction, with GoFundMe reporting a 60% increase in donations for rent and household bills since 2022. In April alone, the platform saw a record number of rent-related fundraisers—more than any month prior—amplifying concerns over the cost-of-living crisis gripping the country. More than 100,000 people now contribute monthly to campaigns helping strangers keep a roof over their heads, up from tens of thousands just two years ago. The surge reflects deepening financial strain, as private rents in the UK hit record l

title: Ray Dalio: China's rise may replace US global defense role

content: Billionaire investor Ray Dalio Ray Dalio warned Monday that the world’s trust in the U.S. as a military defender is eroding, potentially accelerating China’s rise as the new global power broker. Speaking to Bloomberg Television’s Wall Street Week with David Westin, Dalio said countries from Asia to Europe now question whether America will intervene in conflicts, reshaping international alliances. Dalio, founder of Bridgewater Associates with $150 billion in assets under management, cited the U.S. withdrawal from Afghanistan in 2021 as a turning point. He noted that despite maintaining 750 mili

title: Kentish milkwort rebounds 700% after UK conservation push
description: The rare Kentish milkwort plant has seen a seven-fold increase since conservation efforts began in 2013. Learn how targeted protection saved this species from e
content: The Kentish milkwort Pentaglottis sempervirens, a delicate blue wildflower native to southern England, has made a dramatic recovery after nearly disappearing from the wild. Conservationists report a seven-fold increase in its population since 2013, when targeted protection efforts began. Once found in just a handful of locations, the plant now grows in dozens of protected sites across Kent and Sussex. The rebound follows a decade of coordinated action by botanists, landowners and government agencies. Key steps included seed banking, habitat restoration and controlled grazing to prevent overgro

title: Putin to visit China days after Trump's Beijing trip, Kremlin confirms
description: Russian President Putin to meet Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping in Beijing next week. Moscow and Beijing to discuss deepening economic and military ties followin
content: Russian President Vladimir Putin will travel to Beijing next week for high-level talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping, the Kremlin announced Tuesday. The visit is scheduled shortly after former U.S. President Donald Trump’s trip to China, underscoring the timing as significant in global diplomacy. According to the Kremlin, the leaders will discuss ways to deepen their countries’ “comprehensive partnership and strategic cooperation,” signaling Moscow and Beijing’s intent to strengthen economic, military, and political ties. The announcement comes as both Russia and China seek to counter West

title: Putin-Xi talks set for Beijing as Trump China visit ends
description: Russian President Vladimir Putin to meet Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing next week after Trump’s state visit. Few trade resolutions emerged during Trump’s
content: Russian President Vladimir Putin will meet Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing next week for two days of talks, the Kremlin said Saturday. The visit comes just days after US President Donald Trump’s state visit to China, which concluded Friday with high-profile ceremonies but little progress on key issues. Putin-Xi talks set after Trump’s China visit Putin’s trip to Beijing marks a rare high-level meeting between the two leaders since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. The Kremlin did not disclose the agenda but said economic cooperation and regional security would be discussed. Analysts expect th

title: POV: you’re Jim Chalmers using social media to sell the most ambitious budget of your life
description: Australia's treasurer Jim Chalmers shares behind-the-scenes budget prep on social media to connect with digital voters. See how politicians adapt to online tren
content: Australia’s Treasurer Jim Chalmers is breaking from tradition by turning to social media to sell his most ambitious budget yet. In a series of videos released this week, Chalmers provides an unfiltered look at the grueling process behind crafting the federal budget, blending work and personal moments to resonate with digital audiences. The treasurer’s early-morning jogs in running gear and late-night office sessions, illuminated by desk lamps and fueled by sugar-free energy drinks, are designed to humanize the budget process. Chalmers labels the week as “decision week,” signaling the critical

title: Pope Leo XIV to make first papal state visit to France in 18 years
description: Pope Leo XIV to visit Paris in September for first papal state visit to France in 18 years. Vatican confirms upcoming trip.
content: Pope Leo XIV will make his first official state visit to France in nearly two decades when he travels to Paris in September, the Vatican announced Saturday. The announcement comes as the Catholic Church continues global outreach efforts under the leadership of Pope Leo XIV. The Vatican confirmed the trip dates and itinerary, though specific events remain undisclosed. The last papal state visit to France occurred in 2006, when Pope Benedict XVI met with French officials and addressed religious and political leaders. title: AI data centers face discrimination protests over power use and land

content: Artificial intelligence data centers are facing increasing backlash from communities across the United States over their massive energy demands and land use, raising questions about who benefits from the AI boom. Residents in states from Virginia to Iowa are pushing back against plans to build or expand facilities, citing concerns about strained power grids, rising costs and environmental impact. The resistance reflects a growing divide between the tech industry’s rapid expansion and the needs of local populations. Data centers, which power AI systems, consume vast amounts of electricity—some

title: Philippine Leroy-Beaulieu: The art of self-confidence
description: Actress Philippine Leroy-Beaulieu promotes self-confidence at Cannes Film Festival as L’Oréal Paris ambassador. Learn how her journey inspires embracing individ
content: Actress Philippine Leroy-Beaulieu arrives at the Cannes Film Festival this week not just as a guest but as an ambassador for L’Oréal Paris. Her mission: to champion the idea that self-confidence is a gradual process shaped by life’s challenges rather than an instant transformation. Speaking to France 24, she described the journey as learning "not to fear storms." Her role at Cannes aligns with L’Oréal Paris’s global campaign, which emphasizes authenticity over conventional beauty standards. Leroy-Beaulieu’s connection to the festival runs deeper than her ambassadorial duties. She first gained

title: Peacocks overrun Italian seaside town amid local feud over birds
description: Punta Marina, Italy, now resembles a wildlife park as peacocks roam freely, sparking debate among residents. Local ‘peacock rangers’ mediate tensions.
content: PUNTA MARINA, Italy — Federico Bruni was enjoying a piadina romagnola (flatbread sandwich) on a bench when a peacock strutted toward him, hoping for crumbs. The bird’s iridescent tail fanned out behind it as it wandered past, a scene more akin to a wildlife park than an Italian seaside town. This is Punta Marina, a coastal community on the Adriatic Sea in Italy’s Emilia-Romagna region. The town, normally a quiet summer retreat, has been overrun by peacocks, whose loud calls and aggressive behavior have sparked division among residents. Some adore the birds for their striking beauty, while othe

title: PCOS renamed PMOS after 10 years of medical debate
description: Global experts rename PCOS to PMOS to better reflect the disorder’s multisystem effects. Experts announced the change at the European Congress of Endocrinology
content: Medical experts have officially renamed polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) as polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS) following more than a decade of global debate. The change, announced at the European Congress of Endocrinology in Prague, seeks to better reflect the disorder’s widespread impact on the body beyond reproductive health. The new name, PMOS, emphasizes the polyendocrine and metabolic aspects of the condition, which affects an estimated 1 in 8 females worldwide. Historically, PCOS was narrowly defined by ovarian symptoms, but research shows it influences multiple body systems

title: Patricio Valladares Fuses Analog and Interactive in New FMV Horror Video Game (EXCLUSIVE)
description: Chilean filmmaker Patricio Valladares launches first FMV horror video game blending analog filmmaking with interactive choices. Steam and mobile app releases pl
content: Chilean filmmaker Patricio Valladares is stepping into video games with an untitled FMV horror title that fuses analog filmmaking with interactive elements. The English-language project, produced by Vallastudio Films, marks Valladares’ debut in interactive media after years in film and television. The game will be available on Steam and as a mobile app, expanding its reach beyond traditional gaming platforms. Valladares, known for his work in horror films such as The Last International Playboy and Terror 5, is leveraging his cinematic background to create a unique experience. Full motion video

title: Palestinian President Abbas casts vote in decisive Fatah elections
description: Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas casts ballot in Fatah’s internal leadership elections in Ramallah. The vote comes amid questions over succession for the 90-
content: Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas cast his ballot Saturday in Fatah’s internal leadership elections, a vote delayed five years and now held amid Israel’s ongoing war in Gaza and escalating questions over his eventual successor. The elections for the movement’s Central Committee and Revolutionary Council took place at the Ahmad Shuqairi Hall in the presidential headquarters in Ramallah, according to the Wafa news agency. Abbas, 90, has led Fatah since 2005 and the Palestinian Authority since 2005. The eighth Fatah congress, originally scheduled for 2021, was postponed multiple times due to re

title: Dodgers' Pages or Vargas? Best fantasy baseball bat right now
description: Eric Karabell ranks Dodgers' top fantasy baseball bats between James Pages and Julio Vargas for 2024. Which slugger offers better value in roto leagues?
content: Eric Karabell of ESPN declared James Pages the superior fantasy baseball bat over Julio Vargas for the Los Angeles Dodgers in 2024. Pages has emerged as the team’s most consistent power threat, posting 12 home runs in 68 games this season compared to Vargas’ seven in 72 games. Vargas, however, remains the Dodgers’ primary leadoff hitter, offering speed with 10 stolen bases but fewer RBI opportunities due to lower run production. Fantasy managers drafting now must decide whether to prioritize Pages’ power potential or Vargas’ dual-threat capabilities. The Dodgers’ lineup has seen minimal change

title: Ebola kills 80 in DR Congo and Uganda, 246 cases suspected
description: A rare Ebola strain has killed 80 people in DR Congo and Uganda. Health officials report 246 suspected cases amid fears of cross-border spread.
content: At least 80 people have died in a new Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo’s Ituri province, with 246 suspected cases reported, health authorities said. The outbreak, caused by a rare strain, has raised alarms after a Congolese man died in Uganda’s capital, Kampala, marking the first fatality outside DR Congo. The man had traveled from DR Congo before falling ill, according to Uganda’s health ministry. The World Health Organization (WHO) and local health officials are tracking the outbreak, which began in Ituri, a province already grappling with conflict and displacement. The rar

title: OpenAI restructures with Greg Brockman leading product strategy shift
description: OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman takes charge of product strategy as the company merges ChatGPT and Codex. Learn the details of the shift.
content: OpenAI has named co-founder Greg Brockman as its new head of product strategy, the company announced in an internal memo obtained by TechCrunch. The move comes as OpenAI prepares to combine its popular ChatGPT conversational AI with its Codex programming tool. The reorganization follows a series of leadership changes, including the ouster of CEO Sam Altman and subsequent reinstatement in November 2023. Brockman, who previously served as OpenAI’s president, will now oversee the integration of ChatGPT and Codex, two of the company’s most prominent products. The merger aims to streamline OpenAI’s

title: Oil shortages may trigger price spikes within a month, analysts warn
description: Global oil inventories could hit critical lows by June as Strait of Hormuz closure persists. Experts predict price spikes and shortages ahead.
content: Global oil markets stand on the brink of a supply crisis as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz—one of the world’s most critical oil chokepoints—enters its second month with no resolution in sight. The disruption has triggered dire warnings from major energy institutions, including JPMorgan, Saudi Aramco and the International Energy Agency (IEA), all predicting severe shortages and price spikes within weeks. Analysts describe the situation as a tipping point that could force governments and consumers to confront prolonged fuel scarcity and volatile markets. Strait of Hormuz closure deepens sup

title: ‘Obsession’ Director Curry Barker on His Theatrical Debut, Cutting a Darker Ending and Taking on ‘Texas Chainsaw Massacre’ Next
description: Director Curry Barker discusses his horror film ‘Obsession,’ cutting a darker ending and his next project, ‘Texas Chainsaw Massacre.’ Read the full interview no
content: Twenty-six-year-old director Curry Barker made his theatrical debut this week with the twisty horror film Obsession, now playing in theaters nationwide. The film, which Barker originally shot as a social media experiment on TikTok and YouTube, follows a woman who becomes obsessed with a mysterious stranger. Barker revealed he re-edited the ending after test screenings to make it even darker, intensifying the film’s psychological horror elements. Barker’s path from short-form video creator to theatrical director began with his viral horror series Milk Serial, an hour-long film that gained atten

title: NPR’s Manoush Zomorodi talks about living with too much tech
description: NPR host Manoush Zomorodi explores how constant tech use harms physical health in her new book. Learn how screen time affects your body.
content: NPR podcast host Manoush Zomorodi has released a new book examining how constant technology use harms physical health. Body Electric continues her work on the effects of screens on humans, following her 2017 book Bored and Brilliant, which analyzed mental health impacts. The book stems from her podcasting career, including stints at WNYC’s Note To Self and NPR’s TED Radio Hour. Through these platforms, Zomorodi has interviewed experts and conducted research on digital behavior for over a decade. Body Electric expands that focus to include how phones, computers and other devices reshape posture

title: Norway man achieves HIV remission after stem cell transplant
description: A man in Norway achieves long-term HIV remission after a stem cell transplant from his brother. Researchers study rare genetic factors in this breakthrough case
content: OSLO — A man in Norway has entered long-term remission from HIV after receiving a stem cell transplant from his brother, researchers reported Wednesday. The case adds to a small but growing group of patients worldwide who have achieved similar outcomes after such procedures. The patient, identified only as an adult male, was diagnosed with HIV in the 1990s. He later developed a type of blood cancer that required a stem cell transplant. His brother was a rare donor match with a genetic mutation that confers resistance to HIV, known as the CCR5-delta32 mutation. After the 2019 transplant, the ma

title: Northern Lights to dazzle North America Saturday and Sunday nights
description: Millions across North America will see the aurora borealis this weekend as a geomagnetic storm peaks. Best viewing is Saturday night in northern U.S. and Canada
content: A powerful geomagnetic storm will illuminate the night skies over North America this weekend, offering millions their best chance to see the Northern Lights in years. The aurora borealis, known for its vibrant green, purple and pink streaks, will peak Saturday and Sunday nights, according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA). The light show results from charged particles from the sun colliding with Earth’s atmosphere near the magnetic poles. The storm’s intensity is expected to reach its strongest point Saturday night, creating ideal conditions for aurora viewing. Star

title: DR Congo reports 80 deaths in new Ebola outbreak with no vaccine
description: DR Congo confirms 246 suspected Ebola cases, 80 deaths in latest outbreak. No vaccine available for lethal Bundibugyo strain. Health minister warns of severe ri
content: Health authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) Democratic Republic of the Congo confirmed 246 suspected Ebola cases and 80 deaths on Saturday, marking the latest in a series of deadly outbreaks in the country. The current strain, Bundibugyo ebolavirus, has no approved vaccine and carries a high mortality rate, according to Health Minister Samuel-Roger Kamba Samuel-Roger Kamba during a press briefing in Kinshasa. The minister did not specify the timeline for the outbreak’s start but noted the situation remains under close monitoring by national and international health agencies. N

title: Nicole Kidman training to become death doula after mother's death
description: Oscar winner Nicole Kidman is training to become a death doula following her mother’s 2024 passing. Learn what a death doula does and why end-of-life care is ga
content: Oscar-winning actor Nicole Kidman is training to become a death doula following the death of her mother in 2024. The announcement came during a public discussion at the University of San Francisco, where Kidman spoke about shifting her focus toward end-of-life support work. Her decision has sparked broader conversations about the role of death doulas and the need for improved end-of-life care practices. Death doulas, also known as end-of-life doulas, provide non-medical companionship and guidance to individuals nearing death and their families. Unlike hospice workers, they focus on emotional,

title: New GLP-1 drug Survodutide shows 16.6% weight loss in phase 3 trial
description: Boehringer Ingelheim’s survodutide led to 16.6% body weight loss in a phase 3 obesity trial. The dual GLP-1 and glucagon drug may also treat liver disease. Not
content: Boehringer Ingelheim’s survodutide delivered a 16.6% reduction in body weight during the phase 3 SYNCHRONIZE-1 trial, marking a significant advance in obesity treatment. The experimental drug works by activating GLP-1 and glucagon receptors, mimicking hormones that regulate appetite and metabolism. Trial participants, who were obese or overweight with weight-related conditions, saw an average loss of 36.5 pounds over 48 weeks. Waist circumference—a key indicator of metabolic health—also decreased by an average of 4.2 inches. The biopharmaceutical company Boehringer Ingelheim, based in Ingelhei

title: New Zealand level ODI series against England as rain wreaks havoc in Cardiff
description: England and New Zealand’s ODI series ends level after rain disrupts two chases at Sophia Gardens. The T20I series now begins as both teams prepare for the T20 W
content: Rain twice forced the abandonment of England’s ODI chase against New Zealand at Sophia Gardens on Friday, ending the series level at 1-1 after the White Ferns won by 17 runs via the Duckworth-Lewis system. The match, originally scheduled for 50 overs, was reduced to 33 overs per side after a two-and-a-half-hour delay, with England finishing on 181-7. New Zealand’s revised target of 184 was within reach when the weather intervened again, forcing the umpires to call an end to the contest with the hosts 141-4 in 20 overs. England, put in to bat, started strongly before the first rain delay arrive

title: Long Island Rail Road shut down as unions strike in New York
description: North America’s largest commuter rail system halted Saturday after unions representing half its workforce walked out. Service disruption hits New York City’s ea
content: The shutdown began early Saturday after five unions representing about 3,000 workers at the Long Island Rail Road (LIRR) initiated a strike. The action halted operations on the system serving New York City’s eastern suburbs, including Nassau and Suffolk counties, and parts of Queens. The Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA), which oversees the LIRR, confirmed the strike began at 12:01 a.m. and no trains would operate until further notice. Commuters in the region scrambled to find alternative transportation after the announcement. The LIRR carries about 350,000 daily riders, making this

title: Ozempic oral pill approved for type 2 diabetes in U.S.
description: FDA approves new Ozempic pill for type 2 diabetes. Experts say oral tablet may replace injections for some patients. Ends daily Rybelsus use.
content: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has approved a new oral tablet formulation of Ozempic, the widely prescribed GLP-1 medication for type 2 diabetes. Manufactured by Novo Nordisk, the oral tablet became available on May 4 for adults diagnosed with the condition. The drug replaces Rybelsus, Novo Nordisk’s previous daily oral medication approved in 2019 for type 2 diabetes. Experts say the new Ozempic pill may offer a more convenient option for patients who avoid weekly injections. The oral tablet comes in smaller doses compared to the injectable version, which has become increasingly popular

title: Ebola outbreak kills 80 in eastern DR Congo, 246 cases reported
description: Ebola kills 80 in eastern DR Congo as Africa CDC reports 246 cases, including one in Uganda. Follow case updates and response efforts.
content: An Ebola virus outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo has killed 80 people and sickened at least 246 since the surge began, the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) reported Tuesday. The outbreak, which emerged in the volatile North Kivu and Ituri provinces, marks the latest in a series of deadly resurgences in the region since 2018. Health authorities confirmed the first cross-border case on Tuesday after a patient from DR Congo traveled to southwestern Uganda and tested positive for Ebola. The patient, who was asymptomatic upon arrival, developed symptoms

title: May 2026’s new moon perfect for viewing Milky Way, Venus and Mars
description: May 2026’s new moon offers ideal dark skies for Milky Way viewing and bright planets Venus and Mars. Perfect timing for stargazers and astrophotographers.
content: The night of May 11, 2026, will bring the darkest skies of the month as the new moon rises, creating perfect conditions for stargazing and astrophotography. The Milky Way’s glowing band will stretch vertically across the sky, visible to the naked eye in areas free from light pollution. Bright planets Venus and Mars will also shine prominently after midnight, offering a rare celestial show. Astronomers recommend heading to dark-sky preserves or remote locations at least an hour after sunset to let eyes adjust fully to the darkness. The Milky Way will appear most vivid in the eastern sky, rising

title: New Breast Density Treatment May Have Fewer Side Effects, Lower Cancer Risk
description: A new breast cancer prevention drug, (Z)-endoxifen, reduces mammographic breast density more safely than tamoxifen, according to a recent clinical trial. It may
content: > 📰 Continuing coverage: GLP-1 drugs linked to better breast cancer survival in new study A clinical trial published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute on April 27 shows that the experimental drug (Z)-endoxifen reduces mammographic breast density more safely than tamoxifen, a common breast cancer prevention treatment. The study, led by researchers at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, found that (Z)-endoxifen not only matched tamoxifen’s effectiveness but also caused fewer problematic side effects, including hot flashes and joint pain. title: NASA's Psyche spacecraft is flying to a metal asteroid. So, why did it just visit Mars?
description: NASA’s Psyche spacecraft will perform a Mars flyby in 2026 to boost its speed toward a metal asteroid. Here’s how gravity assists work and why they matter.
content: NASA’s Psyche spacecraft will make a key pit stop next year—gravity assisting past Mars on May 15, 2026. The flyby is not just a detour but a calculated move to accelerate the probe toward its ultimate destination: 16 Psyche, a metallic asteroid orbiting the sun between Mars and Jupiter. This maneuver will save fuel and shave years off the journey, a standard but essential tactic for deep-space exploration. title: ‘Ms. Marvel’ Star Iman Vellani to Lead Zarrar Kahn’s Climate Thriller ‘Suffering Is Optional’ (EXCLUSIVE)
description: Iman Vellani, star of 'Ms. Marvel,' will lead Zarrar Kahn’s climate thriller 'Suffering Is Optional,' a Canadian co-production exploring environmental themes th

title: GLP-1 drugs cut heart risks in obese patients by 30% in major study
description: New research shows tirzepatide in Mounjaro and Zepbound reduces heart risks for people with serious heart conditions. Experts warn lifestyle changes remain esse
content: Weight-loss medications containing the active ingredient tirzepatide—sold under the brand names Mounjaro and Zepbound—have been found to significantly reduce cardiovascular risks in people with serious heart conditions, according to research published this week. The drugs, classified as GLP-1 receptor agonists, work by helping patients lose weight and better control blood sugar levels, both of which are critical factors in managing obesity-related heart disease. title: Motherwell secure European football with victory at Hibernian
description: Motherwell secured fourth in Scottish Premiership with 1-0 win at Hibs, booking Europa Conference League qualifiers. Celtic beat Hearts to reach Scottish Cup fi
content: Motherwell secured their fourth-place finish in the Scottish Premiership and a spot in the Europa Conference League qualifiers with a 1-0 victory over Hibernian at Easter Road on Saturday. Lukas Fadinger struck the decisive goal in the 35th minute, capitalizing on a rebound after Hibs goalkeeper Joe Hart parried a shot from Callum Slattery. The visitors held firm despite late pressure and a numerical disadvantage after Elliot Watt was shown a second yellow card midway through the second half. Stand-in goalkeeper Matty Connelly made no saves but his positioning and composure under pressure ensu

title: Monaghan legend reveals how to stop Armagh in Ulster SFC final
description: Conor McManus says Monaghan must target Armagh’s defense to win Sunday’s Ulster SFC final at Clones. Full analysis ahead of the clash.
content: Monaghan must target Armagh’s defense in the final 20 minutes if they hope to derail the free-scoring Ulster champions on Sunday. That’s the assessment from Conor McManus, the former Monaghan forward and current BBC pundit, who believes the Ulster Senior Football Championship final will hinge on who seizes the initiative late in the game. Armagh arrive in Clones as firm favorites after breezing past Derry in the semi-final, where they racked up 2-19 to 0-10. The Orchard County has averaged 2-15 per game in this year’s competition, including a 10-point hammering of Donegal in the quarter-final.

title: 8 killed as freight train crashes into Bangkok bus
description: Eight people died and 25 were injured when a freight train collided with a public bus in Bangkok, Thailand. Authorities are investigating the cause of the crash
content: A freight train plowed into a public bus in Bangkok on Monday, killing eight people and injuring 25 others, authorities said. The collision occurred near the historic Hua Lamphong railway station, a major transit hub in Thailand’s capital. content: Eighteen Americans were airlifted to the U.S. after traveling on an Antarctic expedition cruise ship linked to a rare Andes hantavirus outbreak. The U.S. State Department confirmed the passengers arrived safely, though health officials continue monitoring the situation closely. The outbreak raises concerns about the virus’s spread in confined spaces, but experts stress that the broader risk of a COVID-like pandemic remains low. title: Hamas Qassam Brigades leader killed in Israeli airstrike in Gaza

content: Israel targeted and killed Izz al-Din al-Haddad, the head of Hamas’s armed wing in Gaza, in an airstrike Friday evening that also killed six others, including his wife and daughter. Hamas confirmed the death in a statement Saturday, calling the attack a “treacherous and cowardly assassination.” The strike hit the Remal neighborhood west of Gaza City, according to medical sources and local reports. title: Gunmen kidnap dozens of students in Nigeria’s Borno State
description: Gunmen kidnapped dozens of students from a Borno State school. No group claimed the attack, but Boko Haram’s tactics raise fears. Residents report armed assaila
content: Armed men kidnapped at least 30 students from a school in Nigeria’s Borno State on Friday, local residents and officials confirmed. The attack occurred at Mussa Primary and Junior Secondary School in Askira-Uba Local Government Area around 9 a.m. as classes were in session. Witnesses reported the assailants arrived on motorcycles, forcibly taking several children before fleeing into nearby bushland. title: GLP-1 drugs linked to better breast cancer survival in new study
description: GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic may reduce mortality and recurrence risk in breast cancer patients with obesity or type 2 diabetes. More research needed.
content: A recent study published in Journal Name suggests that GLP-1 drugs such as Ozempic and Wegovy may improve survival rates and reduce recurrence risk in breast cancer patients who also have obesity or type 2 diabetes. The research, which analyzed data from thousands of patients, found that those taking GLP-1 medications had a lower risk of death compared to those treated with insulin or metformin alone. title: Germany poll: 48% want conservative coalition government dissolved
description: A new survey shows only 30% of Germans support the current conservative-led coalition government. Berlin will see pro-Palestinian protests marking Nakba Day.
content: A survey released Friday shows nearly half of Germans want the conservative-led coalition government dissolved, with only 30% backing its continuation. The poll, conducted amid growing public dissatisfaction, reflects widespread discontent with the government's performance since taking office in 2025. Critics point to economic struggles and social tensions as key factors eroding support for the ruling coalition led by Friedrich Merz. title: Germany news: Dead humpback whale off Denmark is 'Timmy,' who spent weeks stranded in Baltic Sea
description: Divers confirm dead humpback whale off Denmark is ‘Timmy,’ the same creature stranded for weeks in Germany’s Baltic Sea. Learn more about the stranding and Bund
content: Divers confirmed Friday that a dead humpback whale spotted off Denmark’s Anholt island is ‘Timmy,’ the same creature that spent weeks beached along Germany’s Baltic Sea coast. Marine biologists and local officials identified the whale through distinctive markings after it was first sighted in distress near the German island of Fehmarn in late April. The animal had become a local symbol of concern as rescue attempts and public attention grew over its prolonged struggle to free itself from shallow waters. title: Gen Z wants AI-proof jobs. The president of a 50-property hotel chain says hospitality is hiding in plain sight
description: Hospitality jobs remain AI-proof as Gen Z enters the workforce. Omni Hotels president says attitude beats experience in hiring. Attitude and service drive succe
content: As forecasts suggest work may become optional in coming years, Gen Z is racing to secure careers that defy automation. The class of 2026 is pivoting toward industries where human connection remains irreplaceable—and hospitality stands out as a prime contender. Kurt Alexander, president of Omni Hotels & Resorts—which manages over 50 properties across North America—argues the sector offers clear advantages for young job seekers. “Hospitality is a learnable business,” Alexander told Fortune. “But what you can’t teach is an attitude of service. That’s the core of what we do, and it’s not for every

title: Amputee cyclists from Gaza arrive at Cannes by boat for film premiere
description: Palestinian paracycling team 'Gaza Sunbirds' makes historic sea arrival at Cannes Film Festival for documentary screening. Fight for visibility and hope documen
content: A team of Palestinian amputee cyclists arrived at the Cannes Film Festival on Monday by boat, marking a historic first for the 'Gaza Sunbirds' documentary premiere. The crew, composed entirely of athletes from Gaza, sailed into the French Riviera to symbolize their journey from blockade to global representation. The documentary, titled 'Gaza Sunbirds,' follows the lives of six members of the Palestinian paracycling team, all amputees from Gaza. Their story centers on survival, resilience, and the transformative power of sport despite the challenges of living in a conflict zone. The film captur

title: Gaza flotilla sails for a third time despite threats: 500 activists depart
description: 500 activists depart southern Turkey on third Global Sumud Flotilla to deliver aid to Gaza despite Israeli interception threats. Follow live updates.
content: A flotilla of ships carrying 500 activists and humanitarian aid set sail Thursday from southern Turkey on a third attempt to reach Gaza, defying repeated warnings from Israel that it would intercept the vessels in international waters. The Global Sumud Flotilla, organized by international solidarity groups, aims to break the Israeli-Egyptian blockade of Gaza by delivering medical supplies, construction materials and other essential goods to the Palestinian territory. title: Gaza’s 85-year-old Nakba survivor recounts 75 years of displacement
description: Abdel Mahdi al-Wuheidi, 85, survived the 1948 Nakba and Gaza’s 2023 war. His story reflects decades of Palestinian displacement and resilience.
content: JABALIA, Gaza — Inside the hollowed-out shell of his home in the Jabalia refugee camp, Abdel Mahdi al-Wuheidi, 85, stirs a small fire to brew coffee. The walls around him are cracked, the roof partially collapsed, and the belongings he once treasured lie buried under rubble. Next to him sits his wife, Aziza, also in her 80s, whom he married 60 years ago. The couple never had children, but they raised five nephews after their father died when they were young. Today, those nephews and their families share the damaged space with them. Al-Wuheidi was just eight years old when the 1948 Nakba forced

title: From non-league to FA Cup hero - Semenyo caps rise with 'incredible' goal
description: Antoine Semenyo’s dramatic 7-goal FA Cup run culminated in a final-winning strike for Bristol City. Full story of his journey from non-league to hero. .
content: Bristol City’s Antoine Semenyo etched his name into FA Cup history by scoring the winner in Sunday’s 2-1 victory over Manchester City Manchester City F.C. at Wembley. The 24-year-old attacker, who joined Bristol City from non-league Bath City in 2020, completed a seven-goal haul across seven rounds to end a 14-year absence from the competition’s final stage for the south-west club. His 39th-minute strike broke a deadlock and handed City their first major trophy since the 1909 FA Amateur Cup. title: Museums shift focus from objects to people in modern role

content: Museums are undergoing a fundamental change as they move beyond their traditional role of housing and displaying objects. On International Museum Day 2026, the focus is on how these institutions are transforming into dynamic community spaces that prioritize human connection over static exhibits. The shift reflects a broader societal demand for relevance and accessibility in cultural institutions. Sandro Debono, a museum consultant and academic, told DW that this evolution represents a move toward museums that serve as platforms for participation rather than mere repositories of artifacts. The decision follows prolonged legal battles over whether the case could proceed under universal jurisdiction principles, which allow nations to prosecute certain crimes regardless of where they occurred. title: Patrick Bruel denies rape allegations by TV presenter Flavie Flament
description: French star Patrick Bruel denies rape allegations from TV presenter Flavie Flament who claims assault occurred when she was 16. Investigation ongoing.

title: 8 killed as freight train hits bus in Bangkok, fire erupts
description: Eight people died after a freight train collided with a bus in Bangkok, sparking a fire that engulfed the vehicle. Emergency crews responded to the scene.
content: A freight train slammed into a passenger bus in Bangkok on Thursday, killing at least eight people and igniting a fire that consumed the vehicle, authorities reported. The collision occurred around 7:30 a.m. local time near the Siam Park area in the city’s eastern outskirts, according to officials with the Royal Thai Police. Emergency responders found the bus engulfed in flames, with passengers trapped inside. title: France tracks hantavirus cases after cruise ship outbreak
description: France confirms at least one hantavirus case after a cruise ship outbreak. Health officials monitor contacts as disease spreads. Period.
content: French health authorities confirmed the first case of hantavirus in the country after an outbreak linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship. The patient, currently in intensive care, is among 26 people being monitored for potential exposure. French officials are tracking four individuals still onboard the vessel, while 22 others who were exposed but show no symptoms are also under observation. The outbreak raises concerns about the spread of the rare virus in confined maritime environments. Health authorities in France have not disclosed the patient’s identity or location. title: Study: Fluoride in water won’t lower children’s IQ
description: Large study finds no link between fluoridated water and IQ loss in children or cognitive decline in older adults. Confirms fluoride’s role in cavity prevention.
content: A large-scale study has found no evidence that fluoride in drinking water affects intelligence or cognitive abilities at any age. The research, published April 13 in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS), analyzed data from thousands of participants across multiple regions, comparing those exposed to fluoridated water with those who were not. The results showed no measurable differences in IQ scores or cognitive decline between the two groups. title: Florida tightens manta ray capture rules after viral video outrage
description: Florida wildlife officials adopt new rules on manta ray capture after viral video showed severe distress. Permits still allowed for exhibition use.
content: Wildlife officials in Florida Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission voted unanimously on Wednesday to adopt amended rules governing the capture of giant manta rays in state waters. The decision comes after a bipartisan push from lawmakers and public outrage sparked by a viral video showing a ray being roughly handled and placed into an unmarked boat off Florida’s coast last year. While the new regulations impose stricter controls, they do not impose an outright ban on capturing the threatened species for “responsible exhibition” in marine facilities. title: French Road Romance ‘Flesh and Fuel’ premieres at Cannes Critics’ Week
description: French feature ‘Flesh and Fuel’ debuts at Cannes Critics’ Week with Alexis Manenti in a trucker romance set across Europe. See the trailer and reviews.
content: French director Pierre Le Gall’s debut feature ‘Flesh and Fuel’ premiered as a special screening in Cannes Critics’ Week on May 15. The film stars Alexis Manenti, known for his role in Les Misérables), as one of the two central characters navigating Europe’s highways in an unlikely road romance. The film explores themes of loneliness and connection through the unlikely bond between two truckers. Set against the backdrop of Europe’s vast network of highways, ‘Flesh and Fuel’ blends romance with the gritty realities of long-haul trucking. title: Finnish duo, Australian star lead Eurovision 2024 boycotted final
description: Finland and Australia's acts are favorites as Saturday's Eurovision final faces an unprecedented boycott over Israel's inclusion. Catch the latest updates.
content: Finland’s Käärijä and Australia’s Electric Fields are the betting favorites to win Saturday’s Eurovision 2024 grand final in Malmö, Sweden. Both acts have drawn strong praise for their performances, but the contest is overshadowed by an unprecedented boycott over Israel’s inclusion after the country’s ongoing conflict in Gaza.
