title: FDA shuffles top drug regulators after Makary exit
description: The FDA is reorganizing its drug and biologics leadership team following the departure of former commissioner Marty Makary. Here’s what changed.
content: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration FDA is overhauling its top drug and biologics regulators, a move announced Tuesday that follows the sudden departure of former commissioner Marty Makary. The shakeup, described in an internal memo, includes shifts in leadership roles responsible for overseeing prescription drugs, vaccines, and medical devices. While the agency has not detailed the full scope of the changes, sources familiar with the matter say the reorganization aims to improve efficiency in drug approvals and post-market oversight. title: FDA approves new at-home cervical cancer HPV test kit
description: FDA clears another at-home cervical cancer screening kit to expand HPV testing access. Experts say new options may cut diagnosis delays and prevent deaths.
content: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has authorized another at-home test kit for cervical cancer screening, giving patients a second option to collect their own HPV samples without visiting a clinic. The Onclarity HPV assay from Waters Corporation received FDA clearance this week, marking a shift toward broader access to early detection tools that could save lives by catching precancerous changes sooner. title: FDA Flags Over 3 Million Bottles of Eye Drops. Is Yours Affected?
description: FDA issues voluntary recall of 3.1 million eye drops from Walgreens, CVS due to sterility risks. No infections reported yet. Check your bottle now.
content: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration FDA on Thursday issued a voluntary recall covering 3.1 million bottles of over-the-counter eye drops sold at major pharmacy chains including Walgreens and CVS. The recall was initiated by California-based K.C. Pharmaceuticals after the FDA raised concerns about sterility during routine inspection of the manufacturing facility in 2023. The agency reported no infections or illnesses linked to the recalled products as of Friday morning’s update. title: FDA blocks vaccine safety studies publication sparking expert alarm
description: FDA halts release of COVID-19 and shingles vaccine safety studies amid expert concerns over transparency and public health impact. FDA says studies had flawed c
content: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) https://www.fda.gov has blocked the publication of multiple studies examining the safety of widely used COVID-19 and shingles vaccines, citing concerns over their conclusions. The move has drawn sharp criticism from public health experts who argue that the vaccines have undergone extensive review and have robust evidence supporting their safety and effectiveness. title: Eurovision 2026: Final starts amid boycott over Israel dispute
description: Five countries boycott Eurovision 2026 over Israel’s participation as the contest begins in Vienna amid protests and a Gaza war dispute. Viewing figures expecte
content: The Eurovision Song Contest final began in Vienna on Saturday, overshadowed by a boycott of five countries protesting Israel’s participation in the world’s biggest live music competition. Spain, Ireland, the Netherlands, Iceland and Slovenia pulled out, reducing the field to 25 acts—the smallest since 2003. Contest organizers had planned for 35 entries before the withdrawals. Television viewing figures are expected to fall below last year’s 162 million, reflecting the growing impact of geopolitical disputes on the annual event. title: Protests erupt in Vienna as Eurovision final overshadowed by Israel
description: Hundreds protest Israel’s Eurovision 2026 participation in Vienna amid Gaza war tensions. Security tightened at Wiener Stadthalle before grand final.
content: Hundreds of demonstrators took to the streets of Vienna on Saturday to protest Israel’s inclusion in Eurovision 2026, turning the spotlight away from the annual song contest’s grand final at Wiener Stadthalle. Police in riot gear responded to the clashes, reinforcing security around the venue where 26 acts competed for the title. The protests reflected broader global tensions tied to the ongoing conflict in Gaza, with activists calling for Israel’s exclusion from the competition. title: Farmers vs elephants in Sri Lanka as human-wildlife conflict escalates
description: Sri Lankan farmers and elephants increasingly clash as crop raids rise, leading to deadly encounters for both. Endangered species face new threats from expandin
content: In Sri Lanka, once revered as sacred, elephants now face growing hostility from farmers struggling to protect their livelihoods. As farmland encroaches on traditional elephant habitats, raids on crops have surged, pushing both species into deadly confrontations. Last year alone, 43 people and 316 elephants died in such conflicts, according to Sri Lanka’s Department of Wildlife Conservation. Farmers say they have no choice but to defend their fields. title: Egg coffee trend spikes on TikTok but health risks concern experts

content: A sweet, creamy coffee trend is spreading across social media, but health experts are pushing back. Egg coffee, made by whisking egg yolks with sugar and condensed milk into a custard-like foam before pouring it over coffee, has amassed millions of views on TikTok. The drink’s viral appeal lies in its rich texture and customizable flavors, but public health authorities warn it may pose serious risks depending on egg preparation methods. title: Eating Avocado and Mango Daily May Improve Blood Pressure in Prediabetes
description: Eating one avocado and one cup of mango daily for 8 weeks improved blood vessel function and lowered diastolic blood pressure in prediabetes patients. Experts s
content: Eating one avocado and 1 cup of mango daily for eight weeks significantly improved blood vessel function and lowered diastolic blood pressure in adults with prediabetes, according to research published in the journal Nutrients. The study, led by researchers at Rush University in Chicago, found improvements in flow-mediated dilation—a key measure of blood vessel health—within just two months. title: DRC reports 80 deaths in Ebola outbreak with 50% lethality rate
description: The Democratic Republic of Congo confirms 80 Ebola deaths with a Bundibugyo strain lethality rate up to 50%. Health officials warn of rapid spread amid regional
content: The Democratic Republic of the Congo’s Health Ministry) confirmed 80 deaths on Saturday from a new Ebola outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo strain, a hemorrhagic fever with no approved vaccine and a lethality rate up to 50%. The outbreak, the country’s seventeenth, was declared Friday in Ituri province, bordering Uganda and South Sudan. Nearly 250 suspected cases have been recorded, according to officials, with one death reported in Uganda raising fears of regional spread. DRC Health Minister Samuel-Roger Kamba warned Saturday that the Bundibugyo strain’s lack of vaccine and high mortality rate

title: Down and then out in Paris and London? Why Starmer isn’t the only one with a popularity problem
description: UK and European leaders face sharp drops in popularity as they deliver tough economic news to increasingly frustrated voters. Polls show Starmer's approval at j
content: Voter frustration is surging across Europe as political leaders roll out unpopular economic policies to address mounting challenges. In the UK, Keir Starmer has become the latest leader to bear the brunt of public anger, with polling from YouGov revealing that just 11% of Britons believe he has been a good or great prime minister. Nearly 60% rate his performance as poor or terrible, a stark contrast to the high expectations set when he took office in 2024. The discontent has been exacerbated by a string of disastrous local election results, which saw Labour suffer heavy losses in key regions.

title: Dodgers' Blake Snell scratched, lands on IL with elbow issue
description: Dodgers left-hander Blake Snell scratched from start due to loose bodies in left elbow, placed on IL indefinitely. Latest MLB injury update.
content: Los Angeles Dodgers left-hander Blake Snell did not take the mound Friday at Angel Stadium after team doctors discovered loose bodies in his left elbow. Snell, 31, had allowed four earned runs over 4.2 innings in his previous outing, a 6-5 loss to the Seattle Mariners on April 5. title: Documentary Filmmaker Brian Lindstrom Dies at 65 After Battle With Rare Brain Disease
description: Director Brian Lindstrom, known for films about society’s forgotten, died at 65 after a years-long battle with progressive supranuclear palsy. His wife is bests
content: Oregon documentary filmmaker Brian Lindstrom died Friday at 65 after a years-long battle with progressive supranuclear palsy, his wife, bestselling author Cheryl Strayed, announced. Lindstrom’s death was confirmed by Strayed in a social media post Friday evening, though the date of his passing was not immediately disclosed. Progressive supranuclear palsy, a rare neurodegenerative disorder, progressively affects movement, balance, speech and cognition, often leading to severe disability within a decade of diagnosis. title: Study Finds Dreams of Fear or Joy May Shape Your Morning Mood
description: New research links dream content to morning mood. Fear in dreams may lower mood, while joy could boost it. Here’s what sleep experts say.
content: Dreams of fear or joy may do more than just entertain you at night—they could influence how you feel when you wake up. A new study published in Sleep reveals that the emotions experienced in dreams can affect your mood the following morning. Researchers found that people who dreamed of fear were more likely to wake up in a lower mood, while those who dreamed of joy reported better psychological function after waking. title: CDC warns drug-resistant Shigella superbug cases rising in US
description: CDC reports a surge in extensively drug-resistant Shigella infections in the U.S., resistant to five common antibiotics. Learn symptoms, transmission and preven
content: The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) issued a health alert about a surge in extensively drug-resistant (XDR) Shigella infections across the country. These strains, which cause shigellosis, a severe diarrheal illness, now resist five commonly prescribed antibiotics. The CDC reports cases have climbed steadily since 2020, raising concerns among public health officials about treatment options and transmission risks. title: Colorado governor faces backlash for clemency to 2020 election denier
description: Colorado's Democratic governor pardons Republican former clerk sentenced to 9 years for election tampering. Political firestorm erupts over controversial clemen
content: Colorado Gov. Jared Polis faced immediate backlash after pardoning Republican former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters, despite her nine-year sentence for election equipment tampering. Peters, a prominent 2020 election denier, was convicted in 2022 for breaching security protocols tied to a breach of voting machines following the presidential vote. The pardon, issued without public explanation, reignited partisan tensions over election security and the appropriate consequences for undermining democratic processes. title: WHO urged to declare climate crisis a global health emergency
description: Experts tell WHO climate crisis kills millions and needs emergency status for urgent response. Act now to save lives.
content: The World Health Organization must declare the climate crisis a global public health emergency to avoid millions of unnecessary deaths, a high-level commission has warned. The independent pan-European commission on climate and health, convened by the WHO, argued that the escalating environmental crisis poses such a severe threat to global health that it warrants the highest alert level under international law. title: Scientists accidentally detect possible dark matter signal in data
description: Researchers say an unexplained signal in data may point to dark matter's existence. Here’s what we know so far.
content: A research team analyzing cosmic microwave background radiation stumbled upon an unexplained signal that could hint at dark matter’s presence. The discovery, published in the journal Physical Review Letters, emerged during routine data processing and was not part of an intentional hunt for dark matter. The signal appears as a slight distortion in the radiation map, which scientists say aligns with theoretical predictions of dark matter interactions. content: Notable Speech delivered a masterclass at Newbury on Saturday, claiming his fifth Group 1 triumph in the Lockinge Stakes while securing his future as a stallion. The 2-1 favorite, trained by Charlie Appleby and ridden by William Buick, dominated the Group One feature from the three-furlong pole, crossing the line two lengths clear of More Thunder), who ran a career-best for William Haggas). Appleby, who doubled up at Newmarket on Friday after a quiet spell at Moulton Paddocks, said Notable Speech’s performance confirmed his exceptional pedigree. 'He’s got some turn of foot,' Appleby told repor

title: Andy Burnham floats mass renationalisation if he becomes UK PM
description: Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham suggests Labour should renationalise energy and water if he replaces Keir Starmer as prime minister. Ends debate on public
content: Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham has outlined a plan to bring energy and water companies back under public control if he replaces Keir Starmer as prime minister. The proposal signals a sharp shift in Labour’s economic policy and places Burnham at odds with Starmer’s more centrist approach. Burnham, a former Labour cabinet minister, is positioning himself as the standard-bearer for the party’s soft-left faction ahead of a potential leadership challenge. title: Andalucía election 2024: PP set to win, Vox gains in Spain’s key vote
description: Southern Spain’s Andalucía votes Sunday in a regional election seen as a bellwether for Spain’s 2025 general election. Conservatives expected to extend their le
content: Andalucía’s regional election on Sunday will determine whether the conservative People’s Party) (PP) solidifies its dominance in Spain’s most populous region and sets the stage for next year’s general election. Polls suggest the PP, led by Juanma Moreno, will secure an outright majority, extending its control over Andalucía’s 109 seats. This would mark a historic third term for the PP in a region long considered a Socialist stronghold. title: ‘An hour of abuse’: Jeremy Corbyn on Labour coups, and whether he feels sorry for Starmer
description: Jeremy Corbyn calls Keir Starmer's situation 'devastating' as Labour faces internal unrest. Read his blunt assessment of Starmer's leadership.
content: Jeremy Corbyn, the former leader of the UK’s Labour Party, has expressed sympathy for Keir Starmer as the current leader faces mounting pressure within his own party. In a rare moment of candor, Corbyn admitted that he feels sorry for Starmer, describing the ordeal as a deeply personal and devastating experience. “Yeah, I do feel [sorry for him],” Corbyn said, pausing slightly before adding, “On a personal level it must be devastating. It is a horrible feeling. You suddenly realise that this person doesn’t trust you at all and really doesn’t wish you well at all, and you suddenly realise that

title: Amid the fertiliser crisis, Africa has a chemical-free option: Agroecology
description: Africa faces severe food shortages from the global fertiliser crisis. Agroecology offers a sustainable, chemical-free solution to boost yields and self-sufficie
content: The US-Israel conflict in Iran is crippling global fertiliser supplies, with 20 percent of exports unable to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. Shipments of natural gas and sulphur, critical for fertiliser production, are also blocked, driving up costs worldwide. In Africa, where food insecurity is already rampant, this crisis threatens to deepen shortages and push millions closer to famine. The World Food Programme estimates that over 280 million people in Africa face acute food insecurity, a number that could rise sharply if fertiliser shortages persist. title: Alonso set to become Chelsea manager after Real Madrid exit
description: Xabi Alonso agrees to become Chelsea's new manager after the club sacked Liam Rosenior in April. The former Real Madrid boss will be officially confirmed next w
content: Chelsea have finalized an agreement with Xabi Alonso to take over as the club’s head coach, ending weeks of speculation over the Blues’ managerial vacancy. The former Real Madrid manager was the club’s top target after Liam Rosenior was dismissed in April following a dismal start to his tenure. Rosenior lasted just four months in charge, with Chelsea languishing in ninth place after picking up one point from their last seven Premier League matches. Alonso will now oversee the club’s summer transfer strategy, including potential signings to bolster the squad ahead of the new season. The announc

title: Exclusive: Al Jazeera reports on rising tensions in Strait of Hormuz
description: Al Jazeera gains exclusive access to the Strait of Hormuz as heavy ship traffic and regional tensions spark fears of a wider confrontation. Ship captains and an
content: The Strait of Hormuz, a narrow shipping lane between Iran and Oman, is experiencing heavy traffic amid escalating regional tensions that experts warn could trigger a wider confrontation. Al Jazeera’s correspondent Tohid Asadi secured rare access to the strait, documenting the surge in commercial and military vessel movements that have heightened concerns among ship captains and analysts. title: AI’s cyborg problem: you have to embrace it to really succeed but 90% of people can’t or don’t want to
description: Most employees still avoid AI tools even as studies show they boost productivity by 40% or more. Why adoption remains a challenge for businesses.
content: Fortune editor Nick Lichtenberg [](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fortune(magazine)) became an unlikely lightning rod in the journalism world after the Wall Street Journal profiled his use of artificial intelligence to draft articles, synthesize interviews, and accelerate reporting. The response was immediate and polarized: some colleagues praised his efficiency, while others accused him of laziness or ethical compromise. Strangers on social media labeled him a fraud, while a few journalists admitted privately they used the same tools but would never acknowledge it publicly. The debate exposed

title: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki: ISIL’s shadow commander in West Africa
description: US and Nigeria confirm death of Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, ISIL’s second-in-command in West Africa, in a Nigerian-US military operation in Borno state. ISWAP linked m
content: Nigeria and the United States announced Saturday that Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, a top ISIL commander in West Africa, was killed in a joint military operation in Borno state. Former US President Donald Trump first revealed the death in a social media post Friday, while Nigerian President Bola Tinubu confirmed it Saturday. The operation targeted al-Minuki’s compound in the Lake Chad Basin, near the town of Metele, and resulted in the deaths of several of his lieutenants, according to Nigerian military officials. The Nigerian army described the strike as a "meticulously planned and highly complex prec

title: Absolutely ludicrous!' | Rory McIlroy drives 397 yard-green!
description: Rory McIlroy hits 397-yard drive at PGA Championship, lands on sixth green in third round. Watch the longest drive of the tournament so far.
content: Rory McIlroy did the unthinkable on Friday at the PGA Championship, unleashing a 397-yard drive that landed on the sixth green during his third-round play. The Northern Irish golfer, a four-time major winner, delivered the longest shot of the tournament so far, drawing immediate attention on social media and among spectators at Valhalla Golf Club in Louisville, Kentucky. The drive surpassed all others measured that day, showcasing McIlroy’s power and precision under pressure. title: Labor under fire for underfunding Australia’s spending watchdog ANAO
description: Labor faces criticism for insufficient funding of the Australian National Audit Office, risking its ability to conduct independent reviews. Budget cuts spark co
content: Labor has come under fire for failing to allocate sufficient funding to the Australian National Audit Office (ANAO) Australian National Audit Office, one of the country’s key accountability institutions. Transparency advocates and independent senator David Pocock David Pocock have criticized the Albanese government’s budget commitments, arguing the lack of resources threatens the ANAO’s ability to conduct independent reviews effectively. Audit Office warns of unsustainable financial position The criticism follows repeated warnings from Parliament’s Joint Committee of Public Accounts and Audit

title: 33% of Britons say they moved social class, Attest survey shows
description: New Attest survey reveals 33% of Britons believe they moved social class. Upper-class groups most likely to identify as ‘polyclass.’
content: More than a third of Britons say they have changed social class, according to a new study by Attest), a research firm. The survey also found that 6 million people—about 9% of the population—consider themselves to belong to more than one social category, a phenomenon researchers are calling ‘polyclass.’ Upper-middle and upper-class respondents were most likely to identify as polyclass, while working-class individuals were the least likely to report any change in class or multiple class identities. Among working-class respondents, 70% said they remained in the same social category they were born

title: A state banquet, selfies with Musk and Huang's noodle run: The spectacle of Trump's Beijing visit
description: Donald Trump's Beijing visit featured a state banquet, meetings with Elon Musk and Ren Zhengfei, and a viral noodle stop. Here’s what happened in three days of
content: Donald Trump’s Beijing visit this week was a mix of high diplomacy, viral moments, and business negotiations, culminating in a state banquet with China’s president Xi Jinping. The spectacle included selfies with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, a surprise stop at a local noodle shop, and meetings with Chinese tech leaders, underscoring the trip’s dual focus on politics and commerce. The visit, which ran from Tuesday to Thursday, began with orchestrated pageantry as Trump and First Lady Melania Trump were greeted with military honors at the Great Hall of the People. title: A Simple Sugar in Ultra-Processed Foods May Drive Obesity, Metabolic Disease
description: New research shows fructose, especially from ultra-processed foods, may raise obesity and metabolic disease risks. Learn how sugar affects your health.
content: Fructose, a simple sugar found in honey, fruits, and sweeteners like high-fructose corn syrup, is under scrutiny for its role in driving obesity and metabolic diseases. A review published in Nature Metabolism suggests the sugar’s impact on health extends beyond its caloric value, triggering metabolic syndrome and related conditions. The study’s authors argue that fructose’s unique metabolic pathway sets it apart from other sugars. Unlike glucose, which cells readily use for energy, fructose is primarily processed in the liver, where it can trigger fat production and inflammation. This process

title: Ebola outbreak kills 87 in Congo, no vaccine available for new strain
description: Health officials report 336 suspected Ebola cases and 87 deaths in Congo’s Ituri province. A case also appeared in Uganda as officials race to contain the outbr
content: Health officials in the Democratic Republic of Congo DRC are scrambling to contain an Ebola outbreak in Ituri province that has already killed 87 people and sickened 336 others. The strain driving the outbreak, detected in late April, has no known vaccine, according to the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention. The World Health Organization WHO confirmed the outbreak Friday, marking the latest in a series of deadly flare-ups in the region. Cases spread beyond Congo’s borders The outbreak has already reached Uganda after a 59-year-old Congolese man died from the virus in Kampala, th

title: A French Open contender? Svitolina stuns Gauff to seal Rome hat-trick
description: Elina Svitolina wins her 20th career WTA title and third Rome crown, ending Coco Gauff’s French Open title defense. Stream live on Sky Sports Tennis and NOW. Fo
content: Elina Svitolina dominated Coco Gauff 6-2, 6-3 Saturday to secure her 20th career WTA singles title and third Rome crown, capping a week where she also defeated world No. 2 Elena Rybakina of Kazakhstan and world No. 3 Iga Świątek of Poland. The 31-year-old Ukrainian, returning to the tour as a mother after giving birth to daughter Skai in October 2022, now turns her focus to the French Open starting May 24 in Paris, where she aims to win her first Grand Slam singles title. Svitolina, who had already beaten Gauff at the Australian Open and Dubai Tennis Championships earlier this year, wasted no

title: A call to stop the global housing: World Urban Forum, why it matters?
description: Over 28,000 delegates meet in Baku for the UN World Urban Forum as UN-Habitat warns 3 billion people lack proper housing. Slum populations could triple by 2050.
content: The United Nations Human Settlements Programme (UN-Habitat) has issued a stark warning ahead of the World Urban Forum in Baku, Azerbaijan: 3 billion people worldwide lack adequate housing, and slum populations could triple by 2050 if current trends persist. The forum, which runs from November 8 to 13, brings together over 28,000 delegates including government officials, urban planners, and community leaders to address the escalating global housing crisis. The World Urban Forum, established in 2001, serves as the premier global conference on sustainable urbanization. This year’s gathering in Ba

title: Study finds 8,500 daily steps may aid long-term weight loss
description: Research suggests walking 8,500 steps daily helps maintain weight loss after diet and exercise programs. Experts call it a simple, effective strategy.
content: A new research analysis published in Healthline reveals that walking 8,500 steps per day can help adults maintain weight loss after completing a structured lifestyle program. The findings, based on a review of multiple studies, suggest that this step count acts as a stable target for long-term weight management without requiring extreme dietary or exercise measures. The analysis found that participants who walked 8,500 steps daily during an eight-month weight loss phase lost an average of 4.4% of their body weight—nearly 9 pounds—when combined with dietary intervention. title: Cerebras burned $8M monthly before $60B AI chip IPO success
description: Cerebras Systems nearly collapsed before its $60B AI chip IPO, losing $8M monthly while building a chip many called impossible. Here’s what saved it.
content: Cerebras Systems, now the darling of the $60 billion AI chip market, teetered on collapse in its early years, hemorrhaging $8 million monthly while chasing a chip many engineers deemed impossible to fabricate. Founded in 2016 by Andrew Feldman), the company bet everything on building the world’s largest semiconductor, a wafer-scale chip measuring 46,225 square millimeters—nearly the size of a dinner plate. title: Lucy Liu at Cannes: Why Her 2024 Visit Feels Different Than 2008
description: Actress Lucy Liu reflects on her Cannes Film Festival return after 16 years, comparing her 2008 'Kung Fu Panda' debut to her 2024 experience. Learn what changed
content: Actress and director Lucy Liu Lucy Liu made a long-awaited return to the Cannes Film Festival this week, marking her first appearance since her 2008 debut with DreamWorks’ animated hit Kung Fu Panda. Speaking exclusively to The Hollywood Reporter, Liu called the 2024 trip “very different” from her earlier experience, reflecting both her personal growth and shifts in the festival’s landscape over the past 16 years. title: YouTube expands AI deepfake tool to all adult users globally
description: YouTube now offers AI-powered deepfake detection to all users over 18. Scan your face to find fake videos and request removals instantly.
content: YouTube is expanding its AI-powered deepfake detection program to every user over the age of 18, the platform announced Thursday. The feature, which initially launched in beta for select groups, now allows just about anyone to scan YouTube for potential deepfakes using their own face as a reference. Users submit a selfie-style photo, and YouTube’s system scans the platform for videos that closely resemble the submitted likeness. If a match is found, the user is notified and given the option to request its removal. The company says removal requests remain rare but did not provide updated figure

title: X pledges quicker action on hate and terror content in the UK
description: Social media platform X commits to quicker removal of hate and terror content in the UK after Ofcom highlighted rising threats to Jewish communities. Period.
content: Social media giant X has promised quicker action on removing hate and terror-related content in the UK, responding to pressure from Ofcom, the UK’s communications regulator. The commitments follow recent crimes targeting Jewish communities, which intensified scrutiny over how platforms handle extremist material. X’s pledge comes as regulators and lawmakers demand faster responses to illegal content online. title: Women's rights and demographics take centre stage at regional forum in Bukhara
description: Regional forum in Bukhara focuses on how women’s rights, education and demographic planning can drive Asia’s long-term development. Experts and officials attend
content: Regional leaders and UN officials gathered in Bukhara, Uzbekistan, for a high-level forum focused on women’s rights, education and demographic planning as tools for sustainable development across Asia. The two-day event, held May 15–16, 2024, brought together policymakers, economists and civil society representatives to address challenges and opportunities in population dynamics and gender equality. title: Lottie Woad surges into LPGA lead mix as Charley Hull collapses late
description: Lottie Woad moves into contention at the Kroger Queen City Championship after a six-under 66. Charley Hull drops eight strokes with six bogeys in eight holes. W
content: Lottie Woad shot a six-under 66 on Friday morning to keep her LPGA Tour hopes alive at the Kroger Queen City Championship. Her round included seven birdies and just one bogey, lifting her to six under for the tournament. Woad, who opened with a 70, moved into a tie for second place overnight at Maketewah Country Club, just two strokes behind Jin Young Ko, the former world No. 1 and defending champion. title: Ireland skips 2024 Eurovision over Israel-Gaza dispute
description: Ireland will not compete in the 2024 Eurovision Song Contest due to tensions with Israel over the Gaza war. Here’s why.
content: Ireland will not take part in the 2024 Eurovision Song Contest, the country’s national broadcaster RTÉ confirmed on Tuesday. The decision comes amid escalating diplomatic tensions between Ireland and Israel over the ongoing war in Gaza. While Eurovision organizers have not imposed any restrictions on participation, Ireland’s refusal reflects its opposition to Israel’s military actions in the Palestinian territory. title: Philippines senator Ronald Dela Rosa evades arrest after ICC summons
description: Philippine Senator Ronald Dela Rosa, wanted by the ICC for crimes against humanity, vanished from the Senate after a chaotic week of evasion. Here's what happen
content: The Philippines’ Senate building became the stage for a dramatic escape this week as Senator Ronald dela Rosa, the architect of the country’s controversial drug war, evaded law enforcement after a court ordered his arrest. Dela Rosa, facing potential charges from the International Criminal Court (ICC) for crimes against humanity, outmaneuvered government forces, rallied supporters, and slipped away unnoticed in the dead of night from one of the most secure facilities in Manila. title: Cerebras debuts on Wall Street with AI chip demand soaring 2024
description: AI chipmaker Cerebras made a stunning Wall Street debut Thursday, signaling strong demand for alternatives to Nvidia's chips. Here's how they compare.
content: Artificial intelligence chip startup Cerebras made a splash on Wall Street Thursday with its initial public offering, delivering a strong vote of confidence in alternatives to Nvidia’s dominance in the AI processor market. The company raised $150 million in its debut, pricing shares at $10 each and achieving a $4.1 billion valuation. Cerebras’ market entry comes as demand for AI chips accelerates, fueled by the rapid growth of generative AI applications and data center expansions worldwide. title: 2 Reading school pupils confirmed with meningitis B illness
description: Two children at Reading schools diagnosed with meningitis B. Health officials urge vigilance as cases confirmed. Public health advice follows.
content: Two children attending schools in Reading have tested positive for meningitis B, public health officials confirmed Friday. The cases involve pupils at two separate institutions, prompting health authorities to issue urgent alerts to parents and guardians. Public Health England South East said it is monitoring the situation closely and has advised immediate medical consultation for anyone with symptoms who may have been in close contact with the affected pupils. title: What was actually achieved at Trump and Xi’s ‘stalemate summit’ in Beijing?
description: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping conclude Beijing summit with vague claims of solving problems but no clear agreements. No progress on key trade or security issues r
content: Donald Trump’s highly anticipated meeting with Xi Jinping Xi Jinping in Beijing concluded Friday with little to show for the two days of talks beyond vague assurances. Trump declared on Twitter and in public remarks that the two leaders had ‘settled a lot of different problems’—a claim that offered no specifics on trade, security, or other disputes between the world’s two largest economies. Neither side released a joint statement, a customary outcome of high-level diplomatic summits, leaving analysts and observers to question what, if anything, was accomplished. title: What is the 'Thucydides Trap' that Xi invoked in his meeting with Trump?
description: Chinese President Xi Jinping invoked the Thucydides Trap during his meeting with Donald Trump in Beijing, warning mishandling Taiwan could spark war. Learn how
content: Chinese President Xi Jinping opened a high-stakes summit with former U.S. President Donald Trump in Beijing by invoking an ancient theory on how nations slide into war. Xi referenced the “Thucydides Trap,” a concept from the Peloponnesian War that describes how a rising power and an established power often stumble into conflict despite neither wanting it. His warning underscored deepening fears in Beijing that U.S. policy toward Taiwan could escalate tensions toward military confrontation. The term originates from the historian Thucydides, who analyzed how Sparta’s fear of Athens’ growing stre

title: Canada court asked to rule on assisted death for mentally ill woman
description: Claire Brosseau wants Canada’s courts to allow medically assisted death for mental illness after delays in access. Here’s how her case could change the rules.
content: Claire Brosseau, a Canadian woman with severe mental illness, has taken her fight for medically assisted death to court after the government twice postponed rules allowing the practice for psychiatric patients. Brosseau argues she cannot endure further delays and wants judges to decide if her request qualifies under Canada’s Medical Assistance in Dying laws. Her case highlights the growing debate over whether mental illness should be grounds for assisted dying, a question Canada has repeatedly kicked down the road. title: What in the World?
description: U.S. President Donald Trump embarks on Asia tour as India and Kenya host major summits while several prime ministers face political challenges. Follow key world
content: U.S. President Donald Trump completed a three-nation tour of Asia this week, visiting China, India and Kenya as both countries hosted major summits. The visits underscored shifting global alliances and economic priorities, with trade agreements and security cooperation at the forefront of discussions. In New Delhi, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi hosted the Raisina Dialogue, an annual geopolitical conference attended by foreign ministers, diplomats and business leaders from around the world. The by-election, expected within the next six months, will determine who replaces him in Parliament. description: Residents of Makerfield constituency speak out after Andy Burnham calls a by-election. Discover key issues and concerns shaping local opinions.
content: Andy Burnham’s resignation as MP for Makerfield has set the stage for a by-election, drawing attention to local concerns ahead of the vote. BBC Radio Manchester canvassed residents to understand their priorities, with many highlighting healthcare access and economic pressures as top issues. The by-election, triggered by Burnham’s move to Greater Manchester’s mayoral role, will test Labour’s dominance in a traditionally safe seat. title: Trump's China policy shift divides MAGA critics ahead of Beijing trip
description: Donald Trump’s softer stance on China ahead of Beijing visit sparks debate among MAGA supporters. Experts say shift will shape Republican base views.
content: Donald Trump’s upcoming trip to Beijing is raising questions about his evolving stance on China, a shift that critics in the MAGA movement are struggling to reconcile. The former president, who previously framed China as an economic and military threat, has recently softened his rhetoric, signaling a possible move toward engagement rather than confrontation. Analysts say this adjustment could influence how his loyal base views U.S.-China relations moving forward. title: What are World Cup 2026 national team base camps and their locations?
description: World Cup 2026 teams finalize base camps in US, Mexico, Canada for training and accommodation during the expanded 48-team tournament. See full list of confirmed
content: The World Cup 2026 will open June 11 in Mexico, with 48 national teams set to converge on the United States, Mexico, and Canada for training, acclimatization, and the group stage. title: What are the symptoms of meningitis and is there a vaccine?

Public Health England confirmed the cases and urged students and staff to remain vigilant for symptoms. The deceased student was not identified, and the two other individuals affected are receiving treatment at Royal Berkshire Hospital. title: Key arguments revealed in Musk vs OpenAI trial closing statements
description: The jury now decides in Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI. Arguments in the high-stakes trial reveal what each side claimed about the AI lab’s founding mission
content: The jury in the closely watched trial between tech billionaire Elon Musk and AI lab OpenAI has begun deliberations after two weeks of testimony and closing arguments. At the heart of the case is whether OpenAI violated its founding mission by abandoning its nonprofit structure to become a for-profit entity, now valued at over $80 billion. Musk filed the lawsuit in 2023, alleging that OpenAI’s shift toward commercialization betrayed its original commitment to open, safe AI development for humanity’s benefit. description: President Trump wrapped up a two-day trip to Beijing after meeting Xi Jinping. Here’s what the high-stakes summit showed about US-China relations.

title: Legal history shows why war crimes often go unpunished worldwide
description: A sweeping legal history reveals why the international community repeatedly fails to enforce war crime prosecutions despite Nuremberg promises. Period.
content: The Nuremberg trials marked a historic moment in 1945 when the international community declared war crimes punishable under international law. Yet eight decades later, justice remains elusive for most perpetrators. A sweeping legal review published in Foreign Policy reveals systemic failures that have allowed impunity to thrive despite the Nuremberg precedent. Legal promises vs. political reality The Nuremberg Charter created a framework to prosecute crimes against peace, war crimes, and crimes against humanity. But enforcement has consistently fallen short. The International Criminal Court (I

title: Study: Museum visits linked to slower biological aging
description: New research suggests frequent museum visits may reduce biological age by up to 5 years compared to peers. Findings highlight arts engagement as a health behavi
content: A new study published in The BMJ suggests that visiting museums and art galleries could slow biological aging, adding arts engagement to the list of health-promoting behaviors. Researchers analyzed data from over 3,000 adults in the UK over a decade, finding that those who frequently visited cultural venues had biological ages up to five years younger than their chronological age. The study, conducted by researchers at University College London and the University of Essex, measured biological age using markers such as blood pressure, grip strength, and cognitive function. title: Vast, Untapped Source of Lithium Found in The US Could Last 300 Years
description: Geologists find a vast untapped lithium deposit in the US that could supply domestic needs for 300 years. Ends US dependence on foreign sources.
content: Geologists have identified a vast lithium deposit beneath California’s Salton Sea, a saline lake in the state’s southeastern desert. The discovery, published in Science Advances, suggests the brine could contain enough lithium to meet U.S. demand for three centuries, transforming the nation’s energy security landscape. The deposit, estimated at 18 million tons, sits in a geothermal reservoir that has long been tapped for renewable energy. Researchers from Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory and Caltech collaborated on the study, which used advanced modeling to assess the resource. If fully e

title: USS Gerald R. Ford to return from 11-month deployment on Saturday
description: USS Gerald R. Ford, the world’s largest aircraft carrier, returns to Virginia after a record 320-day deployment supporting Central Command operations. The ship
content: The USS Gerald R. Ford will arrive at Naval Station Norfolk, Virginia, on Saturday after more than 320 days at sea, marking the longest deployment for a U.S. aircraft carrier since the Vietnam War. Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle announced the return during a House Armed Services Committee hearing Thursday, calling the deployment a historic achievement for the carrier and its crew. title: US stops Poland troop deployment after Germany pullout order

content: WASHINGTON — The Pentagon has halted the planned deployment of 4,000 U.S. troops to Poland, citing orders to reduce forces in Europe following President Donald Trump’s directive to withdraw 5,000 soldiers from Germany. The abrupt policy shift has drawn criticism from both Democratic and Republican lawmakers, who warn it sends a damaging message to allies amid rising global tensions. title: US seeks to indict Raúl Castro amid Cuba's deepening energy crisis

content: The U.S. Department of Justice is reportedly reviewing evidence to indict Cuba’s former president Raúl Castro Raúl Castro as the communist-run island faces a deepening energy crisis. title: US Marines test island-hopping tactics in Philippines 2026 drill
description: US Marine Corps 3rd Marine Littoral Regiment practices dispersed operations across 17 Philippine locations during Balikatan 2026. Period.
content: The U.S. Marine Corps’ 3rd Marine Littoral Regiment MLR conducted a sprawling exercise in the Philippines this spring, testing its ability to operate across 17 separate island locations. Held from April 20 to May 8 during Exercise Balikatan 2026, the drills showcased the regiment’s dispersed warfare capabilities, a core mission for the Hawaii-based unit created in March 2022. The 3rd MLR, with over 2,000 personnel, was designed to function at the forefront of Marine Corps operations, focusing on speed, agility, and expeditionary reach. title: US hotels in World Cup host cities see no tourism boom, survey finds
description: Hotels in US cities hosting World Cup matches say the tournament isn’t boosting tourism like expected. Learn why expectations missed reality.
content: Hotels in the nine U.S. cities that hosted FIFA World Cup matches say the tournament delivered far less tourism than expected, according to a survey by the American Hotel & Lodging Association (AHLA). The industry group found that most hotel owners viewed the event as a 'non-event' for business, with occupancy rates and revenue failing to meet projections despite the influx of international visitors. title: Trump fails to secure China's Iran war help, Xi warns on Taiwan

content: Donald Trump’s high-profile visit to Beijing this week ended with glowing praise from the White House for what it called a productive summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping Xi Jinping. But behind the ceremonial statements and diplomatic niceties, little progress emerged on the two most pressing global conflicts: the war in Iran and tensions over Taiwan. description: The U.S. Army has established the Multi-Domain Command-Pacific by merging the 7th Infantry Division and 1st Multi-Domain Task Force. The new two-star command wi
content: The U.S. Army activated the Multi-Domain Command-Pacific (MDC-PAC) on Tuesday, merging the 7th Infantry Division and the 1st Multi-Domain Task Force (1st MDTF) to better prepare for future conflicts. The new two-star command, headquartered at Joint Base Lewis-McChord in Washington, was announced during the 2026 Land Forces of the Pacific Symposium and Exposition in Hawaii. Army leaders stated the consolidation reflects a shift from traditional formation practices. Lt. Gen. title: US Army orders 3,000+ Barracuda cruise missiles from Anduril by 2027
description: The U.S. Army will receive over 3,000 Barracuda-500M cruise missiles by 2027 under a new $1B deal with Anduril. These long-range weapons can strike targets over
content: The U.S. Army will begin receiving at least 3,000 surface-launched Barracuda-500M cruise missiles from Anduril in 2027 under a $1 billion framework agreement. The deal requires Anduril to deliver a minimum of 1,000 missiles each year for three years, according to a company release. The Barracuda-500Ms are designed for long-range precision strikes against land and maritime targets, with a range exceeding 500 nautical miles and a 100-pound warhead. The agreement reflects a Pentagon push to rapidly expand affordable munitions production at scale. Existing long-range strike options are often criti

title: US president leaves Beijing summit empty-handed after high hopes
description: US president fails to secure major concessions from China on Iran or Taiwan after Beijing summit. Markets react to underwhelming outcome. Investors face disappo
content: U.S. President Donald Trump departed Beijing on Monday after a two-day summit that yielded no major concessions from Chinese counterparts, disappointing investors and raising questions about the administration’s negotiating strategy. The trip, which had been billed as a potential breakthrough on trade and security issues, ended with no visible progress on flashpoints including Iran’s nuclear program and Taiwan’s status, both of which remain unresolved. title: 36 countries join tribunal to prosecute Russia over Ukraine war crimes
description: Thirty-six nations and the EU commit to a future tribunal for Russia over alleged war crimes in Ukraine. Legal framework and enforcement details remain unclear.
content: More than 30 countries including the European Union have signed up to establish a special tribunal targeting Russia for alleged war crimes in Ukraine, officials announced Friday following negotiations in Chisinau, Moldova. The agreement, signed by 34 European nations plus Australia and Costa Rica, marks a formal step toward legal action against Russian officials and military leaders implicated in crimes committed since the 2022 full-scale invasion. title: UK’s Keir Starmer struggles as Reform UK surges in polls
description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces plummeting approval ratings and party defections after local election losses to anti-immigration Reform UK. Nigel Farage’s
content: Less than two years after Labour’s 2024 landslide victory that ended 14 years of Conservative rule, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is under severe pressure. Results from England’s local elections last week showed Labour losing more than half of its council seats to the right-wing, anti-immigration Reform UK, a party led by former Brexit campaigner Nigel Farage. The defeats extended to devolved elections in Scotland and Wales, where Labour also underperformed despite not facing direct ballots for Westminster seats. title: UK in 'political turmoil: Labour MPs openly question whether Labour will survive as political force

content: At a moment of profound instability in British politics, with the Labour Party engulfed by internal revolt, François Picard welcomes Owen Jones, British author, Guardian columnist and political commentator. He offers an unsparing diagnosis of a political movement at a crossroads. “The country is in political turmoil,” Jones declares at the outset. What follows is not merely commentary on a leadership race, but a wider analysis of institutional decay and electoral fragmentation. title: UK backs human rights plan to accelerate illegal migration removals
description: UK government backs plan to reform Europe’s human rights treaty to fast-track deportations of illegal migrants. Ends years of debate over migration enforcement.
content: The UK government has formally supported a plan to revise Europe’s key human rights treaty, the European Convention on Human Rights (ECHR), to speed up the removal of illegal migrants. The move comes as the country faces record numbers of small boat crossings and a growing backlog in asylum claims. Home Secretary James Cleverly confirmed the agreement in a statement to Parliament, framing it as a necessary step to manage migration pressures without undermining human rights protections. title: Uganda at a crossroads as Museveni begins new term
description: Uganda's President Museveni starts his seventh term facing high unemployment and poverty while questions grow over his age and political future. Regional leader
content: Uganda’s President Yoweri Museveni was sworn in for a seventh term on Tuesday, marking four decades in power amid economic struggles and growing uncertainty over his succession. The 81-year-old leader, who first took office in 1986 after seizing power in a civil war, faces mounting pressure to address unemployment and poverty that persist despite his long rule. content: > 📰 Continuing coverage: UAE builds second West-East oil pipeline to bypass Strait of Hormuz The United Arab Emirates UAE has accelerated construction of a strategic oil pipeline designed to bypass the Strait of Hormuz, completing the project by 2027. The pipeline will double the UAE’s crude export capacity, reducing reliance on the vulnerable waterway through which 20% of global seaborne oil and gas passes. title: UAE builds second West-East oil pipeline to bypass Strait of Hormuz
description: The United Arab Emirates is accelerating a second West-East oil pipeline project to bypass the Strait of Hormuz amid limited global energy supplies and shipping
content: The United Arab Emirates UAE is speeding up construction of a second West-East oil pipeline to bypass the Strait of Hormuz, a critical but volatile shipping route. The project comes as global oil supplies tighten and flows through the Hormuz narrow strait remain severely limited due to geopolitical tensions and infrastructure constraints. Why the UAE is prioritizing the new pipeline The UAE’s first West-East pipeline, completed in 2009, already carries oil from fields in Abu Dhabi to the Gulf of Oman, avoiding the Hormuz Strait. title: Trump’s Lebanon Talks Worsen Crisis, Say Analysts
description: Trump’s mediation in Lebanon sparks backlash as analysts warn his approach deepens political and economic turmoil. Experts weigh in on the escalating crisis.
content: Donald Trump’s recent negotiations over Lebanon have drawn sharp criticism from analysts and regional observers, who argue his administration is worsening the country’s deepening political and economic crisis. The talks, framed as efforts to mediate sectarian tensions and economic instability, have instead been accused of publicly undermining Lebanon’s fragile government, deepening divisions and stoking public anger. title: Trump’s China diplomacy drops human rights focus as US shifts stance
description: Donald Trump’s softer stance on China’s human rights record marks a sharp shift from past U.S. policy. Here’s what it means for global diplomacy.
content: Donald Trump’s recent comments on China’s human rights record during his Beijing visit signaled a stark departure from decades of U.S. policy. When asked if he would raise the case of Jimmy Lai, the jailed pro-democracy activist in Hong Kong, Trump responded with a dismissive comparison to James Comey, a former FBI director often criticized by the president. "It’s like saying to me, ‘If Comey ever went to jail, would you let him out?’ It might be a hard one for me," Trump said. content: The first face-to-face meeting between former US President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping in nearly two years produced no concrete agreements but succeeded in reopening stalled diplomatic channels. Held in San Francisco on November 15, 2024, the summit was framed as a reset following years of strained relations under the Biden administration that included tariffs, export controls, and military posturing in the South China Sea. Trump told reporters that both sides should ‘cool it,’ signaling a desire to avoid escalation in the Taiwan Strait amid long-standing tensions. content: US President Donald Trump on Friday set a fresh condition for reviving nuclear talks with Iran, demanding Tehran suspend uranium enrichment and remove nuclear fuel for two decades. Speaking to reporters outside the White House, Trump framed the 20-year suspension as the minimum acceptable commitment from Iran to prove its seriousness about rejoining a revived nuclear accord. content: John Hammond, a father from Bristol, is among 30 parents who told BBC Your Voice they were overcharged by the UK’s Child Maintenance Service (CMS) by as much as £20,000 each. title: The impossible job? The pressure of refereeing Scotland's title decider
description: Pressure mounts on referees in Scotland as Celtic and Rangers battle for the title. Officials face intense scrutiny in every key match.
content: Scotland’s top referees are under unprecedented pressure this season as Celtic and Rangers battle for the league title, with every contentious decision scrutinized for signs of favoritism. The pressure has intensified ahead of the potential title decider, where a single mistake could decide the championship and spark outrage from fans of both clubs. The referee’s impossible task Officials in the Scottish Premiership are caught in a no-win situation, with accusations of bias flying after almost every match involving the Old Firm. Referees are routinely accused of favoring either Celtic or Range

title: Trump's pressure on Fed independence may reshape US economic policy
description: Donald Trump’s push to control the Federal Reserve challenges long-standing US economic independence. Learn how this could shift monetary policy decisions.
content: The Federal Reserve’s independence from political interference has long been a cornerstone of U.S. economic stability. During Donald Trump’s presidency, that autonomy faced its stiffest challenge in decades as the then-president openly pressured the Fed to slash interest rates, arguing that lower borrowing costs would boost his reelection chances. Jerome Powell, who stepped down this week as Fed chair, resisted those demands, drawing Trump’s ire and setting the stage for a broader debate about the Fed’s role in American governance. Powell’s tenure, which began in 2018, was marked by a mix of s

title: Tensions flare near Strait of Hormuz as a ship is seized and another is sunk
description: Tensions are escalating again near the Strait of Hormuz after a ship anchored off the United Arab Emirates was seized and taken toward Iran and another was atta
content: Tensions are escalating again near the Strait of Hormuz after a ship anchored off the United Arab Emirates was seized and taken toward Iran and another was attacked and sank near the coast of Oman. (Image credit: Digital Embed) Map showing where a vessel was seized off the UAE coast. Digital Embed/AP hide caption DUBAI, United Arab Emirates — A ship anchored off the United Arab Emirates was seized and taken toward Iran and another — a cargo ship near Oman — sank after being attacked, authorities said Thursday, as tensions escalated near the Strait of Hormuz. title: Switzerland to open secret files on Auschwitz 'Angel of Death' Mengele
description: Switzerland will open secret archives on Josef Mengele, the Auschwitz doctor known as the 'Angel of Death.' New details may emerge about his post-war life in Eu
content: Switzerland announced Friday it will declassify decades-old intelligence files on Josef Mengele Josef Mengele, the Nazi doctor known as the 'Angel of Death' for his horrific experiments at Auschwitz. The files, held by Swiss authorities in Bern, will be opened to the public in 2025 after years of public and legal pressure for transparency. For decades, conspiracy theories and witness accounts suggested Mengele spent time in neutral Switzerland after disappearing from Europe in 1949. Swiss officials have never confirmed his presence, but the decision to release the files indicates there may hav

title: 5 nations tipped to win Eurovision 2026: big names and dance anthems
description: Denmark, Lithuania, Italy, Belgium and Cyprus among top contenders for Eurovision 2026 victory in Sweden. High-energy tracks and romantic optimism dominate. Fiv
content: Sweden will stage Eurovision 2026 after the United Kingdom declined an invitation to host the event, organizers confirmed Friday. The annual contest is scheduled for May 23, with national broadcasters submitting entries in the coming months. Early speculation points to Denmark, Lithuania, Italy, Belgium and Cyprus as potential front-runners based on recent performances and song styles. Eurovision Song Contest official site said Sweden’s SVT had agreed to host after the UK’s BBC withdrew. The BBC cited financial and scheduling constraints as key reasons. The 71st edition of the competition will

title: Supreme court rejects Virginia Democrats’ bid to restore congressional map
description: Supreme Court rejects Virginia Democrats' effort to revive a congressional map that would have handed Democrats four House seats. The 2024 redistricting battle
content: The Supreme Court on Friday issued an unsigned order rejecting a Virginia Democratic Party effort to reinstate a congressional map that would have reshaped the state’s House delegation in their favor. The order, released without any noted dissent, ends a months-long legal fight over the state’s electoral boundaries and delivers a setback to Democrats just months before the November election. The rejected map, drawn by Virginia’s Democratic-led legislature in 2023, would have redrawn four competitive districts to favor Democratic candidates. Analysts projected the new lines could have flipped a

title: Supreme Court is death knell for Virginia's Democratic-friendly congressional maps
description: US Supreme Court refuses to block Virginia Supreme Court ruling striking down Democratic-friendly congressional map approved by voters in April referendum. Ends
content: WASHINGTON — The U.S. Supreme Court on Friday declined to intervene in a dispute over Virginia’s congressional district maps, leaving in place a state court ruling that blocked a Democratic-friendly map approved by voters in April. The decision ends a last-minute legal effort by Virginia Democrats and Attorney General Mark Herring [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MarkHerring] to implement the new boundaries before the November elections. The Virginia Supreme Court ruled 4-3 on May 8 that lawmakers violated the state constitution by failing to follow proper procedures to place the referendum on t

title: Strange Metal From Beyond Our World Spotted in an Ancient Treasure Stash
description: Researchers detect rare 'strange metal' behavior in 2,000-year-old artifacts, challenging current understanding of materials science. High search volume term in
content: An international research team has uncovered evidence of a rare quantum phenomenon in a 2,000-year-old metal object unearthed from an ancient Roman treasure hoard. The discovery, published in Nature, challenges existing theories about high-temperature superconductivity and strange metals, which exhibit unusual electrical resistance patterns at low temperatures. The artifact, part of a cache found in modern-day Italy, contains traces of a metal alloy that behaves like a 'strange metal' despite being centuries old. The study focused on a small bronze coin from the hoard, which revealed anomalous

title: Ben Stokes ends Zak Crawley's England Test career after poor run
description: England axe Zak Crawley after another poor start to the season. Ben Stokes removes him from the firing line. Watch England vs New Zealand Tests from June 4 on S
content: England have dropped Zak Crawley after another disappointing start to the season, with Test captain Ben Stokes delivering the final blow both with bat and ball. The 28-year-old opener was left out of England’s three-Test series against New Zealand, which begins on June 4 and will be shown live on Sky Sports. His axing follows a string of unproductive performances that have raised serious questions about his future in the red-ball side. Crawley’s struggles have mounted since the Ashes, where he averaged just 20.33 across five Tests, and his form in the County Championship has done little to sil

title: Startups shrink data centers to home size to ease grid strain
description: Home-sized data centers promise lower costs and greener computing than massive warehouse facilities. California startup tests silent, fanless units in residents
content: California-based Span) has begun installing prototype data center “nodes” on homes and small businesses in Northern California to ease strain on the electrical grid. The cabinet-sized units, called XFRA, are designed to operate without fans, eliminating noise pollution that has drawn opposition to nearby warehouse data centers. The company, in partnership with Nvidia, estimates each unit will eventually generate one to two megawatts of compute power, offering a decentralized alternative to traditional data centers. Home data centers target grid strain and cost concerns As data demand surges, t

title: UK politics week leaves public exhausted amid leadership shifts
description: Political drama in the UK this week has left voters exhausted as leadership changes and controversial figures dominate headlines. Public reaction is divided.
content: > 📰 Continuing coverage: Starmer ally admits prime minister is ‘unpopular’ but warns of ‘chaos’ if leadership contest triggered – UK politics live The past seven days in British politics have felt like a marathon rather than a sprint, leaving voters weary and divided. The warning from Keir Starmer that the nation faces a “battle for the soul of our nation” did little to reassure an already fatigued electorate. content: > 📰 Continuing coverage: UK Politics: Starmer Faces Leadership Challenge Pressure is intensifying on Keir Starmer after Andy Burnham was given a route back to parliament What would potential Labour leadership candidates do differently to Starmer? Wes Streeting’s resignation as health secretary , and the resignation of former minister Josh Simons as an MP to clear a path for Andy Burnham to return to parliament, has brought the prospect of a Labour leadership race one step closer, even if he has not triggered a contest himself. title: Starbucks to lay off 300 U.S. employees, shutter some regional support offices
description: Starbucks will lay off 300 U.S. employees and close some regional support offices to accelerate profitable growth. The move affects corporate roles in Seattle a
content: Starbucks NYSE:SBUX will eliminate 300 U.S. corporate jobs and shutter some regional support offices as part of a broader effort to restore profitability. The coffee giant notified affected employees this week, confirming the cuts in a statement released Friday. Regional hubs in cities such as Atlanta, Dallas and Chicago are among those slated for closure, according to internal communications reviewed by Bloomberg. The restructuring targets non-store roles, including support functions like marketing, supply chain and human resources. Starbucks did not specify whether additional layoffs would f

title: South Korea’s 500,000 Drone Warriors Will Be a Hollow Force
description: Ukraine has reshaped the battlefield with cheap, expendable drones. South Korea reads the signals and wants to match the scale. North Korea has been reading the
content: Ukraine has reshaped the battlefield with cheap, expendable drones. South Korea reads the signals and wants to match the scale. North Korea has been reading the same signals through a more direct channel. Since late 2024, North Korea has rotated thousands of troops through Russia s war in Ukraine, alongside what is currently the world s most combat tested drone force tied with Ukraine s, of course. title: South Korea opens Galaxy Robot Park in Seoul with K-pop, AI shows and family fun
description: South Korea’s new Galaxy Robot Park in Seoul blends humanoid robot boxing, K-pop shows and AI attractions for families. A tech entertainment hub now open to the
content: SEOUL — South Korea has opened the Galaxy Robot Park in Seoul, a robot-themed entertainment venue designed to blend cutting-edge technology with family-friendly attractions. The park, located in the city’s Gangnam district, features humanoid robot boxing matches, K-pop dance performances and interactive AI exhibits tailored for children and adults alike. The Galaxy Robot Park is the latest project by Samsung, aimed at showcasing the company’s advancements in robotics and artificial intelligence. Visitors can watch live demonstrations of humanoid robots in simulated boxing matches, where robots

title: Israel-Lebanon truce extended 45 days after Washington talks
description: Israel and Lebanon agree to a 45-day ceasefire extension after US-brokered talks in Washington. New negotiations set for June 2-3. Israel launched strikes on Ty
content: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a 45-day extension of their ceasefire after two days of talks in Washington, the US State Department announced Monday. The extension follows renewed diplomatic efforts aimed at stabilizing the fragile truce between the two nations, which has faced repeated disruptions in recent months. State Department spokesperson Tommy Pigott called the negotiations "productive" and said further discussions would be held from June 2 to June 3. The announcement came as tensions flared on Monday with Israel launching airstrikes on the southern Lebanese city of Tyre. The strike

title: Shakira and Burna Boy drop official 2026 World Cup song 'Dai Dai
description: FIFA unveils 'Dai Dai' by Shakira and Burna Boy as the official song for the 2026 World Cup. Royalties will fund global education and soccer programs.
content: FIFA announced 'Dai Dai,' a collaboration between Colombian superstar Shakira and Nigerian artist Burna Boy, as the official song for the 2026 FIFA World Cup on Friday. The track, available immediately on streaming platforms, marks Shakira’s fourth World Cup anthem and her first since 'Waka Waka (This Time for Africa)' in 2010. FIFA described the song as capturing 'the energy, passion and global spirit' of the tournament, which will take place across the United States, Canada and Mexico from June 11 to July 19, 2026. Royalties from 'Dai Dai' will benefit the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund,

title: Seven killed in Gaza on Nakba Day as Israel says it targets Hamas member
description: Seven Palestinians, including three women and a child, killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza City during Nakba Day commemorations. Dozens injured as Israel claims a
content: Israeli airstrikes on Gaza City on Friday, coinciding with Nakba Day commemorations, killed at least seven Palestinians, including three women and a child, according to medical sources. The strikes targeted a residential building in the Rimal neighborhood and a civilian vehicle, injuring at least 45 others, several critically. Witnesses reported heavy damage to nearby structures, with rescue teams still extracting survivors from the rubble hours after the attacks. Israel’s Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Defense Minister Israel Katz issued a joint statement claiming the strikes targeted

title: arXiv bans AI slop with one-year ban and extra peer review
description: arXiv cracks down on AI-generated content with a one-year submission ban and mandatory peer review for repeat offenders. Fight against academic slop escalates.
content: The physics and astronomy preprint server arXiv announced Thursday that submissions containing inappropriate AI-generated content will face a one-year ban and a permanent requirement for future publications to undergo peer review before being hosted. The move underscores growing concerns about the proliferation of AI slop—low-quality, unchecked AI output—contaminating academic literature before editors or reviewers even see it. Thomas Dietterich, a prominent figure in the decision, outlined the policy on social media, emphasizing the server’s role in maintaining scientific integrity. The new r

title: First photo emerges of US military's secret AIM-260 air-to-air missile
description: First public images of the U.S. military's secret AIM-260 air-to-air missile appear after years of flight testing. Expected to replace the AIM-120 AMRAAM.
content: The U.S. Air Force has released the first public image of its AIM-260 Joint Advanced Tactical Missile (JATM), ending years of secrecy around the next-generation air-to-air weapon. The missile was photographed attached to an F/A-18F Super Hornet from Air Test and Evaluation Squadron 31 (VX-31), which departed Eglin Air Force Base in Florida on May 13 during a test sortie. The jet carried the AIM-260 on its fuselage station, outboard of the right engine intake, marking the first time the weapon has been seen outside classified testing environments. description: The US government will settle a lawsuit filed against Gautam Adani, one of the world's richest people, for hiding an alleged bribery scheme. It's just
content: The US government will settle a lawsuit filed against Gautam Adani, one of the world's richest people, for hiding an alleged bribery scheme. It's just one of the cases Adani faces in US courts. title: Watch scientists film suckerfish diving into manta rays' cloacas
description: Scientists film rare footage of suckerfish entering manta rays' cloacas to hitchhike ocean currents. New study reveals unusual behavior.
content: Marine biologists have documented an unusual behavior in the ocean: suckerfish diving into the cloacas of manta rays to hitchhike on ocean currents. The rare footage, captured during a recent study, shows the fish entering the rays' digestive openings for transport, a behavior previously only theorized but never observed in the wild. The discovery was made by researchers studying the interactions between suckerfish (Echeneis naucrates) and manta rays (Mobula tarapacana) in the Atlantic Ocean. The fish, known for their adhesive dorsal fins, typically attach to larger marine animals for mobility

title: Runway bets AI video could outpace Google in world models race
description: AI video startup Runway aims to challenge tech giants with its video generation models, betting on video as the path to advanced AI. Now expanding beyond filmma
content: AI video-generation startup Runway is betting its future on video—not text—as the fastest route to artificial general intelligence. The New York-based company, which began by selling editing and generation tools to filmmakers, now says its video-first approach could outpace rivals like Google in building AI systems that understand the real world. Runway co-founder and CEO Cristobal Valenzuela argues that starting outside the AI mainstream gave the company a unique advantage. “We were never part of the text-first paradigm,” Valenzuela said in an interview. “That forced us to think differently a

title: Romanian metal, Australian star Delta Goodrem qualify for Eurovision grand final
description: Romanian metal, a Danish club anthem and Australian singer Delta Goodrem advanced to the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 grand final after Thursday’s second semi-f
content: Romanian metal, a Danish club anthem and Australian singer Delta Goodrem advanced to the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 grand final after Thursday’s second semi-final in Vienna. Albania, Bulgaria, Cyprus, the Czech Republic, Malta, Norway and Ukraine also secured places in Saturday’s final at the Wiener Stadthalle. title: RJ Scaringe’s $12B startup haul draws investors despite risks
description: RJ Scaringe has raised over $12 billion for three startups, with Rivian alone securing $10.7 billion. Investor demand remains high despite market challenges. Pe
content: Rivian founder RJ Scaringe has raised more than $12 billion across three startups, with Rivian alone accounting for $10.7 billion in funding rounds since its 2015 launch. The electric vehicle maker’s ability to attract capital has defied broader market skepticism about high-growth startups in competitive sectors. Rivian went public in November 2021 in one of the largest U.S. IPOs of the year, valuing the company at $66.5 billion at the time. Despite a 75% stock decline from its peak, investor interest in Scaringe’s vision persists, with recent funding rounds including a $2.5 billion raise in 2

title: Restrain and Hedge: A New U.S. Nuclear Strategy for a Two-Peer World
description: What if fielding more nuclear weapons makes the United States less secure, not more? That question is now at the center of a growing debate as the United States
content: What if fielding more nuclear weapons makes the United States less secure, not more? That question is now at the center of a growing debate as the United States confronts a nuclear landscape shaped by two major nuclear rivals.China is rapidly expanding and modernizing its nuclear arsenal, while the New Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty (START), the last remaining nuclear arms control deal between the United States and Russia, has expired. title: Protests hit Havana as Cuba runs out of fuel amid shortages
description: Cuba faces severe fuel shortages after reserves run out, sparking protests in Havana over power, food and transport failures. Authorities blame US sanctions.
content: Protests broke out in Cuba’s capital Tuesday as residents took to the streets to demand solutions to worsening fuel shortages that have crippled power, transport, and food supplies. Cuban officials confirmed Tuesday that the country’s fuel reserves had been exhausted, leaving many without access to gasoline, diesel, or public transit. Demonstrators gathered in Havana’s central areas, chanting slogans and blocking streets, according to local reports and social media videos. Cuban authorities placed blame squarely on U.S. sanctions, particularly those imposed during the Trump administration, whi

title: 24 killed in Russian strike on Kyiv flats as prisoner swap proceeds
description: Russia hits residential buildings in Kyiv, killing 24 including a 12-year-old girl. Prisoner swap between Ukraine and Russia moves forward despite escalation.
content: A Russian missile strike on residential buildings in Kyiv, Ukraine’s capital, killed 24 people on Thursday, including a 12-year-old girl whose father had died during the war. The attack targeted a densely populated area, flattening multiple apartments and sparking fires that took hours to contain. Ukrainian officials reported the strike used a high-precision missile, likely a Kh-101 or similar model, launched from Russian airspace. Escalation amid ongoing conflict Ukraine’s military said the strike was the deadliest single attack on Kyiv in months, with rescue workers still searching for survi

title: Pope decries rise of AI-directed warfare, saying it leads to a spiral of annihilation

content: Pope Leo XIV denounced how investments in artificial intelligence and high-tech weaponry were leading the world into a 'spiral of annihilation,' as he called for peace in the Middle East and Ukraine. title: NASA maps reveal how Earth’s night lights shift over 10 years
description: NASA’s Black Marble maps show how Earth’s artificial night lights brighten and dim over nearly a decade. Discover the changing patterns of human activity worldw
content: NASA on Friday released new global maps that track how Earth’s artificial night lights brighten or dim over nearly a decade. The maps, derived from data collected between 2012 and 2021, use NASA’s Black Marble product to visualize changes in human activity across the planet. Bright areas in gold show where lights have intensified, while purple highlights regions where illumination has decreased. How NASA’s Black Marble maps work The Black Marble product is a high-resolution dataset developed by NASA’s Earth Observatory. It processes nighttime imagery captured by the Suomi NPP satellite, which

title: Ronda Rousey says critics were wrong, sets sights on Gina Carano fight
description: MMA star Ronda Rousey dismisses past doubts as she promotes her UFC return against Gina Carano. Read her latest fight plans and UFC opinions.
content: MMA legend Ronda Rousey Ronda Rousey has dismissed years of skepticism about her future in mixed martial arts, revealing plans to return to the UFC as both a fighter and promoter. Speaking to BBC Sport, the 37-year-old said her critics had no grounds to question her resolve. "People should have never doubted me," she told BBC Sport. "I always knew where I was going." Rousey, a former UFC women’s bantamweight champion, stepped away from MMA after a string of losses in 2015, including a first-round knockout loss to Holly Holm.
