title: Fatah elects leaders amid polls showing Palestinian discontent rising
description: Fatah faction holds rare meeting to elect top leaders as polls reveal growing dissatisfaction among ordinary Palestinians. Polls indicate waning public trust in
content: RAMALLAH, West Bank — The Fatah faction of the Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO) held a rare meeting Tuesday to elect its top decision-makers, a move seen as an attempt to address deepening public dissatisfaction revealed in recent polls. The gathering comes as surveys indicate declining trust in Fatah’s leadership among ordinary Palestinians, who criticize the party for perceived detachment from their daily struggles and political stagnation. Analysts say the meeting reflects internal pressure within Fatah to revitalize its image and regain public support. Party officials framed the eve

title: Palestinian activist Ramy Shaath faces deportation from France
description: French authorities label Ramy Shaath a 'serious threat to public order' as deportation from France begins. His lawyer confirms proceedings as rights groups rais
content: French authorities have initiated deportation proceedings against Palestinian activist Ramy Shaath, labeling him a 'serious threat to public order,' his lawyer confirmed on Friday. The move comes amid heightened scrutiny of pro-Palestinian advocacy in Europe following the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza. Shaath, a co-founder of the Paris-based organization Urgence Palestine, was detained without charge in Egypt for nearly three years from 2019 to 2022 before being released and permitted to join his wife in France. Shaath’s legal team has denounced the decision, calling it politically motivated and an

title: Pakistan’s Fatima Sana smashes women’s T20 record with 15-ball 50
description: Pakistan’s Fatima Sana sets women’s T20 world record with 50 off 15 balls in Karachi. Breaks 18-ball record held by Devine, Litchfield, Ghosh.
content: Pakistan’s Fatima Sana wikipedia.org/wiki/FatimaSana has rewritten the record books in women’s T20 international cricket, smashing the fastest half-century ever recorded. The Pakistan captain reached 50 off just 15 deliveries during a dominant innings against Zimbabwe at Karachi’s National Stadium, surpassing the previous mark of 18 balls shared by New Zealand’s Sophie Devine, Australia’s Phoebe Litchfield, and India’s Richa Ghosh. Sana’s explosive batting set the tone for Pakistan’s innings. She struck 10 fours and two sixes in a 19-ball unbeaten 62 that propelled her team to 223 for four in

title: Pakistan faces pressure over mediator role in Iran war
description: Pakistan’s mediation between the US and Iran faces growing pressure after recent US media scrutiny. Explore Islamabad’s high-stakes balancing act in a global co
content: Pakistan has taken a leading role in mediating peace talks between the US and Iran, hosting discussions in April 2026 that included Iran’s top diplomat Abbas Araghchi. The move places Islamabad at the center of a high-stakes geopolitical balancing act as it navigates relations with Washington, Tehran, Beijing, and key Gulf allies like Saudi Arabia and the UAE. The stakes for Pakistan are significant. Officials warn that a broader US-Iran conflict could disrupt Gulf trade routes, exacerbate energy shortages, and fuel sectarian tensions that threaten Pakistan’s border stability with Iran. The co

title: OpenAI adds personal finance tool to ChatGPT with bank account linking
description: OpenAI’s ChatGPT now offers personal finance features allowing users to connect bank accounts and track spending. AI-powered tool provides spending insights and
content: OpenAI has expanded ChatGPT with new personal finance capabilities, allowing users to link bank accounts for real-time tracking of spending, subscriptions and upcoming bills. The feature, available immediately, provides a unified dashboard showing portfolio performance, monthly cash flow and transaction histories across linked financial accounts. The update follows OpenAI’s broader push to integrate ChatGPT into everyday workflows beyond text generation. Users can now authorize secure connections to bank accounts through third-party financial data providers, enabling the AI to analyze spending

title: Iran, Oman discuss Hormuz Strait fees as US opposes Gulf plan
description: Iran says it’s coordinating with Oman on Hormuz Strait management, including fees for transiting ships. The U.S. opposes the plan as Muscat stays silent on deta
content: > 📰 Continuing coverage: Tensions flare near Strait of Hormuz as a ship is seized and another is sunk Oman has become the center of a growing geopolitical dispute after Iran announced it was working with the Gulf state on new management rules for the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran claims the plan includes charging fees to commercial ships and requiring details on the nationality of all vessels transiting the waterway. The U.S. has strongly opposed the proposal, which Muscat has yet to confirm or deny. The silence from Oman’s government has left diplomats and shipping companies guessing about the fut

title: Nvidia hits $5.7 trillion market cap, nears $1 trillion weekly gain
description: Nvidia's market cap surged to $5.7 trillion, up from $4.7 trillion in just days. Here’s what it means for investors and the stock market. Nvidia, market cap, tr
content: Nvidia’s market capitalization crossed the $5.7 trillion mark this week, a staggering increase from $4.7 trillion just days earlier, according to market close data. The chip giant’s rapid ascent has disrupted investor expectations and sent ripples through the stock market, where bullish bets now face heightened scrutiny. Analysts attribute the surge to unrelenting demand for artificial intelligence (AI) chips, which Nvidia dominates with its high-performance GPUs. The company’s valuation now rivals some of the world’s largest economies, underscoring its outsize influence in tech and finance. N

title: Notts County book place at Wembley after holding off Chesterfield
description: Notts County beat Chesterfield 1-0 on aggregate to book their place in the League Two play-off final at Wembley. County defender Jayden Luker scored the only go
content: Notts County will face either Crewe Alexandra or Walsall in the League Two play-off final at Wembley after surviving a late Chesterfield fightback to win 1-0 on aggregate. The Magpies, managed by Ian Burchnall, held firm in a nervy second leg at Meadow Lane to book their place in the showpiece decider. Jayden Luker’s first-half strike on Friday proved the difference as County held on despite Chesterfield’s late pressure. The defender, on loan from Luton Town, capitalized on a loose ball to fire past Ryan Boot in the 37th minute. Chesterfield pressed hard for an equalizer but found Notts County

title: Cost-saving unattended funerals leave families with lasting grief
description: A man says his mother’s unattended cremation saved money but left him with painful memories and no closure. Learn how cheaper funeral options impact grieving fa
content: Ed Cullen, 42, still struggles with the lack of a proper send-off for his mother, who died in April 2020 during the first COVID-19 lockdown. She opted for an unattended cremation arranged by a direct cremation provider, a service that skips visitation, hearse and ceremony. The cost was £295, a fraction of the £3,000 to £5,000 average for a traditional funeral in the UK. 'It felt like she was just gone,' Cullen said. 'No one was there to mark the moment. No one even knew she was being cremated that day.' How unattended funerals work Unattended or direct cremations require no mourners present. T

title: Northampton smash Bristol 94-33 in Premiership Rugby record win
description: Northampton set a Premiership Rugby record with a 94-33 win over Bristol Bears. The victory secured their semi-final spot in a record-breaking game at Franklin
content: Northampton Northampton Saints delivered a record-breaking performance on Saturday, crushing Bristol Bears) 94-33 in the Premiership Rugby semi-finals at Franklin's Gardens. The Saints scored 14 tries to set a new league scoring record in a dominant display that secured their spot in the next round. The 61-point winning margin stands as the largest in Premiership Rugby history, surpassing the previous record of 54 points set in 2005. content: The U.S. Army is expanding its focus from single drones to confronting swarms of unmanned aircraft in a shift reflecting modern battlefield realities. Project Flytrap, a multinational exercise held in Lithuania, brought together nearly 1,000 personnel from the U.S. and allied nations to test tactics against drone swarms, electronic warfare, and counter-unmanned aircraft systems (UAS) defenses. The exercise underscored the urgency of integrating drones into military doctrine as inexpensive, mass-produced drones reshape combat operations from infantry patrols to aviation strategies. title: NFL pushes to ban specific NFL bet types in prediction markets

content: The National Football League (NFL) has formally requested that regulators prohibit certain types of trading contracts in prediction markets, according to a letter reviewed by CNBC. The league specifically targets contracts tied to early-game events, such as the first play of a matchup, as well as those linked to player injuries. The proposal also seeks to raise the minimum age for participating in sports-related contract trading. content: Israel’s prime minister has instructed his government to pursue legal action against The New York Times following its publication of an article that accused Israeli security officials of raping Palestinian detainees. The decision, announced late Sunday, signals a potential defamation lawsuit targeting the newspaper and its reporters for alleged false and damaging claims. description: NASA’s 34th SpaceX resupply mission lifts off May 15, 2026, carrying 6,500 pounds of science gear to the International Space Station. Docking set for May 17. Wa
content: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket lifted off from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida at 6:05 p.m. EDT Friday, May 15, 2026, carrying the Dragon cargo spacecraft to the International Space Station. This marks the 34th commercial resupply mission under NASA’s contract with SpaceX. The Dragon spacecraft is packed with nearly 6,500 pounds of supplies, equipment, and new scientific experiments for the station’s Expedition 74 crew. The truce, which began earlier this year, has reduced fighting but violations remain frequent, particularly in southern Lebanon near the border with Israel. description: London police prepare for massive far-right and pro-Palestine protests on the same day as the FA Cup final. Extra powers granted to officers managing dual march
content: British authorities are mounting one of their most ambitious public order operations in recent years as London braces for two major demonstrations on Saturday: a far-right march organized by Tommy Robinson and a pro-Palestine rally, both scheduled for the same day as the FA Cup final at Wembley Stadium. Police sources told The Guardian that officers across central London have been granted enhanced powers to manage the competing crowds and potential flashpoints. title: Mars orbiter captures striking images of 'chaos and craters' carved by ancient floods
description: ESA's Mars Express orbiter captures high-resolution images of Shalbatana Vallis, revealing Mars' ancient water-carved valleys. Evidence suggests the Red Planet
content: The European Space Agency's Mars Express orbiter has released new high-resolution images of Shalbatana Vallis, a large channel near the Martian equator. The images reveal a patch of chaotic terrain within the valley, marked by craters and ridges believed to have been shaped by ancient floods. Shalbatana Vallis stretches approximately 650 miles (1,050 kilometers) long, with widths reaching up to 62 miles (100 kilometers) in some areas. The orbiter's latest observations focus on a small section of this valley, showcasing the dramatic geological features left behind by Mars' watery past. Scientis

title: Mandelson papers being withheld, says intelligence watchdog

content: The UK’s intelligence watchdog has formally accused the government of withholding records related to Lord Peter Mandelson, including intelligence files, despite requests under public records laws. The criticism escalated pressure on ministers over transparency and access to historical documents. content: A man has been charged in connection with a data leak that forced Scottish Football Association SFA referee John Beaton and his family into police-protected surveillance following a high-profile penalty controversy. Police Scotland confirmed the arrest on Thursday, stating the 32-year-old man from Glasgow was detained after an investigation into unauthorized access and distribution of Beaton’s personal data. content: Mali’s military, supported by Russian mercenaries, launched airstrikes on Tuesday targeting the rebel-held northern town of Kidal, regaining control after the city fell to a coalition of Islamist extremists and Tuareg separatists in a surprise offensive last month. The strikes mark a significant escalation in the junta’s campaign to reclaim territory lost since the rebel alliance advanced in late April, forcing government forces into retreat across northern Mali. content: Maine’s political landscape is shifting, and the state’s lone Republican senator, Susan Collins, faces a tough re-election battle that could reshape Maine’s financial future. As chair of the Senate Appropriations Committee, Collins wields significant power over federal spending, directing billions of dollars to states across the country. Her potential departure raises questions about whether Maine would retain its influence in Washington—or lose billions in critical federal funding in the process. content: Lawyers for Mahmoud Khalil Columbia University student, have petitioned an immigration appeals court to reopen and terminate his deportation case, citing new evidence they say proves the Trump administration manipulated the outcome to make an example of him. Khalil, a US permanent resident who is married to a US citizen, was detained by immigration enforcement agents in March 2025, one of several students targeted for their involvement in pro-Palestine campus protests across the US the previous year. title: Magnum Ice Cream shares jump 15% on private equity takeover talk
description: Magnum Ice Cream stock surges 15% after reports of a potential private equity takeover. The world's largest standalone ice cream maker spun off from Unilever in
content: Shares of Magnum Ice Cream surged 15% Thursday after a report suggested private equity firms are exploring a potential takeover of the company. The move comes just six months after Magnum completed its separation from Unilever, becoming the world’s largest standalone ice cream maker with a market valuation of over $5 billion. Magnum’s Split from Unilever in Early 2024 The separation was finalized in March 2024, ending Magnum’s decades-long affiliation with the consumer goods giant. The spin-off was part of Unilever’s strategy to focus on core brands and streamline operations. Magnum, known for

title: Libya football riots spread to Tripoli as fans torch government building
description: Violence erupts in Libya after disputed referee call sparks football riot, spreading to Tripoli where protesters set government HQ on fire. Local league match t
content: Violent clashes broke out in Libya after a contentious referee decision ended a local football league match in Tarhunah, a town 65 kilometers southeast of the capital. The unrest quickly escalated, with hundreds of football fans storming the Government of National Unity's headquarters in Tripoli, setting part of the building ablaze late Tuesday. Security forces responded with tear gas and live rounds, though no immediate reports of casualties were confirmed. title: 6 killed in Israeli strike as US extends Lebanon ceasefire talks
description: Israel strikes Lebanon as US extends ceasefire talks. Hezbollah and Israel continue cross-border fire despite truce efforts. Deadliest escalation in months.
content: An Israeli airstrike struck southern Lebanon on Friday, killing six people and escalating tensions with Hezbollah, the militant group linked to Iran. The strike came hours after the U.S. announced a 48-hour extension to a fragile ceasefire agreement brokered last month. The renewed violence marks the deadliest escalation in the region since heavy fighting began in October, with both sides exchanging rocket and artillery fire across the border. content: BRUSSELS — European Union heads of state and government convened an emergency summit in Brussels May 15, 2026, to approve a landmark migration agreement that would overhaul asylum policies across the bloc. The deal, negotiated over six months, includes stricter border controls, faster deportation processes and a mandatory relocation scheme for asylum seekers. content: Labour’s national executive committee (NEC) has granted Andy Burnham Greater Manchester mayor permission to seek selection as the party’s candidate for the Makerfield byelection. The decision clears the first major hurdle for Burnham, who served as an MP from 2001 to 2017 before becoming mayor in 2017. The byelection is triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Yvonne Fovargue, who stepped down last month. title: James Webb Space Telescope maps our universe's largest structure in unprecedented detail
description: Astronomers use the James Webb Space Telescope to map the cosmic web of galaxies with unprecedented clarity, revealing structures nearly 14 billion years old. T
content: Astronomers have used the James Webb Space Telescope to produce the most detailed map yet of the cosmic web, the universe’s largest structure. The new observations, part of the COSMOS-Web survey, reveal a vast network of galaxies, filaments, and voids stretching nearly 14 billion years into cosmic history. The cosmic web is a web-like arrangement of galaxies and dark matter that forms the skeleton of the universe. content: The Israeli military announced it carried out an airstrike in Gaza City targeting Izz al-Din al-Haddad, identified as Hamas’s military chief in the area. Izz al-Din al-Haddad is a senior figure within Hamas, though the militant group has not yet confirmed his status or whether he was hit in the strike. title: Israel kills Hamas commander in Gaza airstrike, says military
description: Israel says it killed Izz ad-Din al-Haddad, a Hamas commander and alleged architect of the October 7 attacks, in a Gaza airstrike. Here’s what we know.
content: Israel’s military announced late Thursday that it had killed Izz ad-Din al-Haddad, a senior Hamas commander, in an airstrike on Gaza City. The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) identified Haddad as one of the architects behind the October 7 attacks that killed about 1,200 people and triggered the ongoing war in Gaza. The IDF stated in a social media post that Haddad was targeted due to his role in planning and executing attacks against Israeli civilians. The negotiations, hosted by the U.S., aim to address long-standing border disputes and reduce escalating violence between the two sides. Diplomats from both countries are under pressure to reach a framework agreement to prevent further military confrontation. title: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire by 45 days after US talks

content: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire by 45 days following high-level talks in the United States, the State Department confirmed on Monday. The temporary truce, which has limited cross-border violence since October, was set to expire at midnight. Diplomats say the extension provides more time for negotiations to secure a lasting solution. The US State Department announced it will host further negotiations aimed at reaching a permanent political agreement on June 2 and 3. content: Muslim women in Bosnia and Herzegovina are increasingly challenging traditional norms by demanding access to mosques on Fridays and greater decision-making roles within the Islamic Community. While Friday prayers are typically male-dominated across the Muslim world, a growing number of devout Bosnian women are pushing for inclusion. This shift reflects broader Islamic feminism trends in the country, where women are redefining their roles despite persistent conservative social norms. title: England’s river bathing sites warn swimmers of unsafe water risks

content: Nearly all of England’s 11 designated inland river bathing sites now carry warning signs advising against swimming due to unsafe water conditions. The Environment Agency EnvironmentAgency confirmed that tests detected elevated levels of harmful bacteria, including E. coli and intestinal enterococci, at these locations. Al-Zaidi, 43, told lawmakers in a televised address that his government would enforce a state monopoly on weapons, signaling a direct challenge to militias that have operated with near impunity for years. title: Inflation rate projected to hit 6% in the second quarter, top economic forecasters say
description: Top economists project U.S. consumer prices to rise 6% in the second quarter, accelerating the worst inflation surge since 1982. Federal Reserve faces pressure
content: The U.S. inflation rate is poised to climb to 6% in the second quarter of 2022, the highest level since February 1982, according to a survey of top economic forecasters released Friday by CNBC. The projection underscores growing concerns over the fastest price surge in four decades, fueled by soaring energy and food costs. The survey, conducted amid mounting pressure on the Federal Reserve to curb inflation, signals that relief for consumers may not arrive soon. Economists polled by CNBC expect the inflation rate to peak above 6% before stabilizing later in the year. The forecast aligns with r

content: India's Adani Group has agreed to pay $18m to settle a civil fraud case in the US. The US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) had accused the company of paying bribes and misleading investors, which they denied. The case was settled without admission of guilt. The Adani Group is a major Indian conglomerate with interests in energy, logistics, and other sectors. The company is led by Gautam Adani, one of India's richest men. The sandwich contained 6.5g of salt, surpassing the UK’s recommended 6g daily maximum for adults. Gallagher’s experiment was simple: consume the sandwich and track how his body reacted over the next several hours. Within 30 minutes, his blood pressure rose noticeably. Workers report feeling abandoned in their career growth as companies prioritize AI-driven efficiency over human leadership. title: Harvey Weinstein's third sex crimes trial in New York ends in mistrial
description: Harvey Weinstein's third New York sex crimes trial ends in mistrial after jury deadlocks on rape charge from Jessica Mann. Next steps due by late June.
content: A New York jury failed to reach a unanimous verdict Friday in Harvey Weinstein's third sex crimes trial, marking the second mistrial in a year on the same rape charge brought by Jessica Mann. The deliberations lasted three days before jurors notified Judge Curtis Farber they could not reach a decision. Farber then issued a modified deadlock instruction, known as an Allen charge, urging jurors to resume talks. content: Nearly 200 U.S. cruise passengers are entering their fourth week of a mandatory 42-day quarantine after a hantavirus outbreak was detected aboard their ship, federal health officials confirmed Tuesday. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) and state health departments moved quickly to isolate passengers at Nebraska’s National Quarantine Unit in Omaha, a specialized facility designed to handle high-risk disease exposures. content: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said Friday he would not recommend that young Germans, including his own children, consider moving to the United States for education or employment at this time. Speaking at a Catholic convention in Germany, Merz cited what he described as a rapidly deteriorating social climate in the U.S. as the primary reason for his stance. ‘My great admiration for America is not increasing at the moment,’ Merz said, adding that the current environment raises concerns about personal safety and societal stability. content: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has broken from his traditionally pro-American stance, stating he would not recommend his children pursue education or careers in the United States. content: Residents of Jabalia in northern Gaza buried two Palestinians on Sunday after an Israeli airstrike targeted a funeral wake, defying a declared ceasefire. content: Reble, born in Assam, has carved a niche in India’s hip-hop scene by infusing his rhymes with the cultural rhythms of the northeast. title: Gaza hunger crisis worsens as aid drops 37% and funding plummets
description: Food aid in Gaza falls 37% as only 10% of needed funding is secured. Hundreds of thousands rely on weekly community kitchens for basic meals. Access to food rem
content: Humanitarian aid entering Gaza has plummeted 37% in recent months while only 10% of required funding has been secured, leaving families to scramble for food as community kitchens become their sole lifeline. Aid workers describe families sharing single meals while children go hungry, with malnutrition rates rising sharply across the territory. content: Former Sinaloa public security secretary Gerardo Merida Sanchez, 66, was arrested in Arizona on May 11 and transferred to New York on federal drug trafficking and cartel conspiracy charges, court records show. The charges stem from an indictment unsealed April 29 in New York, accusing Merida Sanchez and former Sinaloa Governor Ruben Rocha of conspiring with the Sinaloa Cartel to import narcotics into the U.S. in exchange for political support and bribes. title: 12 of 14 England river bathing sites fail bacteria safety tests

title: 2-year Treasuries hit 4% as Fed’s Warsh resists Trump’s rate cut push

The latest challenge came Thursday when 2-year Treasury yields, a key market indicator of future rate expectations, surged to 4%—their highest level this year. title: DoD faces 2028 clean audit deadline amid skepticism from Congress

content: The U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) is under increasing pressure to achieve a clean financial audit by the end of 2028, a deadline set by Congress in the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA). On Wednesday, the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform questioned whether the DoD can meet the statutory requirement given its long history of financial reporting failures. The deadline comes as the DoD prepares to account for roughly half of the federal government’s discretionary spending, making its financial transparency a critical issue for national fiscal oversight. title: Keir Starmer faces Labour turmoil after poor election results

description: Death toll rises to 24 including three teens after Russian missile flattened Kyiv apartment. Zelenskyy leads mourning as rescue crews finish rubble search. One
content: The death toll from a Russian cruise missile strike that flattened a nine-story apartment building in Kyiv rose to 24 on Friday, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced. The attack occurred Thursday as Russia launched its largest aerial barrage on Ukraine since the start of the full-scale invasion in February 2022, according to the Ukrainian air force. Emergency crews completed their search of the rubble after more than 36 hours, recovering the final bodies from the collapsed corner apartment block. Zelenskyy led the official day of mourning in Kyiv, standing alongside grieving famil

title: Death toll from Russia's largest Ukraine attack in 4 years rises to 24
description: Russia launches one of its biggest strikes on Ukraine in years. At least 24 killed as missiles and drones hit Kyiv and other areas. EU leaders condemn attack.
content: Russia struck Ukraine with one of its most extensive aerial assaults in years on Thursday, killing at least 24 civilians and injuring dozens more as missiles and drones rained down on Kyiv and nearby regions. The barrage marked Moscow’s largest coordinated attack since 2020, according to Ukrainian officials, and came amid escalating frontline fighting and stalled peace negotiations. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called the attack a deliberate attempt to terrorize civilians, while Kyiv’s mayor reported widespread power outages and damage to critical infrastructure. title: NASA's Curiosity rover detaches stuck Mars rock after drilling attempt
description: NASA's Curiosity rover successfully dislodged a stuck rock nicknamed 'Atacama' after days of drilling attempts on Mars. Discover how engineers freed the 28.6 lb
content: NASA’s Curiosity rover) successfully dislodged a stubborn rock sample nicknamed 'Atacama' from the Martian surface on May 1, 2026, after engineers spent six days troubleshooting the drill mechanism. The rock, estimated at 1.5 feet in diameter and 6 inches thick, became lodged in the rover’s drill during an April 25 drilling attempt. Engineers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Pasadena, California used a combination of robotic arm repositioning and high-frequency drill vibrations to free the sample, which ultimately broke into pieces upon detachment. title: Study: Crickets show pain-like responses to injuries, study finds
description: Researchers find crickets respond to injuries with pain-like behaviors, raising ethical questions about invertebrate suffering. Study published in leading scien
content: In a groundbreaking study published in Proceedings of the Royal Society B, researchers have documented that crickets exhibit behaviors strikingly similar to pain responses when injured. The findings suggest that even simple invertebrates may experience suffering, a revelation that could reshape ethical debates about animal welfare and scientific experimentation. The study, led by Matilda Gibbons at the University of Queensland, observed that injured crickets groomed wounds, avoided further harm and displayed reduced activity—behaviors previously associated only with vertebrates. content: > 📰 Continuing coverage: Scientists identify massive new dinosaur following Thailand dig The remains of a previously unknown dinosaur species, dubbed Nagatitan chaiyaphumensis, has been excavated in Thailand, according to a study published in the journal Scientific Reports. Scientists estimate the long-necked herbivore weighed as much as nine adult Asian elephants and is the largest ever found in Southeast Asia. description: Colorado’s Democratic Gov. Jared Polis commuted Tina Peters’ nearly nine-year prison sentence, allowing her release on parole June 1, 2026. The decision follows
content: Democratic Colorado Gov. Jared Polis reduced the prison sentence of Tina Peters, the former Mesa County clerk convicted of tampering with election equipment, and set her parole eligibility for June 1, 2026. The decision, announced Thursday, comes after a sustained lobbying effort by former President Donald Trump and his allies to secure Peters’ early release from state custody. Peters, 61, was convicted in 2024 on multiple felony counts including tampering with voting machines and identity theft. description: The weeks-long court battle between Elon Musk and Sam Altman exposed claims, counter-claims, and troubling tech industry practices. Here’s what we learned.

title: Canada OKs 49,000 Chinese EV imports annually at 6.1% tariff rate
description: Canada permits 49,000 Chinese-made electric vehicles for annual retail sales at a 6.1% tariff. Dealers prepare for new market competition.
content: Canada will permit up to 49,000 Chinese-made electric vehicles into the country annually for retail sales at a 6.1% tariff, the federal government announced Thursday. The decision marks a significant shift in Canada’s vehicle import policy, potentially reshaping the electric vehicle market by increasing competition and affordability for consumers. Under the new rules, Chinese automakers can ship EVs into Canada at the reduced tariff rate, which is substantially lower than standard import duties. The move aligns with Canada’s climate goals to accelerate EV adoption while balancing trade relatio

title: China will buy more U.S. oil because it is a natural trade partner, says Energy Secretary Wright
description: Energy Secretary Wright says China will buy more U.S. oil as Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, cutting off Middle East crude supplies. China faces oil shortage.
content: China will increase purchases of U.S. oil as Iran’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz cuts off most Middle East crude supplies, Energy Secretary Wright announced Tuesday. The disruption has forced Beijing to seek alternative sources, with U.S. oil emerging as a key option. The Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil shipments, has seen reduced traffic due to Iran’s actions, severely limiting China’s access to Middle Eastern crude. title: Cerebras stock drops 10% after blockbuster market debut
description: Cerebras Systems shares fell 10% in its first full day of trading after a blockbuster debut. Here’s what investors need to know.
content: Cerebras Systems’ shares dropped 10% in early trading Friday, marking a sharp pullback from its blockbuster market debut just one day earlier. The company’s stock opened at $18 per share on Thursday, giving it a valuation of $4.1 billion, but ended the day at $19.50—a 7.9% gain from its $16 IPO price. By Friday morning, the stock had retreated to $16.50, reflecting broader investor caution toward newly listed tech stocks. title: CENTCOM Commander Dismisses Reports That Iran Retains Most Of Its Missile And Drone Arsenal (Updated)
description: Admiral Brad Cooper says Iran can no longer threaten U.S. partners or forces as claimed in recent reports. Iran has seized another ship amid Strait of Hormuz te
content: The top U.S. military commander overseeing operations in the Middle East on Thursday rejected assertions that Iran still possesses a sizable portion of its missile and drone stockpile. Admiral Brad Cooper, who heads U.S. Central Command CENTCOM, told the Senate Armed Services Committee that Tehran can no longer project power through its arsenal as it once did. "Iran can no longer threaten regional partners, or the United States, in ways that they were able to do before, across every domain," Cooper said during testimony in Washington, D.C. title: Canada-led brigade in Latvia moves beyond tripwire role, commander says
description: Canada-led NATO brigade in Latvia now focuses on active defense against potential Russian threats, commander says. Brigade claims tactical credibility near east
content: RIGA, Latvia — The Canada-led NATO brigade in Latvia has moved beyond its original deterrence posture and is now training for active defense against potential Russian threats, according to its commander, Col. Kris Reeves. The shift toward what Reeves calls tactical credibility involves establishing forward positions and stationing troops in areas where they would engage in combat if a conflict were to arise. “Right now I have a brigade, there is nothing on the other side of the border that can take out this brigade,” Reeves said in an interview at the Sēlija training range in central Latvia. “

title: S&P 500 aims for 7th straight weekly gain as stocks close near records
description: The S&P 500 inches closer to a historic 7-week winning streak as weekly gains build momentum. Friday's top and bottom stocks show market trends.
content: The S&P 500 Standard & Poor's 500 Index edged higher Friday, staying within striking distance of a historic seven-week winning streak. The index closed just 0.2% below its record, as tech and consumer stocks led gains. A late-day surge pushed the benchmark closer to the milestone after a choppy morning of trading. Weekly gains hinge on economic data and earnings Investors gauged fresh economic reports and corporate earnings as the week wound down. title: Burnham cleared to run for selection in pivotal by-election
description: Andy Burnham has been approved to contest a UK by-election, potentially allowing him to challenge Prime Minister Rishi Sunak. Here’s what it means.
content: Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham Andy Burnham has been cleared to run in an upcoming UK parliamentary by-election, setting up a potential direct challenge to Prime Minister Rishi Sunak Rishi Sunak. Election officials confirmed Burnham’s eligibility to stand as a candidate in the vacant constituency, which became available after the sitting MP resigned last month. The announcement means Burnham could enter parliament if he wins the by-election, giving him a platform to publicly oppose Sunak’s leadership. title: Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week
description: Thousands of beds 45 feet underground in London’s Clapham deep shelter were rented for 3 shillings a night during the 1951 Festival of Britain. This unusual lod
content: London’s Clapham deep shelter, a relic of World War II, became an unlikely tourist attraction during the 1951 Festival of Britain. The London County Council London County Council transformed the mile-long bunker, situated 45 feet beneath the city’s underground railway London Underground, into a temporary lodging for festival-goers. Each night, visitors paid 3 shillings—equivalent to about 75 pence today—for a bed in the sprawling underground complex. The shelter, originally built during the Blitz to protect Londoners from air raids, was one of many deep shelters constructed across the city. By

title: Russian Vigilante Group Targets Birthday Party
description: Russkaya Obshina stages raids on activities contravening traditional Russian values, sparking concern over rising vigilantism in Russia.
content: A Russian vigilante group, Russkaya Obshina, has staged a raid on a woman's birthday party, highlighting the group's efforts to enforce traditional Russian values. The group, which has been active in recent years, has been known to target activities and events that it deems to be in contravention of traditional Russian values. The raid on the birthday party is the latest in a series of incidents involving Russkaya Obshina, which has sparked concern over the rise of vigilantism in Russia. title: British Gas pays £20m over prepayment meter force-fitting scandal
description: Energy giant British Gas has been ordered to pay £20m after regulator found it breached rules by forcing prepayment meters on vulnerable customers. Regulatory a
content: British Gas, one of the UK’s largest energy suppliers, has been ordered to pay £20 million after the energy regulator confirmed it breached licence conditions designed to protect vulnerable customers. The penalty follows an investigation into allegations that the company forced prepayment meters on households unable to pay bills through standard methods. The scandal has drawn sharp criticism from consumer groups and lawmakers, with calls for stricter oversight of the energy sector. title: British jet maker Aeralis collapses amid fight for RAF Red Arrows deal
description: Aeralis, the UK firm competing to replace the RAF's Red Arrows Hawk jets, has collapsed after failing to secure government funding. Company’s future now uncerta
content: British aerospace startup Aeralis has collapsed after its bid to build the next generation of jets for the RAF’s Red Arrows display team failed to secure government funding, the company announced Friday. The firm, which had been in talks with the Ministry of Defence for months, confirmed its closure in a brief statement, leaving workers and investors facing an uncertain future. Aeralis had pitched its two-seat, twin-engine jet as a modern replacement for the Hawk T1 aircraft currently flown by the Red Arrows, the UK’s famous aerobatic team. content: U.S. pharmaceutical giant Bristol Myers Squibb finalized a $2.2 billion partnership with Shanghai-based JW Therapeutics to co-develop three experimental drugs, signaling a major expansion of cross-continental drug development efforts. The deal includes an upfront payment of $300 million with potential milestone payments reaching up to $1.9 billion, according to regulatory filings. This agreement marks one of the largest international collaborations between a Western pharma company and a Chinese biotech firm in recent years. title: BRICS fails to issue joint statement as Iran war splits members
description: BRICS foreign ministers end New Delhi talks without a joint statement due to deep divisions over the Israel-Iran war. Iran’s FM had urged condemnation of US and
content: The BRICS alliance concluded a two-day meeting of foreign ministers in New Delhi on Friday without reaching a consensus on a joint statement, reflecting deep divisions over the escalating Israel-Iran conflict. India, which currently chairs the bloc, acknowledged “differing views among some members” regarding the Middle East crisis during a press briefing. The failure to issue a unified statement marks a rare public split among the 10-nation grouping, which includes Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa, Ethiopia, Egypt, Iran, Indonesia and the United Arab Emirates. title: Berkshire triples Alphabet stake and buys Delta stock while dumping Amazon in Greg Abel’s first quarter as CEO
description: Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel triples Alphabet stake, buys $2.6B in Delta stock and exits Amazon in Q1. Read the full investment shift details.
content: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. significantly increased its exposure to Alphabet Inc. and Delta Air Lines Inc. during the first quarter under CEO Greg Abel, who took over from Warren Buffett at the start of 2024. The conglomerate raised its stake in Google’s parent company by more than 200%, now holding $2.2 billion in Alphabet shares. Berkshire also disclosed a $2.6 billion investment in Delta Air Lines, marking a notable shift in the investment firm’s portfolio strategy. title: Berkshire Hathaway returns to airlines with $2.6 billion stake in Delta Air Lines
description: Berkshire Hathaway acquires a $2.6 billion stake in Delta Air Lines, marking its return to airline investments after an eight-year absence. Company disclosure s
content: Berkshire Hathaway has resumed investing in airlines, purchasing a $2.6 billion stake in Delta Air Lines, the company revealed in its latest regulatory filing. The Omaha-based conglomerate, led by Warren Buffett, last held airline stocks in 2016 before selling off its entire position in the sector. The stake in Delta represents Berkshire’s largest airline investment in nearly a decade. At the end of March, the company’s holdings in Delta were valued at $2.6 billion, making it the 14th-largest position in Berkshire’s $400 billion-plus equity portfolio. The game, inspired by the classic FTL: Faster Than Light, drops players into a high-stakes escape where every decision could mean the difference between life and death. Unlike its inspiration, Scattered Hopes amps up the tension with relentless Cylon pursuits and oppressive atmosphere. The roguelite structure ensures no two playthroughs feel the same. title: Astronaut shares stunning auroras over Australia from space
description: NASA astronaut captures rare green and pink auroras over Australia from the International Space Station. See the breathtaking photo shared May 15, 2026.
content: A NASA astronaut aboard the International Space Station ISS captured a rare and vivid display of auroras over Australia on May 15, 2026. The photograph shows a bright green streak of light curving along Earth's horizon, with a distinct pink glow near the top, highlighting the planet's curvature against the blackness of space. The image, shared on social media and by space agencies, offers a unique perspective of the natural phenomenon typically seen from the ground. title: Aston Villa qualify for Champions League with Liverpool win
description: Aston Villa secured a top-four finish in the Premier League with a 2-0 win over Liverpool, qualifying for next season's Champions League. Aston Villa move above
content: Aston Villa secured a top-four finish and Champions League qualification for the first time since 1983 with a 2-0 win over Liverpool at Villa Park on Sunday. Ollie Watkins scored twice as Aston Villa moved above their rivals into fourth place in the Premier League, ending Liverpool’s hopes of Champions League football next season. Watkins, who has scored 20 league goals this season, opened the scoring in the 11th minute with a low shot past Alisson Becker. His second, a clinical finish in the 58th minute, sealed the win and confirmed Aston Villa’s place in Europe’s elite competition. The victo

title: Asteroid 2026 JH2 to safely pass Earth next week
description: Asteroid 2026 JH2 discovered days before close pass. Scientists confirm no impact risk as space rock flies within 100,000 km of Earth.
content: A newly detected asteroid, designated 2026 JH2, is set to make a close pass by Earth on May 18, 2026, according to astronomers. The space rock, roughly 320 meters in diameter—nearly twice the height of the Eiffel Tower—was spotted just eight days before its closest approach. Despite its size and proximity, scientists at the European Space Agency’s Planetary Defense Office confirm there is no risk of impact. title: 4,000 US soldiers' Poland deployment canceled by Pentagon
description: Pentagon halts 4,000-soldier Poland deployment after advanced team and equipment already deployed. Army leaders face Congress over abrupt cancellation timing.
content: Army leadership faced sharp questioning Friday as lawmakers demanded answers over the Pentagon’s sudden decision to scrap a major deployment to Eastern Europe. Acting Army Chief of Staff Gen. Christopher LaNeve told a budget hearing that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth personally ordered the halt to the 9-month rotation of the 2nd Armored Brigade Combat Team, 1st Cavalry Division to Poland. The unit had already begun preparations, including casing its colors on May 1, dispatching an advance team and shipping equipment overseas, only to be pulled back at the last minute. title: Argentina retires last A-4 Fightinghawks after 60 years of service
description: Argentina retires its final A-4AR/OA-4AR Fightinghawk jets after six decades, as F-16s replace the aging fleet. The move marks a shift in the country's air comb
content: BUENOS AIRES — Argentina has officially retired its last A-4AR/OA-4AR Fightinghawk jets, capping six decades of service with the Argentine Air Force (FAA). The final decommissioning ceremony took place at Villa Reynolds Air Base in San Luis province on May 13, 2026, marking the end of an era for the 5th Air Brigade (V Brigada Aérea), the last unit operating the aging fleet. The FAA announced the retirement in a statement, calling it the "definitive decommissioning" of the Fightinghawk fleet. title: Anti-immigration AI videos traced to overseas fakers, BBC finds
description: Investigation finds UK anti-immigration social media profiles were run from overseas. Deepfake AI videos targeted British voters before key elections. Period.
content: A BBC investigation has exposed how AI-generated, anti-immigration videos that flooded UK social media feeds were produced by content farms in Sri Lanka and Vietnam. The videos, which featured fake British presenters attacking immigration policies, were designed to mimic authentic grassroots activism while concealing their foreign origins. Researchers traced the digital footprints of these accounts to commercial operations in Colombo and Hanoi, where teams used AI voice cloning and deepfake video tools to create persuasive but fabricated content. title: Judge delays $1.5B Anthropic AI copyright settlement over objections
description: Federal judge pauses approval of Anthropic's $1.5B AI copyright settlement after authors and class members object to lawyer fees and class payouts. Hearings set
content: A federal judge has delayed final approval of Anthropic’s $1.5 billion copyright settlement, the largest of its kind in U.S. history, after authors and class members raised objections over the terms. On Thursday, U.S. District Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin declined to approve the agreement without further clarification, signaling that the legal process is far from over. The settlement stems from allegations that Anthropic, an AI company, used copyrighted books without permission to train its AI models. content: A brief diet change may slow or even reverse aging at the cellular level, according to findings published in Aging journal. title: US reportedly dropped fraud charges against Indian billionaire after he hired Trump’s lawyer
description: US Department of Justice reportedly drops fraud charges against Gautam Adani, Asia's richest man, after he hired Trump’s lawyer and pledged $10bn US investment.
content: The U.S. Department of Justice has dropped fraud charges against Gautam Adani, the Indian billionaire ranked as Asia’s richest man, following an April meeting where his legal team led by Donald Trump’s personal lawyer, Robert J. Giuffra Jr., proposed a $10 billion U.S. investment and the creation of 15,000 jobs in exchange for the case’s dismissal, according to reports from The New York Times and Bloomberg. Federal prosecutors confirmed the decision to drop the case against Adani’s companies, which had faced allegations of conspiring to pay $250 million in bribes to Indian government officials

title: US waives $15,000 World Cup visa bond for 5 African teams' fans
description: U.S. State Department drops visa bond requirement for FIFA World Cup fans from Algeria, Cape Verde, Ivory Coast, Senegal and Tunisia. Fans must hold tickets and
content: The U.S. State Department announced Wednesday that fans from five African nations who qualified for the 2026 FIFA World Cup no longer face a hefty visa bond requirement. The exemption, effective immediately, applies to ticket holders from Algeria, Cape Verde, Ivory Coast, Senegal and Tunisia. These nations were among 50 countries subjected to a visa bond policy introduced by the Trump administration in 2025, requiring visitors to pay between $5,000 and $15,000 to obtain a U.S. tourist visa. content: The remains of U.S. Army Spc. title: Russian drone and missile attack kills 1, injures 31 in Kyiv

content: KYIV, Ukraine — A coordinated Russian drone and missile assault struck Ukraine’s capital Kyiv early Thursday, leaving at least one dead and 31 injured, according to local authorities. The attack triggered widespread air raid alerts and sent residents scrambling for shelter as explosions echoed across the city. Damage assessments were still underway late Thursday, with officials warning the toll could rise. title: UK economy grows 0.3% in March defying forecasts amid Iran tensions
description: UK economy expands by 0.3% in March, surprising economists who predicted a contraction. Figures released amid Iran tensions and global uncertainty. UK GDP growt
content: The UK’s economy grew by 0.3% in March, official figures from the Office for National Statistics (ONS) revealed on Friday, surprising economists who had anticipated a slight decline. The unexpected uptick contrasts with the 0.1% contraction recorded in February, marking a rebound in economic activity at the start of the second quarter. The 0.3% expansion in gross domestic product (GDP) for March was driven by growth in the services sector, which accounts for nearly 80% of the UK economy. title: The New Era of Air and Missile Defense
description: Modern air and missile defense is approaching a structural limit. The model that protected forces over the past two decades remains effective, but only within a
content: Modern air and missile defense is approaching a structural limit. The model that protected forces over the past two decades remains effective, but only within a narrower envelope than current threats demand. A new approach is required, built on fire-control-level integration, disaggregated survivable architectures, affordable magazine depth, and the integration of offensive action as the central element of defense.I am a retired U.S. Air Force brigadier general and now lead international business development and strategy for Northrop Grumman in Europe, North Africa, and the Middle East. title: The Guardian view on a cabinet resignation: Labour’s leadership crisis is really an identity crisis | Editorial
description: The prospect of a contest exposes a deeper truth: the party’s problems go far beyond Keir Starmer In politics, opportunities for supreme power are rare and flee
content: The prospect of a contest exposes a deeper truth: the party’s problems go far beyond Keir Starmer In politics, opportunities for supreme power are rare and fleeting. Yet rather than making challengers to Sir Keir Starmer more ruthless, this truth seems to have made them more cautious. The health secretary, Wes Streeting, resigned from the cabinet but did not launch a leadership bid. Rather than provoke a contest, Mr Streeting’s message to Sir Keir was that since his authority was gone, his duty was to depart and enable an orderly transition rather than cling to office. title: Doctor Who 1996 TV movie split US and UK fans 30 years ago
description: The 1996 Doctor Who TV movie starring Paul McGann failed in both the UK and U.S. after its May 1996 premiere. Explore why the hybrid production disappointed fan
content: The 1996 Doctor Who TV movie, starring Paul McGann as the Eighth Doctor, aired May 14, 1996, but alienated fans in both the U.K. and U.S. The film was a joint British-American production, blending elements of the long-running BBC series with Hollywood-style action and romance. While American networks hoped to revive interest in the franchise, British viewers found it too Americanized. Meanwhile, U.S. audiences struggled with its dense British sci-fi references and slower pacing. content: A team of researchers has proposed a new method to detect alien life by analyzing the distribution of amino acids, which could serve as a more reliable signature than previously used chemical markers. The study, published in a leading astrobiology journal, suggests that amino acids produced by living organisms exhibit a distinct and more diverse pattern compared to those generated by non-biological chemical reactions. The research team, led by Noah T. Goldberg), a planetary scientist at the University of Washington, argues that this approach could overcome limitations of current life-detection

title: New video shows alleged salmon abuse at US fish farm under Trump plan
description: Undercover footage from a major US salmon farm appears to show animal cruelty. Critics warn Trump's push for industrial fish farming could worsen conditions. Th
content: Undercover footage released Thursday appears to show workers at a Maine salmon hatchery repeatedly striking fish with hard objects and letting them pile up in overcrowded tanks, prompting renewed scrutiny of the Trump administration’s push to expand industrial fish farming. The video, filmed inside Cooke Aquaculture’s Cooke Aquaculture facility in Jonesboro, was obtained by the animal rights group Mercy For Animals and shared exclusively with The Guardian ahead of public release. title: Overnight Russian strikes kill five in Kyiv after rare daytime attack
description: Russian drone and missile strikes hit Kyiv overnight, killing at least five people. This follows a rare daytime attack that killed six earlier the same day. Ukr
content: Russian forces launched multiple drone and missile strikes on Kyiv overnight, killing at least five people, Ukraine’s emergency services reported Friday. The overnight assaults followed a rare daytime attack earlier in the day that killed six people, according to Ukrainian officials. Civilian infrastructure and residential neighborhoods bore the brunt of the latest strikes, which began around midnight local time and continued for several hours. title: Poll: Democrats would give up Black voting power to beat the GOP

content: A lot of Democrats are willing to sacrifice Black voting power to beat the GOP. In the two weeks since the Supreme Court significantly narrowed a key provision of the Voting Rights Act , Republicans have kicked off a fresh round of redistricting across the South . Their aim is to dismantle majority-minority districts, which they’ve long argued are unconstitutional, and to try to keep control of the House. description: Peter Thiel leads $140M funding round for Panthalassa, a floating wave-powered data center startup now valued at $1B. AI demand strains U.S. energy grid.
content: Billionaire investor Peter Thiel has taken the lead in a $140 million funding round for Panthalassa, a U.S.-based startup pioneering floating data centers powered by ocean waves. The investment, disclosed earlier this month, values the company at nearly $1 billion, according to the Financial Times. content: State officials in New York say the Salmon River district's special education program confined young children with disabilities in wooden boxes. Parents weren't notified. title: NASA's Perseverance rover is positively glowing in its new selfie on Mars
description: NASA's Perseverance rover captured a striking selfie on Mars in March 2026. See the latest image from Jezero Crater's western rim. The rover continues exploring
content: NASA’s Perseverance rover has beamed back a fresh self-portrait from the surface of Mars, this time showing its metal frame gleaming against the barren, rust-colored landscape. The image, captured on March 11, 2026, marks another milestone in the rover’s mission to study the geology and climate of Jezero Crater, a 45-kilometer-wide basin believed to have once held a lake billions of years ago. The latest selfie was taken along the western rim of Jezero Crater, specifically in a region dubbed 'Lac de Charmes'—French for 'Lake of Charms'—a nod to the crater’s watery past. title: NASA tests space gas stations with cryogenic fuel demo in orbit
description: NASA partners with Eta Space to test cryogenic fuel depots in orbit. The nine-month LOXSAT mission aims to enable long-term deep space missions with in-space re
content: NASA is teaming up with Eta Space of Rockledge, Florida, to launch a nine-month in-orbit demonstration of cryogenic fuel technology. The mission, called the Liquid Oxygen Flight Demonstration or LOXSAT, will test technologies critical for building orbital propellant depots—essentially gas stations in space—that could support long-term human and robotic exploration beyond Earth’s orbit. The demo is designed to validate 11 cryogenic fluid management technologies in microgravity, a foundation for future in-space refueling systems. The LOXSAT payload is already on display at Rocket Lab’s Spacecraf

title: NASA Seeks Industry Help for Mars Communications Network RFP
description: NASA issues RFP for Mars Telecommunications Network to support future Mars missions with reliable high-bandwidth data relay. Public invited to respond by June 1
content: NASA on Thursday issued a formal Request for Proposal (RFP) inviting industry collaboration to design the Mars Telecommunications Network. The network will consist of high-performance orbiters around Mars to provide reliable, high-bandwidth communications for future surface, orbital, and human exploration missions. The RFP follows a draft released April 2 and a NASA industry day held at Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland. title: Labour Byelection News
description: Labour MP to stand down, paving way for Andy Burnham to run in byelection and challenge Keir Starmer's leadership, amid party row, triggering UK politics update
content: Introduction to the Leadership Row: Labour MP to stand down to allow Burnham run for byelection amid leadership row. The Labour MP for Makerfield announced he would stand down, triggering a byelection, and putting the senior politician in contention to become the party’s next leader. The Greater Manchester mayor confirmed on Thursday he would be asking Labour’s ruling national executive committee to allow him to stand in the contest, putting a beleaguered Keir Starmer under intense pressure to not try to block him. This move has significant implications for the party and its future direction.

title: Japan fires missiles from Philippines in historic military drill 2026
description: Japan conducts first-ever missile firing from Philippine soil during Balikatan 2026 war games, sinking target ship amid rising South China Sea tensions.

description: Iran's small businesses struggle as war, inflation and internet blackout take toll on economy, causing widespread job loss and currency devaluation, with no end
content: Introduction to Iran's Economic Crisis: Iran's economy is facing an unprecedented crisis, with small businesses being the hardest hit. content: Introduction to the Strait of Hormuz Situation Iran's Revolutionary Guard (IRGC) has announced that a number of Chinese ships were allowed passage through the strategic Strait of Hormuz since the night before, marking a significant development in the ongoing tensions between Iran, the US, and Israel. The critical waterway, through which a fifth of global oil and liquefied natural gas shipments transited in peacetime, has largely been blockaded by Iran since US and Israeli attacks against it on February 28 triggered the current conflict. title: Iran military threat is diminished but not eliminated, CENTCOM chief says
description: Adm. Brad Cooper tells Senate the US-led 38-day bombing campaign weakened Iran’s global threat capacity but did not erase it. Both sides remain deadlocked in th
content: WASHINGTON — A 38-day U.S.-led bombing campaign has significantly diminished Iran’s capacity to threaten global security, but the threat has not been erased, the top U.S. military commander in the Middle East told lawmakers Thursday. Adm. Brad Cooper, commander of U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM), testified before the Senate Armed Services Committee that while Iran’s offensive abilities have been degraded, the Islamic Republic retains a “very moderate, if not small, capability” to conduct strikes against regional neighbors. Cooper emphasized the scale of the challenge posed by Iran’s size and di

title: Iran Internet Access
description: Iran expands tiered internet access amid continued online blackout, limiting connectivity to approved individuals and entities, raising concerns about digital r
content: Introduction to Iran's Internet Access. Iranians have had limited internet access during the ongoing war, with the government now looking at tiered services. It is unclear how regular internet connections jeopardise national security, but others sold by the government do not. The move is seen as an attempt to control the flow of information and maintain national security, while also providing some level of internet access to approved individuals and entities. title: Orchid breeders keep 10-year secrets to launch new hybrids
description: Breeders spend a decade perfecting new orchid hybrids in secret labs. Discover how rare blooms reach market and why profits drive the industry.
content: Orchid breeders around the world are spending up to a decade in secret labs to develop new hybrids, a process guarded as fiercely as any trade secret. The result? Rare orchids that can sell for thousands of dollars per plant. Unlike mass-produced varieties, these hybrids require precision, patience, and a hefty investment in technology to achieve unique colors, shapes, and fragrances that collectors demand. title: In Talks with Israel, Lebanon is Walking a Thin Line
description: This week, Israel and Lebanon are holding their third round of unprecedented direct talks on crucial issues, including borders, the disarmament of Hizballah, an
content: This week, Israel and Lebanon are holding their third round of unprecedented direct talks on crucial issues, including borders, the disarmament of Hizballah, and ongoing Israeli military action. The United States, which is clearly driving these talks, views them expansively as seeking a comprehensive peace and security agreement, although one that is contingent on Lebanese action against Hizballah. title: NASA images show ice retreat on Alaska's Aniak River in 2026
description: NASA Earth Observatory images reveal rapid ice breakup on Alaska's Aniak River in May 2026. See how warming temperatures are reshaping Arctic waterways.
content: Satellite imagery from NASA’s Earth Observatory shows the Aniak River in Alaska transitioning from a frozen state to open water by May 7, 2026—weeks ahead of typical seasonal patterns. The river, which winds east to west past the town of Aniak, had been completely ice-covered in late April, but recent images reveal stretches of flowing water cutting through previously solid ice. Nearby tributaries and surrounding terrain remain snow-covered, highlighting the uneven pace of thaw across the region. title: Hundreds of illegal motorbikes bulldozed in New York City crime crackdown
description: New York City officials destroyed over 300 unregistered motorcycles and mopeds linked to 1,000+ crimes in a week-long enforcement push. The crackdown follows a
content: NEW YORK — New York City officials on Monday demolished more than 300 unregistered motorcycles and mopeds as part of a broader enforcement push targeting organized crime groups using two-wheelers for quick getaways and illicit activities. What led to the crackdown The operation follows the fatal stray-bullet shooting of a 7-month-old in Brooklyn last month, where police said the suspect fled on an unregistered moped. title: EU Student Fees: 16x Hike
description: International student tuition fees in France to increase 16 times, sparking protests and debate about access to higher education in Europe.
content: Introduction to the Issue: The French government's plan to charge students from outside the EU 16 times more than previously has stoked discontent among international students and sparked a debate about access to higher education in Europe. The 'Choose France for Higher Education' scheme aims to remove an opt-out system that French universities often used to keep non-EU students' fees the same as their EU counterparts. title: Honda reports first loss since 1957 as it waters down EV strategy, but shares rise on 2026 forecasts
description: Japanese automaker Honda posts first loss since 1957 due to EV write-downs but shares climb on 2026 profit forecast. Stock rises despite $2.6 billion operating
content: Japanese automaker Honda on Thursday reported its first operating loss since 1957, totaling 413.4 billion yen ($2.6 billion), driven largely by massive write-downs in its electric vehicle operations. The company also posted a net loss of 423.9 billion yen for the fiscal year ending March 31, marking a sharp reversal from its long-standing profitability. Despite the historic setback, Honda’s shares surged after CEO Toshihiro Mibe announced the company expects to return to profitability in 2026. The loss reflects a major strategic pivot away from the rapid electrification of its vehicle lineup,

title: Honda reports first annual loss in 70 years, drops 2040 EV target
description: Japanese automaker Honda reports its first annual loss in seven decades, scrapping a 2040 target for all vehicles to be electric. Ends 70-year streak of profita
content: Japanese carmaker Honda has posted its first annual net loss in seven decades, ending a streak of profitability that began in 1955. The company reported a ¥11.8 billion ($78 million) net loss for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2024, reversing a ¥678.8 billion profit the prior year. The rare financial setback forces Honda to abandon its long-standing target of making all its vehicles electric by 2040, a goal it had reaffirmed as recently as 2021. title: HMRC signs £175m AI fraud detection deal with British firm Quantexa
description: UK tax authority HMRC awards £175m contract to Quantexa to use AI for detecting fraud and tax return errors. System goes live within 18 months.
content: The UK’s tax authority HMRC has awarded a £175m contract to Quantexa, a British financial data analytics firm, to implement an AI-driven system designed to detect fraud and inaccuracies in tax returns. The decision, announced on Thursday, marks one of the largest government investments in AI technology to date. The system is expected to go live within 18 months, according to HMRC officials. Quantexa’s platform will process millions of tax records daily, using machine learning algorithms to identify suspicious patterns, inconsistencies, and potential fraud. title: Jury begins deliberations in Elon Musk's OpenAI fraud trial
description: A nine-person jury now decides if OpenAI and Sam Altman breached agreements with Elon Musk. The high-stakes Silicon Valley trial wraps up with closing arguments
content: A nine-person jury began deliberations Thursday in Elon Musk’s lawsuit accusing OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman of fraud and breach of contract. The case, heard in a federal courthouse in Oakland, California, centers on whether the artificial intelligence company and Altman misled Musk and exploited his early financial contributions to OpenAI for their own gain. Closing arguments wrapped up Wednesday after weeks of testimony that featured Silicon Valley’s most prominent figures. title: Haiti gang clashes kill at least 78 in Port-au-Prince suburbs, UN says
description: Clashes between rival gangs in the suburbs of Haiti’s capital have killed at least 78 people and wounded 66 since Saturday, the United Nations office in Haiti s
content: Clashes between rival gangs in the suburbs of Haiti’s capital have killed at least 78 people and wounded 66 since Saturday, the United Nations office in Haiti said on Thursday. The toll included 10 civilians. Haiti, the Caribbean’s poorest country, has faced spiralling gang violence and worsening insecurity over the past two years. title: Dinosaur Fossil Found
description: A giant dinosaur has been identified from fossils in Thailand, weighing as much as nine elephants and being the largest in South-East Asia, according to BBC New
content: Introduction to the Discovery: The nagatitan is the largest dinosaur found in South-East Asia, with estimates suggesting it weighs as much as nine elephants, making it a significant discovery in the field of paleontology. This massive creature is a new species that has been identified from fossils found in Thailand, providing valuable insights into the region's prehistoric past. The discovery of the nagatitan is a testament to the rich biodiversity that once existed in South-East Asia, with the dinosaur's massive size indicating a thriving ecosystem. title: Germany’s domestic intel agency picks French software over Palantir
description: Germany’s domestic intelligence agency reportedly chooses French data analysis software over US-based Palantir. Learn why civil society supports the move.
content: Germany’s Federal Office for the Protection of the Constitution (Bundesamt für Verfassungsschutz, BfV) has reportedly declined a data analysis contract with US-based Palantir Technologies in favor of French competitor ChapsVision, according to reporting by Süddeutsche Zeitung, NDR, and WDR. The move, if confirmed, reflects growing European skepticism toward US-owned surveillance and data analytics firms amid privacy and sovereignty concerns. title: Geomagnetic storm may light up northern skies Friday and weekend
description: A fast solar wind stream could spark northern lights visible in parts of the northern U.S. Friday night and through the weekend. Check NOAA's forecast for your
content: A fast-moving stream of solar wind is expected to trigger G1 geomagnetic storm conditions overnight, boosting the odds of seeing the northern lights across parts of the northern United States starting Friday night and continuing through the weekend. The NOAA’s Space Weather Prediction Center issued a storm watch Thursday, warning that the solar wind could disturb Earth’s magnetic field enough to make auroras visible farther south than usual. title: NASA delivers fresh food and science gear to space station in 2026
description: NASA's Cygnus XL cargo ship delivered fresh oranges, apples, onions, and peppers to the International Space Station on April 19, 2026. The shipment also include
content: A NASA Cygnus XL) cargo spacecraft docked at the International Space Station on April 19, 2026, bringing fresh food and critical research supplies to the crew. The shipment included a variety of fresh produce—apples, oranges, onions, and peppers—giving astronauts a rare taste of Earth aboard the orbiting lab. Alongside the food, the cargo delivered over 2,300 pounds of new research hardware and science experiments designed to advance human health in space and on Earth. title: Four key excerpts from Streeting's resignation letter
description: Wes Streeting steps down as UK health secretary after reports he’s considering challenging Keir Starmer for Labour leadership. Full resignation letter excerpts
content: Wes Streeting has resigned as the UK’s health secretary, becoming the most high-profile Labour figure to step down amid growing speculation he was preparing a leadership challenge against Sir Keir Starmer. Streeting confirmed his departure in a resignation letter released on Monday, ending days of intense media scrutiny over his political future. The resignation comes after repeated reports that Streeting, a prominent centrist within Labour, was considering challenging Starmer for the party leadership. title: Nigeria Corruption Verdict
description: Former Nigerian Power Minister Saleh Mamman sentenced to 75 years in rare corruption case, authorities currently unaware of his location and unable to take him
content: Introduction to the Case: Former Power Minister Saleh Mamman was convicted last week for his involvement in a corruption scandal, receiving a 75-year sentence in a rare verdict for the country. The conviction is seen as a significant step towards addressing corruption in Nigeria's government. Mamman's sentence is one of the longest ever handed down to a public official in the country. The case against him was built around allegations of embezzlement and abuse of power during his tenure as Power Minister. title: Foreign student fee hikes in France trigger backlash
description: France plans to charge non-EU students up to €3,941 yearly, 16 times higher than EU rates. See how this compares to Europe's education fees.
content: France’s government has approved a sharp increase in tuition fees for students from outside the European Union, drawing immediate backlash and comparisons to higher education policies across Europe. Starting in the 202026/27 academic year, non-EU students enrolling in French universities will face annual fees of €2,895 for bachelor’s programs and €3,941 for master’s degrees—16 times the previous rates. The move is expected to generate an additional €250 million annually for French higher education institutions. Critics argue the change undermines access to education for international students

title: 5 Italians Die in Maldives Cave Dive
description: Five Italians died in a cave dive accident in the Maldives at 50m depth, with one body found so far by Maldivian rescuers. Maldives cave diving accident.
content: Five Italian divers died in a cave dive accident in the Maldives. The incident occurred at a depth of 50m, according to Italian authorities. Maldivian rescuers have so far recovered one body. The cause of the accident is not yet known. The accident is being investigated by local authorities. The Maldivian government has offered its condolences to the families of the victims. The Maldives is a popular destination for scuba diving and snorkeling. title: UK economy resilience: Six key trends in official data explained
description: UK economic resilience revealed in six key trends from official ONS data. What the latest figures show about growth, jobs, inflation and more.
content: The United Kingdom’s economy has outperformed expectations in early 2024, with gross domestic product expanding by 0.6% during the first three months of the year. The growth figure, released by the Office for National Statistics (ONS), contradicts widespread recession forecasts from late 2023 and suggests Britain may avoid the downturn predicted by many economists. This resilience comes despite persistent challenges including high borrowing costs, weak business investment, and ongoing geopolitical tensions affecting global trade routes. title: Extremely positive and productive talks': Trump praises Xi meeting
description: Chinese President Xi Jinping warned his US counterpart Donald Trump that missteps on Taiwan could push their two countries into 'conflict', a stark opening salv
content: Chinese President Xi Jinping warned his US counterpart Donald Trump that missteps on Taiwan could push their two countries into 'conflict', a stark opening salvo as they met in Beijing on Thursday for a two-day summit. The Chinese Foreign ministry said the comments came in a closed-door meeting that lasted more than two hours. It was followed by a friendlier, opulent banquet during which Trump and Xi stressed the importance of working together. Story by Carys Garland. title: Satellite snaps rare photo as Earth photobombs reflector deployment
description: ViaSat-3 F2 satellite deploys giant reflector while Earth photobombs the space shot. See the striking image of the semi-transparent honeycomb reflector overlapp
content: A ViaSat-3 F2 satellite from ViaSat captured a striking image Wednesday as its massive reflector fully deployed with Earth photobombing the shot. The semi-transparent yellow honeycomb pattern partially overlapped the left third of the planet, creating a rare visual contrast against the black backdrop of space. The deployment marks a critical milestone for the high-capacity communications satellite, designed to expand global broadband coverage. content: Dyson’s new air purifier can target multiple people. | Image: Dyson Dyson has announced a new air purifying bladeless fan that's its first to incorporate an AI-powered camera for tracking the location of people in a room. Dyson already uses a mix of cameras and AI in its robovacs , but since the new Find+Follow Purifier Cool is stationary and not at risk of running into abandoned socks, here it's used to direct fresh air towards you and to conserve power by automatically turning the fan off when a room is empty. title: Directive 8020' is an introspective look at sci-fi horror and our fear of the unknown
description: Supermassive Games’ new space horror title asks what the unknown future holds for its franchise and players. Explore the fear of cosmic exploration here.
content: Supermassive Games is taking a hard look at its sci-fi roots with Directive 8020, a space horror game that forces players to confront the cold dread of exploring the cosmos. The title marks a shift from the studio’s traditional narrative-driven horror, instead prioritizing psychological terror tied to the unknown. Set against the vast expanse of space, the game challenges players to navigate uncharted territories while grappling with existential questions about humanity’s place in the universe.
