title: Dell and RAMageddon are watering down the Alienware brand
description: I remember a time when Alienware refused to make a thinner laptop - the company didn't want to compromise on its builds. But today, Dell is slapping the Alienwa
content: I remember a time when Alienware refused to make a thinner laptop - the company didn't want to compromise on its builds. But today, Dell is slapping the Alienware name on a piece of hardware that sounds utterly watered down. It's partly RAMageddon 's fault. The new five-pound Alienware 15 is supposedly an entry-level gaming laptop, one designed to invite those with less cash into the fold. That's an admirable goal at a time when all kinds of gaming hardware suddenly costs more . title: Burned out and going nowhere: the American worker is too mentally drained to even look for a new job
description: The résumé sits unfinished in a Google Doc. The LinkedIn tab stays open, untouched. For millions of American workers, the search for something better has ground
content: The résumé sits unfinished in a Google Doc. The LinkedIn tab stays open, untouched. For millions of American workers, the search for something better has ground to a halt — not because the jobs aren t there, but because they ve done the math. The door, it turns out, is barely open. More than half of U.S. workers — 53%, according to a new Glassdoor poll of over 1,300 professionals — say they have paused their job search entirely to protect their mental health. title: AI job fears overstated, warns Raspberry Pi founder Eben Upton
description: Raspberry Pi CEO Eben Upton says fears AI will destroy tech jobs are overblown and could hurt the economy. AI impact on jobs debunked.
content: Artificial intelligence will not wipe out tech jobs and could harm the economy if people shy away from computing careers due to exaggerated fears, warned Eben Upton, co-founder of the low-cost computer company Raspberry Pi. Upton, also a chip designer at Broadcom, said recent warnings about AI’s job-destroying potential are overblown. He pointed to AI’s role in automating repetitive tasks, freeing workers to focus on higher-value problem-solving. title: ‘You have to be where the pollution is’: the inventor hoping to fix your washing machine to stop microplastics
description: A new microplastics filter developed by Matter Industries founder Adam Root traps fibers before they enter wastewater. Here’s how it works and why it matters.
content: A washing machine in a quiet London home hums softly after each wash, but this sound signals more than clean clothes. It signals the quiet operation of a microplastics filter designed by Adam Root, founder of Matter Industries. Root’s invention, a small box installed above the machine, connects via a pipe to the wastewater line. After each cycle, the device whirs to life, trapping fibers that would otherwise flow into drains and waterways. Root says the problem starts in every household wash. title: Will Starship launch from foreign shores? SpaceX 'constantly exploring' options for megarocket liftoff sites
description: SpaceX is actively exploring new launch sites for its Starship megarocket beyond U.S. borders to expand operations and meet demand. Launch sites outside U.S. un
content: Elon Musk’s SpaceX is actively pursuing international launch sites for its Starship megarocket, according to recent statements. The company is evaluating locations both within and outside the U.S. as it seeks to accelerate flight cadence and reduce dependence on its primary base at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida. Starship, the largest and most powerful rocket ever built, has faced repeated delays at U.S. sites due to regulatory reviews and technical challenges, pushing SpaceX to explore alternatives abroad. description: The US Congress faces challenges in stopping Trump's Iran war due to constitutional design issues.

content: Scientists have long puzzled over the origin of cosmic rays—high-energy particles that constantly bombard Earth. A new study published in Physical Review Letters suggests that some of the most powerful cosmic rays may originate from cataclysmic events: the merger of two neutron stars. These collisions release energy so intense that they could fling superheavy particles into space at speeds approaching the speed of light. content: Terence Robinson, a Cornell University horticulture professor, still calls the 2015 Valentine’s Day thaw-and-freeze cycle the “Valentine’s Day Massacre” — but not because of gangsters. The sudden temperature swing, from 60°F to below freezing in hours, killed 90% of the apple buds in his New York orchards, crippling the season’s crop. title: Lasers reveal space junk pollution threat to Earth's upper atmosphere

content: Scientists at the Leibniz Institute of Atmospheric Physics have discovered a new source of pollution in Earth's upper atmosphere. This finding challenges previous assumptions about natural atmospheric composition and introduces potential risks to the ozone layer and climate systems. title: How Wegovy and Mounjaro work to aid weight loss explained
description: Wegovy and Mounjaro are GLP-1 drugs that reduce appetite and slow digestion to help people lose weight. Available via NHS and privately.
content: Wegovy and Mounjaro belong to a class of drugs called GLP-1 receptor agonists, which mimic the action of a natural hormone in the gut. Both medications are approved for chronic weight management in adults with obesity or overweight conditions related to other health risks. Wegovy contains semaglutide, the same active ingredient in the diabetes drug Ozempic. Mounjaro contains tirzepatide, which targets both GLP-1 and GIP receptors, offering a dual approach to appetite regulation. title: Venus’s 3,700-mile acid cloud explained by giant hydraulic jump
description: Scientists say Venus’s towering 3,700-mile acid cloud forms like water spreading in a sink. Study reveals solar system’s largest hydraulic jump.
content: A vast, 3,700-mile-long bank of sulfuric acid clouds on Venus is created by the largest hydraulic jump ever observed in the solar system, a new study reveals. Researchers say the phenomenon works exactly like water flowing from a tap into a sink, where fast-moving fluid suddenly slows and thickens as it spreads outward. The study, published in The Planetary Science Journal, describes how sulfur dioxide and water vapor high in Venus’s atmosphere drive this massive jump. title: Swindon opens UK’s first women-only mental health crisis house
description: Swindon launches UK’s first women-only mental health crisis house to provide intensive support as an alternative to hospitalisation. Community-based care begins
content: Swindon will open the UK’s first women-only mental health crisis house on June 15, providing intensive residential support as an alternative to hospitalisation for women in acute mental distress. The facility, funded by Swindon Borough Council and run in partnership with Avon and Wiltshire Mental Health Partnership NHS Trust, aims to address gaps in crisis care for women who may feel unsafe or unsupported in mixed-gender hospital wards. title: What now for the role of high representative in Bosnia?
description: Christian Schmidt resigns as Bosnia's High Representative after five years. International powers rethink the powerful post’s future role and authority.
content: Christian Schmidt, Germany’s former agriculture minister, will step down as Bosnia’s High Representative on May 15, the Office of the High Representative (OHR) confirmed Monday. His resignation marks the end of a five-year term during which he wielded extraordinary authority, including the power to impose laws and dismiss elected and appointed officials without local consent. title: Uranus and Neptune could be full of rocks, new study suggests
description: Research indicates Uranus and Neptune could have rocky interiors beneath their atmospheres. Scientists re-examine planet formation theories. A new study challen
content: The outer planets Uranus and Neptune may not be as icy as once believed. A new study published in Nature Astronomy suggests their interiors could be dominated by rock rather than ice, upending decades of planetary science assumptions. Researchers from Arizona State University conducted simulations showing that the traditional ice giant model may oversimplify the planets' compositions. New evidence challenges old models The long-held view has been that Uranus and Neptune are composed primarily of water, ammonia, and methane ices. title: UK: Keir Starmer tells Cabinet he will continue in place, as four junior ministers quit
description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces pressure after 4 junior ministers quit following UK election losses. Starmer insists he will remain in office.
content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Tuesday reaffirmed his commitment to remain in office despite the resignation of four junior ministers following last week’s UK election losses. Starmer met with his Cabinet as internal pressure against his leadership mounted, with lawmakers demanding clarity over his future plans. A major speech on Monday, where Starmer declared he was “not walking away,” failed to quell dissent within his party. title: Thousands of deaths per year caused by invisible wildfire pollutant, satellite data shows
description: Satellite data links wildfire smoke to thousands of U.S. ozone-related deaths each year. Air quality risks from wildfires extend beyond visible smoke.
content: Satellite observations have exposed a hidden health threat from wildfires: ozone pollution linked to thousands of premature deaths in the U.S. every year. A joint study by NASA and university researchers found that wildfire smoke significantly raises ground-level ozone concentrations, a key component of smog that harms respiratory and cardiovascular health. The findings, published in Nature Communications, quantify how smoke from distant fires can degrade air quality even in regions untouched by flames or visible haze. title: Russia and China use lawfare to challenge Arctic control

content: Russia and China are increasingly using lawfare to assert dominance over Arctic waters, transforming a region once defined by cooperation into a contested geopolitical battleground. Their tactics—ranging from restrictive maritime regulations to coordinated legal challenges against Western continental shelf claims—mirror the confrontational strategies seen in the South China Sea, raising concerns among Arctic nations about growing instability. title: The Kabul rehab centre hit by deadly Pakistani strike
description: UN and victims' families reject Pakistan's claim of targeting 'terrorist infrastructure' in deadly Kabul rehab center strike. 40+ confirmed dead. Learn more.
content: A Pakistani military strike struck a rehabilitation center in Kabul on Monday, killing at least 43 people and injuring 24, according to Afghan officials. The attack targeted the Kabul Orthopedic and Physiotherapy Center, a facility supporting disabled and injured Afghans, many of whom are war victims. content: Startup Star Catcher Star Catcher has secured $65 million in funding to develop the world’s first orbital power grid, a system that will use lasers to transmit energy to satellites. The company says its technology can deliver up to 10 times more power on demand to existing satellites without requiring any modifications to their hardware. A test flight is planned for March 2025 at EverBank Stadium in Jacksonville, Florida, where engineers will demonstrate the wireless energy transfer system in real-world conditions. title: Lebanon's displaced find relief in sports at Beirut stadiums
description: More than a million Lebanese displaced by war now use sports to cope with trauma while sheltering in Beirut stadiums. Over a dozen activities available.
content: Beirut’s Camille Chamoun Sports City Stadium [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CamilleChamounSportsCityStadium] has become an unexpected sanctuary for displaced Lebanese families fleeing relentless bombings in southern Lebanon. Since fighting between Israeli forces and Hezbollah escalated in February, over a million people have been uprooted, many seeking shelter in the stadium’s rows of tents. Among them is 65-year-old Hassan Seif al-Din, a martial arts coach who now teaches children self-defense techniques to help them process trauma. content: > 📰 Continuing coverage: SpaceX launches NASA cargo mission to ISS with 6,500 lbs of supplies A SpaceX Dragon cargo capsule rocketed toward the International Space Station Thursday, carrying 6,500 pounds of supplies, science experiments and hardware for the orbiting lab. This marks the first time the new V3 configuration has undergone a full fueling rehearsal, a key step in validating its readiness for launch. title: South Carolina Republicans tank redistricting, for now
description: The South Carolina Senate just made it harder for the state to redraw its congressional map, resisting pressure from President Donald Trump. Lawmakers on Tuesda
content: The South Carolina Senate just made it harder for the state to redraw its congressional map, resisting pressure from President Donald Trump. Lawmakers on Tuesday failed to reach the two-thirds majority needed to approve a measure that would have allowed them to take up a vote on redistricting even after the legislative session ends later this week. Five Republicans joined all Democrats in voting against the proposal. Republican Gov. Henry McMaster could still call a special session, though his office has so far dismissed that idea. title: Russia to deploy Sarmat ICBM this year after successful test
description: Russia announces operational deployment of Sarmat ICBM later in 2024 after successful test launch from Plesetsk Cosmodrome. Details here.
content: MOSCOW — Russia has carried out a successful test of its long-delayed Sarmat intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM), Russian President Vladimir Putin announced Tuesday, with plans to deploy the weapon operationally by the end of the year. The test launch from the Plesetsk Cosmodrome in the Arkhangelsk region occurred at 11:15 a.m. Moscow time, according to the Kremlin. Russian officials said the missile traveled thousands of kilometers and struck its designated target at the Kura Missile Test Range on the Kamchatka Peninsula in Russia’s Far East roughly 30 minutes later. The Department of Defense had initially proposed no funding for the E-7 in its budget blueprint, raising alarms about the future of the program that aims to replace the U.S. Air Force’s E-3 Sentry fleet. content: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. The Increment 2 upgrade, backed by a $296 million request in the 2027 Fiscal Year budget, introduces an advanced seeker capable of tracking moving targets. This shift marks a dramatic turnaround for a program that faced cancellation in 2021 after years of developmental setbacks and failed tests. title: NASA's Psyche asteroid probe will fly within 3,000 miles of Mars today: Here's what to expect

content: NASA’s Psyche spacecraft is set for a close encounter with Mars on Friday, executing a precisely timed gravity assist to slingshot toward its final destination: the metal-rich asteroid Psyche. The flyby, scheduled for May 15, 2026, will bring the probe within 3,000 miles of the Red Planet’s surface, allowing Mars’ gravity to accelerate the spacecraft and adjust its trajectory for the 2029 arrival at the asteroid belt target. title: Mesmerizing vortexes swirl near an Antarctic volcano | Space photo of the day for May 12, 2026

content: A striking satellite image from NASA released on May 12, 2026, captures Von Kármán vortex streets near Peter I Island in Antarctica. The photograph, taken on February 11, 2026, shows symmetrical swirling patterns in the snow, with dark water visible at the centers of each vortex. These vortices form when steady winds flow around tall obstacles, creating alternating spinning eddies on the lee side of the obstruction. title: JP Morgan could scrap £3bn London HQ if Starmer is replaced by PM ‘hostile to banks’
description: JP Morgan could scrap its £3bn Canary Wharf headquarters plan if a Labour leader hostile to banks replaces Keir Starmer. Bank warns of risks to UK investments.
content: JP Morgan’s £3bn ($3.9bn) London headquarters project in Canary Wharf could be scrapped if a Labour prime minister hostile to the banking sector takes office, CEO Jamie Dimon warned on Friday. The US banking giant revealed plans for the 60-storey tower in November 2023, just hours after Labour’s then-shadow chancellor Rachel Reeves avoided tax increases on lenders in her autumn budget statement, following intense lobbying from the financial industry. title: Mamdani's Tax Fight: Is It Over?
description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's 'Tax the Rich' initiative faces uncertainty as the city's budget deficit is addressed without state-level tax hikes.
content: Introduction to the Budget Debate. For months, Zohran Mamdani has pressed the case that Albany must raise income and corporate taxes to help plug New York City's $5.4 billion budget deficit. So in rolling out an executive budget that closes the gap without his favored state-level tax hikes, Mamdani made a significant concession, showing he did not, in fact, need the increases from Albany to shore up his spending plan. title: IRGC Navy Claims Vast Expansion In Its Definition Of Strait Of Hormuz (Updated)
description: Iran's IRGC navy redefines Strait of Hormuz closure zone, expanding it from 20-30 miles to over 200 miles. Global shipping faces new risks amid U.S.-Iran tensio
content: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy has redefined the boundaries of the Strait of Hormuz, expanding its closure zone from a narrow 20-30 miles to a sweeping 200-300 miles, according to its naval commander. Political Deputy of IRGC Navy Mohammad Akbarzadeh told state-run FARS News Agency that the waterway is no longer confined to a handful of islands but now encompasses a vast crescent-shaped area that disrupts long-standing shipping routes. The announcement follows the IRGC’s recent enforcement of a partial closure to most vessels, a move that has already disrupted global oil

title: Record 32.3m Displaced in 2025
description: Internal displacements caused by violence or conflict reach record high, surpassing disaster-driven displacements for the first time, with 82.2m people displace
content: Introduction to the Crisis: The number of internal displacements triggered by conflict or violence around the world reached a record high in 2025, surpassing the number of disaster-driven internal displacements for the first time. A report published by the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre shows that by the end of 2025 there were 32.3m conflict-driven internal displacements. That is 60% higher than those recorded the previous year, and – for the first time since data collection began in 2008 – above displacements driven by natural disasters, which reached 29.9m in 2025. title: What is hantavirus? Risk levels after passenger exposure on flight
description: Hantavirus exposure reported on international flight. Health experts outline transmission risks and prevention. Public health advice now available.
content: Health authorities confirmed a potential hantavirus exposure incident involving passengers on an international flight. The repatriation effort follows reports that individuals may have come into contact with infected rodents during transit. The World Health Organization and local health departments are investigating the case, which has raised concerns about the virus’s transmission risks. What is hantavirus and how does it spread? title: Keir Starmer's progress on NHS waits and housing pledges in 2025
description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer reviews his 2024 pledges on NHS waiting lists and housing targets. Find out how his government is performing on key promises.
content: Prime Minister Keir Starmer delivered his first major policy update of 2025 this week, highlighting progress—and setbacks—on two of his administration’s most watched promises from 2024: cutting NHS waiting lists and building more new homes. While some targets have been met, others have fallen short, raising questions about the pace of change under his government. NHS England data released in January shows that despite additional funding and targeted interventions, the number of patients waiting longer than 18 weeks for non-urgent treatment fell by just 3% in the last six months. title: 2026 wildfires burn 150M hectares as El Nino threat grows
description: Over 150 million hectares burned globally in early 2026 as scientists warn a strong El Nino could worsen wildfires this year. Record fire season sparks climate
content: Global wildfires scorched over 150 million hectares in the first months of 2026, more than twice the size of Texas, as scientists warn a supercharged El Nino could make the second half of the year even worse. Researchers at World Weather Attribution described the year’s fire season as starting “very fast,” with burned areas 50% higher than the average for this time of year. The current global burn total exceeds the previous record set since tracking began in 2012 by over 20%, according to Theodore Keeping, an extreme weather researcher at Imperial College London and a WWA member. title: Miss Germany 2026 contestant in hijab sparks AfD debate
description: Büsra Sayed became the first hijab-wearing Miss Germany contestant, drawing criticism from the far-right AfD. Her response went viral across German media and so
content: Büsra Sayed, 27, entered the Miss Germany pageant in March 2026 wearing a hijab from her own collection, becoming one of the first contestants in the competition's history to do so. The move drew immediate attention from Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) party, which criticized her participation during a parliamentary debate on International Women's Day. Sayed, an entrepreneur who sells hijabs, wore the headscarf on stage during the pageant's final round, a competition now emphasizing empowerment and achievements beyond physical appearance. title: EU invites Taliban officials to Brussels for migrant return talks
description: European Commission invites Taliban representatives to Brussels for talks on deporting Afghan migrants. Brussels insists no recognition of Taliban authority.
content: The European Commission invited officials from Afghanistan’s Taliban to Brussels for talks on deporting Afghan nationals who have been denied asylum in the European Union. European Commission spokesman Markus Lammert confirmed the invitation Tuesday, calling the planned Brussels meeting a follow-up to technical talks held in Afghanistan in January. Lammert emphasized the invitation ‘does not by any means constitute a recognition’ of the Taliban as Afghanistan’s government. title: Poland to arm M28 Skytruck with counter-drone gear for NATO
description: Poland confirms it is modifying its M28 Skytruck aircraft for the counter-drone mission, a first for a NATO air force. Here’s why this matters.
content: Poland is arming its M28 Skytruck twin-turboprop utility aircraft to counter drone threats, a move confirmed by a top Polish Air Force general. The announcement marks the first time a NATO air arm will deploy a modified utility aircraft for this mission. Maj. Gen. Ireneusz Nowak, inspector of the Polish Air Force, revealed the plan during a panel on air superiority at the Defence24 Days event. The M28 Skytruck, derived from the Soviet-era Antonov An-28 Cash, will join a growing list of aircraft repurposed for drone defense. title: New daily pill may help maintain weight loss after obesity injections
description: A new once-daily pill called orforglipron could help people keep weight off after stopping obesity injections like Wegovy or Ozempic. US trials show promising e
content: A new oral medication, orforglipron, is showing early success in U.S. trials as a potential follow-up treatment for people who stop taking weekly obesity injections like Wegovy or Ozempic. Unlike daily diet pills of the past, orforglipron belongs to a class of drugs that mimic the action of GLP-1 hormones, similar to the active ingredients in the popular obesity drugs currently on the market. The compound, developed by Eli Lilly and Company, is designed to be taken once a day as a tablet. Early phase trial results, published in medical journals, indicate it helped participants maintain reduced

title: Cowboy Space raises $275 million to launch AI data centers on brand-new rocket
description: Cowboy Space Corp. secures $275 million to launch AI-powered data centers into orbit using its own rocket. Company plans to deploy orbital servers by 2026.
content: Cowboy Space Corp. Cowboy Space Corporation, formerly known as Orbital Data Systems, announced Friday it secured $275 million in Series B funding to construct and operate artificial intelligence data centers in low Earth orbit. The company plans to deploy these orbital servers using its own rocket, the SkyRider-1, under development since 2022. The funding round was led by Silicon Valley venture capital firm Horizon Capital with participation from SpaceTech Partners and sovereign wealth funds in Singapore and the United Arab Emirates. title: Closing the Gap? Italy Sets New Rules for Its First National Security Strategy Amidst Old Obstacles
description: Italy prepares to publish its first-ever national security strategy, ending decades of delays. Learn about the reasons behind the move and its potential impact.
content: Italy is poised to publish its first-ever national security strategy, breaking a decades-long impasse that made it the only G7 nation without such a framework. The document, expected in the coming months, marks a significant shift in how Italy approaches defense and security amid rising global tensions. For generations, Italy avoided formalizing a national security strategy due to a mix of political instability, institutional rivalries, and reliance on U.S. security guarantees in Europe and the Mediterranean. title: 40 Tory MPs lose confidence in UK PM Chris Mason as revolt grows
description: Chris Mason faces growing party revolt with 40 Tory MPs publicly losing confidence. What happens next to the UK prime minister's leadership? Follow live updates
content: Prime Minister Chris Mason’s tenure is under severe strain as Conservative MPs publicly abandon him, with 40 members now declaring their loss of confidence in his leadership. The rebellion marks a rapid escalation in party dissent, forcing Mason to confront the most serious challenge to his authority since taking office. Escalating revolt threatens PM’s survival The revolt began quietly but gained momentum overnight, with Labour MPs also posting public statements expressing their lack of confidence in Mason’s ability to govern. title: Easy Cherry Cobbler With Lemon-Cream Biscuits for Summer Dessert
description: Make this sweet cherry cobbler with tender lemon-cream biscuits in one bowl. A simple summer dessert with juicy fruit and golden topping.
content: Cherry cobbler with lemon-cream biscuits ranks among summer’s most effortless showstoppers. The dessert layers a bubbling, ruby-red cherry filling beneath tender, golden biscuits flecked with bright citrus. Each bite offers juicy fruit balanced by a slightly tangy, buttery top. The recipe comes from the test kitchen at Bon Appétit, where editors developed it as a foolproof way to showcase peak-season cherries without fuss. title: AI and Rubin Observatory to probe dark energy with star explosions
description: Scientists use AI and the Rubin Observatory to reexamine Type 1a supernovas as cosmic yardsticks for dark energy research. Researchers hope to uncover missing p
content: Astronomers are turning to artificial intelligence and the upcoming Vera C. Rubin Observatory to challenge long-held assumptions about the universe’s expansion. The observatory, set to begin operations in 2025, will survey the entire southern sky every few nights, capturing vast amounts of data on Type 1a supernovas—exploding stars that serve as cosmic yardsticks for measuring distances across the universe. title: Hamburg appoints first woman as Bundesliga sport director
description: Hamburg SV names Kathleen Krüger as new sport director, the first woman to hold the role permanently in Bundesliga history. Period.
content: Hamburg SV announced Tuesday that Kathleen Krüger will become the club’s new sport director effective July 1. The appointment makes her the first woman to hold such a permanent position at a Bundesliga club, marking a historic shift in German football leadership. The club described Krüger as a highly respected figure with extensive experience at the highest international level, including 17 years at FC Bayern Munich, where she most recently served as head of organization and infrastructure. title: Global oil inventories hit 8-year low as second Iran shock looms
description: Global oil inventories near eight-year lows with Goldman Sachs warning of 98-day supply by May. Markets appear calm despite looming supply crunch. Learn key ris
content: Global oil inventories are approaching their lowest level in eight years, raising alarms about a potential supply crunch despite relatively stable market prices. Goldman Sachs analysts estimate that global oil stocks could fall to just 98 days of demand by the end of May 2026, signaling a severe shortage. Yet, Brent crude prices have eased to around $100 a barrel, down from a post-Iran war peak of $126 in April. West Texas Intermediate crude also hovers near $100, down from its April 7 high of $113. Both benchmarks remain far above pre-war levels. Market observers warn that the futures market

title: Where Are The Carriers As Of May 11, 2026: 20 Warships Enforce Iran Blockade
description: Over 20 U.S. Navy warships, including two carrier strike groups, are enforcing the Iran blockade as of May 11, 2026. CENTCOM has redirected 61 commercial vessel
content: The U.S. Navy has deployed more than 20 warships, including two carrier strike groups, to enforce a blockade against Iran as of May 11, 2026. The operation, conducted under U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM), involves multiple naval assets in the Arabian Sea and broader CENTCOM area of responsibility. Among the ships active in the blockade are the USS George H.W. Bush (CVN 77), a Nimitz-class aircraft carrier, and the guided-missile destroyer USS John Finn (DDG 113). title: Liverpool groups push for better women’s health services access
description: Groups in Liverpool aim to fix longstanding gaps in women’s healthcare access with new community initiatives. Ending today.
content: Groups in Liverpool are stepping up efforts to fix longstanding shortages in women’s healthcare, launching programs to make services more accessible. The push comes after years of under-resourcing in gynecological, reproductive, and general women’s care across the city, leaving many residents struggling to get timely treatment. Local organizations, backed by public health experts, say the move is a direct response to decades of inadequate funding and fragmented service delivery. title: Watch: Soldier on parachuting to remote island to bring Hantavirus aid
description: British paratrooper parachutes onto remote Tristan da Cunha delivering first Hantavirus treatment and medical supplies. First aid mission in five years ends suc
content: A specialist team from the British military parachuted critical medical supplies and the first Hantavirus treatment to Tristan da Cunha Tristan da Cunha, the world’s most remote inhabited archipelago, on Thursday. The operation required a Royal Air Force C-130 Hercules to airdrop the aid 1,200 miles from the nearest landfall after severe weather prevented the Royal Navy’s support ship from docking at the island’s sole landing site. title: UN confirms Russia holds at least 20,000 abducted Ukrainian children
description: UN investigators say Russia has systematically deported and 'reeducated' over 20,000 Ukrainian children. Many remain trapped in Russia with forced adoptions und
content: Russia has forcibly taken at least 20,000 Ukrainian children and placed them in state-run programs designed to erase their national identity, according to the United Nations. The Independent International Commission of Inquiry on Ukraine concluded in March that the systematic deportations and transfers constitute war crimes and crimes against humanity under international law. The commission also found Russian authorities have 'unjustifiably delayed' the return of these children, a violation of the Geneva Conventions and a separate war crime. title: UK alcohol deaths drop for first time since Covid lockdowns
description: UK alcohol-related deaths fell in 2023 for the first time since the Covid pandemic began. Experts warn against complacency as figures remain high.
content: Alcohol-related deaths in the UK declined in 2023, ending a three-year streak of annual increases tied to the Covid-19 pandemic, provisional government figures reveal. The Office for National Statistics (ONS) reported 8,604 deaths directly caused by alcohol misuse last year, down from 8,756 in 2022—a 1.7% decrease. Public health experts say the drop is encouraging but stress it is far from sufficient. The rise in alcohol deaths began in 2020, coinciding with the first lockdowns. Deaths climbed each year, reaching record highs as pandemic stress, isolation, and disrupted healthcare access took

title: Macron interrupts speech to demand silence at Kenya conference
description: French President Emmanuel Macron stood up during a conference in Kenya to silence the audience, saying speakers couldn't be heard. Disruptive moment sparks crit
content: French President Emmanuel Macron interrupted a conference speech in Nairobi, Kenya on Wednesday to demand silence from the audience. Video of the moment shows Macron standing from his seat, waving his hand, and asking the crowd to quiet down, saying it was 'impossible' for speakers to be heard over the noise. The incident occurred during a panel discussion at the Africa CEO Forum, an annual business conference held in Kenya’s capital. title: Top 5 Grill Brushes for Spotless Grates in 2026
description: We tested 15 grill brushes against 24 pounds of BBQ chicken to find the top 5 that truly clean grates. See which brushes work and which failed.
content: Bon Appétit put 15 grill brushes to the test, pitting them against 24 pounds of sticky BBQ chicken to determine which tools clean grates most effectively. The experiment aimed to cut through marketing claims and identify brushes that actually remove baked-on gunk without damaging grates or posing safety risks. After months of grilling, scrubbing, and evaluating, five brushes stood out for their performance, durability, and safety. title: US and Israel hint at Iran uranium seizure after failed nuclear talks
description: President Trump and Israel’s Netanyahu suggest military options to seize Iran’s enriched uranium as negotiations stall. Here are the risks and next steps.
content: President Donald Trump on Monday raised the prospect of U.S. troops entering Iran to remove its stockpile of highly enriched uranium (HEU), a statement echoed the previous day by Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. The comments follow the collapse of negotiations between Western powers and Iran over its nuclear program, signaling a potential shift toward military options after years of diplomatic failure. title: OVO Energy takeover planned: Customers told tariffs safe as deal looms
description: OVO Energy customers won’t see tariffs change under takeover plan that would create Britain’s fifth-largest energy supplier. Major deal secures existing rates.
content: OVO Energy customers can keep all existing tariffs under a planned takeover that would create one of Britain’s largest energy suppliers. The deal, pending regulatory approval, guarantees no price changes or service cuts for households already on OVO contracts. Parent company OVO Group confirmed the acquisition would create a combined business serving over 5 million customers nationwide. All tariffs—including fixed-rate and variable deals—will remain intact under the agreement, easing concerns about sudden price hikes or policy shifts. content: > 📰 Continuing coverage: Astrophotographer captures remarkable view of galactic 'Eyes' with backyard telescope Astrophotographer Graeme Dury has captured a stunning image of the Lyrid meteor shower streaking across the night sky beside the Milky Way from the remote Scottish Isle of Skye. title: Tech transforms woodworking with safer, precise digital tools
description: Digital tools and automation are making woodworking shops safer and more precise. See how CNC machines and AI cut risks and boost efficiency in the industry.
content: Woodworking workshops are shedding decades of dust and danger as digital tools reshape the trade. Computer Numerical Control [CNC] machines now handle cuts with micrometer accuracy, reducing injuries tied to manual saws and routers. Industry data shows CNC adoption jumped 28% in U.S. small shops last year alone, driven by lower equipment costs and simpler software. "These tools let one person run what used to take three," says Mark Stevens), owner of Precision Craft Furniture in Portland, Oregon. title: Newcastle paints park benches to promote mental health support
description: Newcastle United Foundation paints benches in Leazes Park to encourage mental health conversations. Defender Dan Burn discusses why asking for help matters.
content: Newcastle United’s community arm has transformed benches in Leazes Park into beacons for mental health awareness. The project, led by the Newcastle United Foundation, uses brightly colored seats to prompt conversations among fans about emotional well-being. Defender Dan Burn, who has been vocal about mental health, spoke to BBC Sport about the initiative and its role in normalizing open discussions. The benches, painted in shades of blue and yellow, stand out against the park’s greenery. Each carries a simple message: a reminder to check on friends and family. The foundation chose Leazes Park

title: New York’s trendiest 2024 restaurant is a pub with no kitchen
description: Dean’s, New York’s buzziest new restaurant, is a pub with no kitchen. Learn how it’s redefining the city’s dining scene.
content: Dean’s, the East Village pub that opened in May 2024, has become New York City’s most talked-about new restaurant without serving a single plate of food. The bar, tucked on a quiet stretch of East 7th Street near Tompkins Square Park, has drawn lines around the block daily, defying the city’s usual dining hype cycle. Unlike traditional New York openings that prioritize elaborate tasting menus or Instagram-worthy dishes, Dean’s operates as a no-kitchen pub with a short selection of beer-friendly snacks. title: NASA's Artemis 3 Moon rocket core stage raised ahead of 2027 launch
description: NASA’s Artemis 3 Moon rocket core stage now stands inside the Vehicle Assembly Building awaiting engine installation. The 2027 test flight will validate lunar l
content: NASA’s Space Launch System (SLS) core stage for the Artemis 3 mission has been raised and positioned inside the Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center in Florida. Engineers are preparing to attach the four RS-25 engines that will power the rocket during its Moon-bound flight, currently scheduled for no earlier than 2027. The core stage, the backbone of the SLS, stands 212 feet tall and houses cryogenic liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen tanks to fuel the engines. Once fully assembled, the rocket will undergo final checks before being rolled to the launch pad for the uncrewed mission.

title: US Air Force tests MQ-9 Reaper firing laser-guided APKWS rockets
description: U.S. Air Force MQ-9 Reaper drones now test-fire laser-guided APKWS rockets against ground and aerial targets, expanding drone combat capabilities. A General Ato
content: The U.S. Air Force has conducted flight tests of an MQ-9A Reaper drone armed with laser-guided Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System (APKWS) rockets, marking a significant expansion of the drone’s combat capabilities. The tests, confirmed by manufacturer General Atomics Aeronautical Systems, Inc. (GA-ASI) General Atomics, were carried out at the Nevada Test and Training Range. The trials demonstrated the MQ-9’s ability to fire APKWS rockets in both standard air-to-ground attacks and against aerial targets, including drones. title: Haggling prices and chasing debts - tradespeople hit with cost of living headache
description: UK tradespeople face rising late payments and debt struggles as inflation and high costs push household budgets to the limit. Late payment rates jump 54% in a y
content: Late payments to tradespeople surged 54% in the last year, according to a survey by Paysafe, a payments firm tracking small business struggles amid the cost of living crisis. The findings underscore deepening financial strain on contractors, plumbers, electricians and builders already grappling with inflation-driven cost increases for materials and fuel. Many now face a dual squeeze: delayed income from clients while their own expenses climb, pushing some toward debt to stay afloat. The survey, conducted among 1,200 UK tradespeople, found 54% reported a rise in late payments compared with 2023

title: French passenger quarantined after hantavirus symptoms on ship return

content: Five passengers from the Antarctic expedition ship MV Hondius are quarantined in Paris after displaying symptoms consistent with hantavirus infection upon their return from a voyage to South America. France’s Prime Minister Gabriel Attal confirmed the quarantine Sunday, stating the passengers would remain isolated "until further notice" as health authorities assess the situation. The ship, operated by Holland America Line, arrived in France over the weekend with the five passengers among its 200-person crew and guest roster. title: Democrats' tax cut push risks backfiring on middle class voters
description: Democratic lawmakers push middle class tax cuts but critics warn plans may backfire on economic equity goals. Here’s what’s at stake.
content: Democratic lawmakers, led by Senator Chris Van Hollen, are advancing a tax-cutting strategy that opponents argue does little to help the middle class. The move comes amid widespread Democratic soul-searching following the party’s loss in the 2024 election, where economic competence became a defining voter issue despite a year of volatile policymaking. The push for tax cuts reflects a broader rethink of the party’s platform after voters signaled concerns over economic management. title: Doctrine vs. Reality: Golden Hour Evacuation Fails in Modern Wars
description: Western military evacuation strategies from the War on Terror face new challenges in Ukraine and beyond where surveillance and battlefield conditions complicate
content: Western militaries have long depended on rapid casualty evacuation within an hour of injury, known as the golden hour. This doctrine, rooted in civilian trauma care and formalized by Dr. R. Adams Cowley in the 1970s, became a cornerstone of military medical strategy during the Global War on Terror. The assumption was simple: move the wounded quickly to surgical care to maximize survival. Yet the war in Ukraine and other modern conflicts are testing this model like never before. title: 1 in 3 young adults see SARMs ads weekly online study warns
description: UK Anti-Doping research finds one-third of 16-25-year-olds see SARMs ads weekly. Learn about the risks of these dangerous performance enhancers sold online.
content: Social media platforms are flooded with ads for SARMs—selective androgen receptor modulators—a class of performance-enhancing drugs linked to serious health risks. A study by UK Anti-Doping UKAD reveals that 33% of 16-to-25-year-olds encounter SARMs promotions at least once a week. The findings underscore growing concerns about the unchecked sale of these substances online, often to young adults seeking muscle growth or athletic performance boosts. title: C-5 Galaxies Now Slated To Keep Flying Until 2050 As Readiness Plummets To 37 Percent
description: The U.S. Air Force pushes back retirement of its C-5 Galaxy cargo planes to 2050 due to maintenance delays and readiness drops. The fleet faces critical readine
content: The U.S. Air Force has formally pushed back the retirement of its C-5M Galaxy strategic airlifters to Fiscal Year 2050, marking a five-year extension from previous plans. The decision, revealed in the 2027 budget request, reflects ongoing struggles with the fleet’s reliability despite decades of upgrades. The service currently operates 52 C-5M aircraft, a critical component of its global airlift capability. title: Architecture of remembrance and hope: Daniel Libeskind at 80
description: Celebrating 80 years, Polish-American architect Daniel Libeskind remains influential with projects like Berlin’s Jewish Museum and New York’s Ground Zero. A loo
content: Polish-American architect Daniel Libeskind marked his 80th birthday on May 12 with no signs of slowing his pace in designing some of the world’s most culturally significant buildings. His firm, Studio Daniel Libeskind, remains one of the most sought-after architecture practices globally, known for large-scale projects that merge deconstructivist design with complex historical narratives. title: Russian Navy adds anti-drone 'cope cage' to Black Sea patrol boat

The modification, captured in photographs published this month by Ukrainian defense adviser Serhii Sternenko, reflects the increasing threat posed by drones to naval operations. While it remains unclear whether this is a one-off adaptation or part of a broader fleet-wide update, the move underscores the urgency of countering drone warfare in modern conflicts. title: Israel-Turkey tensions rise over Syria, Gaza and regional power
description: Israeli and Turkish leaders exchange sharp rhetoric over Syria, Gaza and regional influence. Experts warn of escalating risks of direct conflict.

His remarks came amid growing military posturing and diplomatic confrontations tied to Syria and Gaza, raising concerns among regional analysts about the potential for direct military conflict. title: Hantavirus on cruise ship: How Andes strain spreads and symptoms
description: Cases of hantavirus, including Andes strain, found aboard cruise ship. Learn how it spreads, symptoms, and risks of person-to-person transmission. Period.

But conditions worsened as temperatures swung wildly from 70°F to 80°F during winter-to-spring transition days to lows in the teens or low 20s. title: Alaska megatsunami ranks as second largest ever recorded in study
description: Alaska's 2020 megatsunami was the second largest ever recorded after a 1936 Greenland event. Climate change-driven glacier melt raises risks of future giant wav
content: A 2020 megatsunami in Alaska’s Barry Arm fjord was the second largest ever recorded, new research confirms. The wave reached nearly 200 meters (656 feet) high, according to scientists who analyzed seismic data and computer models. The event ranks just behind a 1936 Greenland megatsunami that reached 243 meters (797 feet) at its peak. The Alaska wave occurred on October 17, 2020, after a massive landslide sent rock and ice plunging into the fjord. The test flight marks a critical step in validating propulsion technology designed for high efficiency and minimal acoustic signature, which could enable new operational capabilities for military and intelligence missions.

title: B-1B “Apocalypse II” Out Of The Boneyard And Back In Service

content: The U.S. Air Force has brought a retired B-1B Lancer bomber back into service after nearly two years of intensive restoration. The swing-wing aircraft, serial number 86-0115 and formerly named Rage, was pulled from the 309th Aerospace Maintenance and Regeneration Group in Arizona—commonly known as the aircraft boneyard—before undergoing depot maintenance at Tinker Air Force Base in Oklahoma. The bomber departed the base on April 22, marking its return to operational status after a prolonged overhaul process led by the Oklahoma City Air Logistics Complex. title: India completes sea-based nuclear deterrent with INS Aridhaman in 2026
description: India’s INS Aridhaman, its third nuclear-powered submarine, entered service on April 3, 2026, completing its sea-based nuclear deterrent. Learn how this shifts
content: India’s nuclear deterrent took a major step forward on April 3, 2026, when the country’s third nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine, INS Aridhaman, entered service. The milestone completes the sea leg of India’s nuclear triad, joining its land- and air-based capabilities. With three submarines now operational, India can reliably maintain at least one submarine on deterrent patrol at all times, ensuring a continuous second-strike capability. India’s Nuclear Triad Now Complete India’s nuclear triad consists of land-based ballistic missiles, air-launched weapons, and sea-based submarines.

content: Airlines in Europe are bracing for potential disruptions to summer holiday travel as jet fuel shortages threaten operations. title: England’s biggest renting shakeup in 30 years explained

content: Landlords in England can no longer evict tenants without a valid legal reason starting April 1 under the Renters’ Rights Act, the biggest change to private renting rules since 1996. The law replaces no-fault Section 21 evictions, which allowed landlords to reclaim properties without giving a reason, with new grounds for possession that require proof of fault such as rent arrears or property damage. title: Bowel cancer: 5 key symptoms to check and how to reduce risk

The disease often begins as non-cancerous polyps that can turn malignant over time. Regular screening and quick action on warning signs can prevent progression and save lives. content: Global tropical rainforest loss slowed in 2023, with 3.7 million hectares destroyed—a 6% drop from the previous year, according to new satellite analysis by Global Forest Watch. title: US risks gallium nitride chip supply as China bans exports
description: China bans gallium exports to the U.S., controlling 99% of global supply. America risks repeating its silicon chip mistake. Learn why it matters now.
content: China escalated its economic retaliation against the United States by banning all gallium exports in December 2024, cutting off nearly 100% of the global supply of the critical semiconductor material. The move follows a 2023 restriction on gallium shipments and comes as tensions over advanced chip technology continue to rise between the two nations. Gallium is essential for producing gallium nitride (GaN) semiconductors, a technology widely seen as the next generation for microchips used in 5G networks, electric vehicles, and military systems. Unlike traditional silicon chips, GaN semiconducto

title: AI war games show 95% of nuclear crises end in simulated strikes
description: New study reveals AI models escalate 95% of simulated nuclear crises to tactical strikes and threaten strategic attacks in 76% of cases. AI wargaming risks alar
content: Artificial intelligence systems escalate nuclear crises to strikes in 95% of simulated war games, according to new research that highlights the risks of deploying AI in defense strategies. The study, conducted by Kenneth Payne), a reader in the Department of War Studies at King’s College London, tested three frontier large language models across 21 match-ups, producing alarming results. In nearly all scenarios, at least one AI side resorted to nuclear signaling, with tactical nuclear use in 95% of games and strategic threats in 76%. The findings, presented in a pre-print paper, underscore the

title: How the War in Iran Is Affecting Its Northern and Eastern Neighbors
description: Neighboring nations to Iran's north and east brace for fallout as the Iran-Israel war disrupts trade, fuels refugee flows and raises security threats. Iran's ne
content: The escalation of the Iran-Israel conflict on Feb. 28 has expanded beyond the immediate war zone, sending shockwaves into regions rarely associated with Middle Eastern violence. Iran's northern and eastern neighbors—Russia, Central Asian states and South Asia—now confront a cascade of challenges ranging from economic disruption to heightened security risks. Analysts warn these nations, already grappling with their own domestic pressures, may face prolonged instability as the conflict's ripple effects deepen. Russia's balancing act grows more precarious Russia, a key ally of Iran, finds itself

title: First human trial begins for H5N1 bird flu vaccine
description: Scientists start first human trials of an H5N1 bird flu vaccine as global health officials monitor avian flu outbreaks. Bird flu has killed millions of birds bu
content: The first human trial of a vaccine against the H5N1 bird flu strain has begun, researchers announced Monday. The experimental jab targets a flu variant that has caused widespread infections in bird populations across multiple continents but has not yet demonstrated the ability to spread between humans. Public health experts view the trial as a critical early step in preparing for a potential pandemic. The trial, led by University of Oxford researchers in collaboration with AstraZeneca, aims to enroll 60 healthy adult volunteers. Participants will receive two doses of the vaccine, followed by s

title: Should you really trust health advice from an AI chatbot?
description: AI chatbots give inconsistent health advice, experts warn. Learn how reliable medical guidance from artificial intelligence really is. AI chatbot health advice
content: Abi, a 32-year-old freelance writer, has spent months testing AI chatbots for health advice after her own mixed experiences. She asked a popular chatbot about persistent back pain and received three different explanations in one week. One response linked the pain to poor posture, another suggested a herniated disc, while the third dismissed it as stress-related. None of the suggestions matched her actual diagnosis of a muscle strain. "It was frustrating," Abi said. "I ended up ignoring the advice and seeing my doctor." Her experience reflects a growing concern among healthcare professionals ab

title: Scammers are becoming ever more sophisticated - this is what the fightback looks like
description: Scam losses hit $55 billion in 2023 as criminals use AI and deepfakes. Governments and tech firms launch coordinated crackdowns to protect victims. Scam victim
content: Fraudsters took in $55 billion globally last year, a 30% jump from 2022, as scams evolved from simple phishing emails to AI-driven schemes that mimic voices and faces. The rise forced governments and corporations into an unprecedented alliance to disrupt criminal networks, with U.S. authorities alone tracking 40 million annual scam victims. Law enforcement agencies now share real-time threat data with banks and tech platforms, while new legislation targets scam call centers and cryptocurrency laundering hubs in Southeast Asia and West Africa. The crackdown follows a surge in investment fraud,

title: UK Covid inquiry: How the pandemic response is being examined
description: The UK Covid inquiry releases its fourth report praising the vaccine rollout as an 'extraordinary feat'. Here’s how the investigation works and what it found.
content: The UK’s public inquiry into the Covid-19 pandemic released its fourth report Tuesday, highlighting the vaccine rollout as a rare success amid widespread criticism of the government’s handling of the crisis. The probe, led by retired judge Lady Heather Hallett, is examining decisions made during the pandemic to determine what went wrong and how future responses can improve. The inquiry’s latest findings commended the rapid deployment of vaccines as an 'extraordinary feat,' noting that over 150 million doses were administered in the UK by early 2022. The rollout began in December 2020, just mon

title: New Yarm wellness garden opens Saturday to fight isolation
description: A new public garden at Yarm Wellness opens Saturday to help combat loneliness with outdoor activities and green spaces. Free entry available.
content: A new wellbeing garden at Yarm Wellness Yarm will open to the public for the first time this Saturday, offering a free space for outdoor activities aimed at reducing isolation. The outdoor area, part of the Yarm Wellness initiative, will be accessible from 9 a.m. daily, with no admission fee required. Officials say the garden is designed to provide a calming environment for locals to relax, socialize or participate in light exercise programs. The garden’s launch comes amid growing concerns about loneliness in the UK, particularly among older adults. Local health leaders highlighted the project

title: Vaccines a huge success, but public trust must be earned - key findings from Covid report
description: UK Covid vaccination program prevented 400,000 deaths but vaccine hesitancy persists despite success. UK Health Security Agency report reveals key findings on p
content: Covid-19 vaccines prevented at least 400,000 deaths in the United Kingdom during the pandemic, a new UK Health Security Agency report has found. The analysis, published Wednesday, confirms the overwhelming success of mass immunisation programs while warning that growing vaccine hesitancy could undermine future protection efforts. The report reveals that vaccination prevented an estimated 440,000 hospitalisations and 40,000 intensive care admissions in England alone. Researchers calculated that without vaccines, deaths would have been 3.2 times higher during the pandemic period. The analysis co

title: Covid vaccines saved hundreds of thousands of lives, inquiry finds
description: Covid vaccines prevented hundreds of thousands of deaths but a small group harmed need better support, says official inquiry report. Latest findings on vaccine
content: The rapid development and deployment of Covid-19 vaccines prevented an estimated 20 million deaths worldwide during their first year, according to a landmark inquiry report released Tuesday. The independent panel, commissioned by the World Health Organization World Health Organization, credited the unprecedented global collaboration between scientists, regulators and manufacturers for the extraordinary feat. However, the report highlighted a critical gap: the small minority of people who experienced severe side effects, including rare blood clots and heart inflammation, often struggled to acce

title: Why did resident doctors go on strike and how much are they paid?
description: Resident doctors in England returned to work after 15 strikes over pay disputes. Learn how much they earn and why negotiations failed. Pay details confirmed.
content: Resident doctors in England concluded a 15th strike on [date], marking one of the longest-running disputes in NHS history. The walkouts forced the cancellation of tens of thousands of appointments and procedures, straining an already overburdened healthcare system. The British Medical Association (BMA), which represents junior doctors, cited pay erosion as the primary reason for the industrial action, arguing that real-term wages have fallen by nearly 30% since 2008 after accounting for inflation. The dispute centers on a demand for a 35% pay rise to restore earnings to 2008 levels, a proposal

title: How doctors' strikes cut NHS waits and improve care - but can it last?
description: Hospital trusts report shorter waits and faster decisions after doctors' strikes. But can these benefits continue long-term? Find out what research shows.
content: Hospital trusts in England have told the BBC that previous doctors’ strikes led to shorter patient waits, faster decision-making and quieter corridors. The unexpected improvements came during periods of industrial action by junior doctors and consultants, but whether these benefits can continue remains unclear. NHS data obtained by the BBC shows that in some trusts, average waiting times dropped by up to 20% during strike periods. One trust in the Midlands reported that consultant decisions on patient discharges were made 48 hours faster than pre-strike averages. Another in the North West said

title: West of England news roundup: Doctors strikes and space paw prints
description: Doctors strikes hit West of England as NASA finds mysterious paw prints in space photos. Catch up on last week's top local and national stories in this week's r
content: Emergency departments at Bristol Royal Infirmary and Southmead Hospital were forced to close for several hours this week as junior doctors and consultants walked out in a dispute over pay and working conditions. The strikes, organized by British Medical Association and NHS Providers, affected services in Bath’s Royal United Hospital and Gloucestershire’s Cheltenham General Hospital as well. NASA’s lunar discovery stuns scientists NASA released images this week showing what appear to be animal paw prints on the surface of the moon. title: Study probes why Isle of Wight boosts mental health and well-being
description: Scientists investigate the Isle of Wight’s unique environmental factors that may improve mental health and well-being. New research could explain the island’s p
content: > 📰 Continuing coverage: Midlife fitness boosts lifespan and health by 5+ years study finds A new research initiative is probing whether the Isle of Wight’s unique environment holds the key to better mental health. Led by epidemiologists and environmental psychologists from the University of Exeter University of Exeter, the study will assess how factors such as green spaces, air quality and coastal exposure may influence residents’ psychological well-being. title: Doctor strike delays UK obesity surgery by another month
description: UK patients face further delays to life-changing weight loss surgery as doctor strikes add to 3-year wait times. More than 50,000 procedures postponed nationwid
content: Tom Lawson’s three-year wait for a gastric bypass surgery has stretched further after doctors in England began a week-long strike on Monday, forcing another delay to the life-saving procedure. Lawson, 34, from Manchester, was originally told he would undergo the surgery in 2021 but has seen multiple postponements due to staffing shortages and now industrial action. "Every delay adds to the anxiety," Lawson said. "I’ve lost 10kg while waiting, but my BMI is still dangerously high." title: England's doctors strike for 15th time amid NHS pay dispute
description: NHS boss says hospitals managing despite England's resident doctors' 15th strike over pay. Emergency care remains available during walkout.
content: England’s resident doctors—formerly known as junior doctors—began their 15th strike on Tuesday as part of a prolonged dispute over pay. NHS England confirmed that hospitals are managing the disruption, with emergency care fully operational despite the walkout. The strike, which lasts 24 hours, is the latest in a series of industrial actions dating back to early 2023 over demands for a substantial pay rise to match inflation and rising living costs. NHS England’s chief executive, Amanda Pritchard, stated that trusts are handling the strike effectively, with alternative staffing arrangements in

title: UN warns US waste and pollution are poisoning Mexican communities
description: UN expert says the US is using Mexico as a toxic garbage dump, exposing communities to dangerous pollution from industrial waste and pesticides. The US-Mexico b
content: A United Nations human rights investigator has accused the United States of using Mexico as a toxic waste sink, exposing communities to harmful pollution from industrial production and imported garbage. Marcos Orellana, the UN special rapporteur on hazardous substances and waste, said in an interview with The Guardian and Quinto Elemento Lab that weak environmental enforcement and oversight have allowed pollutants to accumulate near Mexican cities producing goods for American consumers. Orellana’s warning comes after residents in industrial zones near the US-Mexico border reported severe healt

title: NASA Artemis Moon mission faces 40-minute blackout during lunar flyby
description: NASA's Artemis mission will lose Earth contact for 40 minutes during a lunar flyby. Here’s why this happens and what it means for future Moon missions.
content: NASA’s Artemis mission is set to experience a 40-minute communication blackout as the spacecraft passes behind the Moon, temporarily severing contact with Earth. The disruption is a planned part of the mission and occurs when the Orion spacecraft) flies around the far side of the lunar surface, blocking signals between the crew and mission control in Houston. The blackout period is a known challenge for lunar missions, as the Moon’s bulk obstructs radio waves needed for communication. Engineers at NASA have accounted for this in mission timelines, ensuring the spacecraft can operate autonomous

title: UK shoppers pay £5.30 for orange juice as supermarket prices surge
description: A single carton of orange juice now costs £5.30 in UK supermarkets. Rising production, shipping and energy costs push up prices across shelves. Follow the trail
content: A 1-litre carton of chilled orange juice now retails for £5.30 on shelves across the UK, up from £3.20 two years ago, according to retail analysts. The jump mirrors increases seen in butter, coffee and milk, exposing how global pressures are reshaping domestic food prices. Analysts trace the sharp rise to multiple upstream factors: rising fuel costs, higher shipping rates and increased energy bills on farms and in processing plants. The squeeze is visible from orchard to supermarket aisle. Orange growers in Brazil, which supplies about 80% of the UK’s orange juice imports, report fertilizer pr

title: Prepare for turbulence - how a prolonged Middle East conflict could reshape how we fly
description: Major Gulf airports cut long-haul flight costs but now face uncertainty due to prolonged Middle East conflict disrupting global travel routes.
content: The Gulf’s major airports, including Dubai International Dubai International and Hamad International Airport in Doha Hamad International Airport, have long served as critical waypoints for long-distance flights. Their strategic locations and modern infrastructure helped slash long-haul travel costs over the past two decades. Now, a prolonged conflict in the Middle East threatens their stability and could force airlines to rethink global routing strategies. title: Labour’s Brexit shift targets Remainers: Why Leavers feel left behind
description: Labour’s focus on closer EU ties risks losing Brexit-backing voters, warns pollster Sir John Curtice. Explore the political shift and voter reactions.
content: Labour’s strategy to forge closer ties with the European Union has reignited debate over whether the move risks electoral backlash from voters who backed Brexit in 2016. Sir John Curtice, a leading pollster and professor of politics at the University of Strathclyde, argues the party’s pivot toward Remain-leaning policies could alienate its traditional working-class base in northern England and the Midlands—areas that delivered key victories in the 2019 general election. His warning comes amid polling data suggesting a growing appetite among Remain supporters for a more integrated relationship

title: NHS report reveals failures during Covid rules and staff risk
description: A damning report shows NHS collapse risk, patient failures and staff danger under Covid rules. Key findings question government policies and patient care.
content: A newly released Covid-19 inquiry report has exposed severe failures within the National Health Service during the pandemic. The document, published Wednesday, details how England’s health system nearly collapsed under the weight of pandemic restrictions, how patients were failed in critical care moments and how frontline staff worked in dangerous conditions. The findings directly challenge government policies that were meant to protect both patients and medical workers. The report highlights three primary areas of failure: near-system collapse, patient neglect and staff safety risks. During p

title: AI billionaires replace industrial tycoons in new tech oligarchy
description: Today’s tech moguls like Gates, Musk and Altman now steer AI and global tech, reshaping fortunes far beyond old industrial billionaires. Their wealth exceeds $1
content: Bill Gates became the first modern tech billionaire in 1992, joining a Forbes billionaires list dominated by industrial and retail moguls from Japan, Germany, Canada, South Korea and Sweden. The list included family fortunes from Britain and America, with fortunes totaling $100 billion—equivalent to 0.4% of U.S. GDP. Most billionaires then made their wealth from retail, media, property, packaging and industrial conglomerates. That oligarchy has changed drastically. Today, Bernard Arnault of French luxury group LVMH and tech titans like Elon Musk, Bill Gates and Sam Altman steer AI and global t

title: Women underrepresented in UK government despite recent appointments
description: UK politics still dominated by men as recent ministerial changes raise questions about gender balance in government. Analysis of power structures in Westminster
content: The appointment of Peter Mandelson to a senior government role has reignited scrutiny of gender representation in UK politics. Critics argue the decision underscores the persistent dominance of men in Westminster, despite recent efforts to address imbalances. Women currently hold just 35% of ministerial positions in the government, a figure unchanged since 2019, according to official data. The decision to bring back Mandelson, a long-time Labour figure and former EU Trade Commissioner, has been met with skepticism by advocates for gender equality in politics. title: Mexico fines toxic waste plant $4.8m after Guardian pollution probe
description: Mexico to spend $4.8m on pollution crackdown after Guardian investigations revealed dangerous heavy-metal contamination near a Monterrey plant processing US haz
content: The Mexican government announced a sweeping crackdown on industrial pollution Friday, including $4.8 million in fines against a plant processing toxic waste from the U.S. The penalties target Zinc Nacional, a Monterrey-based factory linked to severe heavy-metal contamination in nearby neighborhoods, according to investigations by the Guardian and Mexico’s Quinto Elemento Lab. Government officials confirmed the fines and outlined a broader plan to deploy a new industrial air-monitoring system across Monterrey’s metropolitan area. The move follows revelations that Zinc Nacional, which recycles m

title: ‘I ain’t goin nowhere’: Gullah Geechee people fight off developers with a historic referendum
description: Georgia voters weigh historic referendum to block zoning amendment threatening Gullah Geechee heritage on Sapelo Island. A 70-year legacy of self-sufficiency ha
content: Ire Gene Grovner stood behind his weathered wooden house on Sapelo Island, skinning a raccoon with a sharp knife while chickens clucked nearby and collard greens shivered in the winter wind. “If you ain’t lazy, you can live good here,” Grovner said, gesturing to the garden and livestock that have sustained his family for decades. His home sits on land his ancestors worked since emancipation, part of the Gullah Geechee people’s 70-year legacy of self-sufficient island life. Now, that legacy faces its greatest threat in a zoning amendment that could open Sapelo Island to mass development. On Mar

title: The debate about whether the NHS should use magic mushrooms to treat depression
description: Clinical trials in the UK since 2022 show psychedelic mushrooms may help treat depression. NHS considers expanding options beyond traditional drugs.
content: The National Health Service is weighing whether to incorporate psychedelic mushrooms into depression treatment protocols after a surge in clinical trials since 2022. Researchers in the UK have joined international efforts to test psilocybin, the psychoactive compound in magic mushrooms, as a potential alternative to standard antidepressants. Early data suggests the substance may rapidly reduce symptoms in patients with treatment-resistant depression, often within hours of a single dose. Imperial College London and other institutions have led these studies, which follow a global movement toward

title: COP30: Trump absent as 20 nations skip UN climate summit
description: Leaders from the U.S. and 19 other nations won’t attend COP30, raising questions about the UN climate summit’s effectiveness. Leaders face pressure as climate p
content: The UN climate summit COP30 is underway in Belém, Brazil, but the absence of the U.S. president and 19 other world leaders has overshadowed the event. Among the missing are leaders from Canada, France, and the UK, raising concerns about the summit’s ability to drive meaningful climate action. Delegates from nearly 200 countries are attending the two-week talks, where they will negotiate funding for poorer nations and set new emissions targets. title: Mexico factory to relocate toxic waste plant after US import report
description: Mexican zinc plant Zinc Nacional to move polluting operations after Guardian investigation reveals heavy-metals pollution from US hazardous waste imports. A new
content: Zinc Nacional, a Mexican zinc producer, announced it will move its most polluting operations after a Guardian investigation revealed evidence of heavy-metals pollution near its Monterrey plant. The factory recycles toxic steel dust imported from the U.S. steel industry to recover zinc, but the joint probe with Quinto Elemento Lab found hazardous contamination in surrounding neighborhoods. The Monterrey metropolitan area plant has faced scrutiny over its handling of hazardous waste, which originates from U.S. steel mills. title: 1 in 10 Americans say partner is financial bully in marriage
description: Guardian survey finds 10% of Americans describe their spouse as a financial bully. Read first-person accounts of money disputes in marriage.
content: Americans are divided over the term “financial bullying” in marriages, with a Guardian survey showing that one in 10 describe their partner’s financial behavior that way. The findings come after the Guardian sought first-person accounts from readers about money disputes in relationships. While some respondents embraced the term, others pushed back, arguing that monitoring expenses is simply responsible budgeting.
